law_summary: Take the breast of Aaron's consecration ram and wave it as a wave offering before the LORD, which becomes the priest's portion
classification_reasoning: Priestly portion from consecration ram, but priestly portions from offerings are established as ongoing throughout Torah
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:26 - 'wave it for a wave offering before the LORD: and it shall be thy part'
interpretive_questions: Whether this priestly portion allocation applies to ongoing wave offerings or only consecration ceremonies
notes: The breast becomes the officiant's portion.

verse_text:
And thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the shoulder of the heave offering, which is waved, and which is heaved up, of the ram of the consecration,
even of
that which
is
 for Aaron, and of
that which is for his sons:
law_summary: The breast and shoulder portions from the consecration ram offering must be sanctified
requires_specific_role: sanctifying specific portions of consecration offering
classification_reasoning: Sanctification of specific portions from consecration ram, but sanctification of wave and heave offerings appears as ongoing requirement in Torah
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:27 - 'thou shalt sanctify the breast of the wave offering, and the shoulder of the heave offering'
interpretive_questions: Whether this sanctification procedure applies to all wave and heave offerings or specifically to consecration offerings
notes: Sanctification of the wave breast and heave shoulder as priestly portions -- a principle that extends beyond the consecration ceremony to all peace offerings.

verse_text:
And it shall be Aaron’s and his sons’ by a statute for ever from the children of Israel: for it
is an heave offering: and it shall be an heave offering from the children of Israel of the sacrifice of their peace offerings,
even their heave offering unto the LORD.
law_summary: Aaron and his sons shall receive the heave offering from Israel's peace offerings as a perpetual statute
classification_reasoning: Explicitly uses 'statute for ever' language making it eternal, but requires functioning priesthood and peace offerings which need temple system
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:28 - 'by a statute for ever from the children of Israel'
interpretive_questions: Does this apply to all Aaronic priests or only those actively serving? What constitutes valid peace offerings without temple?
notes: Explicitly a 'statute for ever' -- the heave offering from peace offerings belongs to Aaron and his sons perpetually.

verse_text: And the holy garments of Aaron shall be his sons’ after him, to be anointed therein, and to be consecrated in them.
law_summary: Aaron's holy garments shall be passed down to his sons for their anointing and consecration as priests
generational_phrase: shall be his sons' after him
classification_reasoning: Implies ongoing inheritance pattern 'after him' suggesting perpetual succession, but requires active priesthood and holy garments
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:29 - 'shall be his sons' after him, to be anointed therein'
interpretive_questions: Are the original garments required or can they be recreated? Does this apply without temple service?
notes: Succession law for the high priesthood: garments passed down to successors.

verse_text:
And that son that is priest in his stead shall put them on seven days, when he cometh into the tabernacle of the congregation to minister in the holy
place.
law_summary: The son who becomes priest in his father's place must wear the priestly garments for seven days when entering the tabernacle to minister
classification_reasoning: Part of ongoing priestly succession procedures, but requires tabernacle/temple and active priestly ministry
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:30 - 'when he cometh into the tabernacle of the congregation to minister'
interpretive_questions: Does 'tabernacle of the congregation' require the specific Mosaic structure or any legitimate sanctuary?
notes: The successor high priest must wear the garments for seven days during his investiture.

verse_text: And thou shalt take the ram of the consecration, and seethe his flesh in the holy place.
law_summary: The ram of consecration must be boiled in the holy place
classification_reasoning: Specific procedural instruction for consecration ceremony, appears to be for the initial consecration of Aaron and sons
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:31 - 'thou shalt take the ram of the consecration'
interpretive_questions: Is this only for initial Aaronic consecration or for all future priestly consecrations?
notes: The consecration ram must be boiled in the holy place.

verse_text:
And Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram, and the bread that
is in the basket,
by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
law_summary: Aaron and his sons must eat the flesh of the ram and bread from the basket at the door of the tabernacle during their consecration
requires_specific_role: Aaron and his sons during consecration
classification_reasoning: Part of specific consecration ceremony for Aaron and sons, tied to the tabernacle door context
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:32 - 'Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram'
interpretive_questions: Does this apply to all future priestly consecrations or only the initial Aaronic consecration?
notes: Priests eat the consecration offerings at the tabernacle entrance.

verse_text:
And they shall eat those things wherewith the atonement was made, to consecrate
and to sanctify them: but a stranger shall not eat
thereof, because they
are holy.
law_summary: Only those being consecrated may eat the consecration offerings; strangers are forbidden to eat them because they are holy
classification_reasoning: General principle about holy things and who may consume them, extends beyond initial ceremony
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:33 - 'a stranger shall not eat thereof, because they are holy'
interpretive_questions: What constitutes a 'stranger' in this context? Does this apply to all consecration ceremonies?
notes: Non-priests (strangers) are forbidden from eating consecration offerings. This establishes an ongoing access restriction.

verse_text:
And if ought of the flesh of the consecrations, or of the bread, remain unto the morning, then thou shalt burn the remainder with fire: it shall not be eaten, because it
is holy.
law_summary: Any remaining consecration meat or bread must be burned with fire the morning after and not eaten because it is holy
classification_reasoning: General principle for handling holy consecration offerings, repeated elsewhere suggesting ongoing applicability
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:34 - 'it shall not be eaten, because it is holy'
interpretive_questions: Does this apply only to initial consecration or all future consecration ceremonies?
notes: Remainder of consecration offerings must be burned by morning. This principle of not leaving holy food overnight applies broadly.

verse_text:
And thus shalt thou do unto Aaron, and to his sons, according to all
things which I have commanded thee: seven days shalt thou consecrate them.
law_summary: Aaron and his sons must be consecrated for seven days according to all the commanded procedures
requires_specific_role: Moses as consecrator, Aaron and sons as consecrated
classification_reasoning: Specific instruction for the seven-day consecration of Aaron and his sons, appears to be for the initial establishment
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:35 - 'thus shalt thou do unto Aaron, and to his sons'
interpretive_questions: Is this pattern to be followed for all future high priestly consecrations?
notes: Seven-day consecration period.

verse_text:
And thou shalt offer every day a bullock
for a sin offering for atonement: and thou shalt cleanse the altar, when thou hast made an atonement for it, and thou shalt anoint it, to sanctify it.
law_summary: Daily offering of a bullock for sin offering and atonement, with cleansing and anointing of the altar for sanctification
classification_reasoning: Part of initial altar consecration ceremony, 'every day' refers to the seven days of consecration period
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:36 - 'thou shalt offer every day a bullock for a sin offering'
interpretive_questions: Is this for initial altar consecration only or for consecrating any new altar?
notes: Daily sin offering for altar atonement during consecration. The anointing of the altar is part of the sanctification process.

verse_text: Seven days thou shalt make an atonement for the altar, and sanctify it; and it shall be an altar most holy: whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy.
law_summary: The altar must be consecrated through atonement for seven days, after which anything that touches it becomes holy
classification_reasoning: Establishes ongoing principle that properly consecrated altar makes holy whatever touches it, extends beyond initial ceremony
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:37 - 'whatsoever toucheth the altar shall be holy'
interpretive_questions: Does this principle apply to any properly consecrated altar or only the original tabernacle altar?
notes: Seven-day altar consecration and the principle that whatever touches the consecrated altar becomes holy.

verse_text:
Now this
is that which thou shalt offer upon the altar; two lambs of the first year day by day continually.
law_summary: Offer two yearling lambs on the altar daily as a continual sacrifice
classification_reasoning: Uses 'continually' and is clarified as perpetual in verse 42 with 'throughout your generations'
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:38 'day by day continually' and 29:42 'throughout your generations'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'continually' in this context implies perpetuity without the altar/temple structure
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > Daily and Communal Offerings
notes: The tamid (continual daily offering) of two lambs per day. This is one of the most fundamental ongoing obligations of the sanctuary service.

verse_text: The one lamb thou shalt offer in the morning; and the other lamb thou shalt offer at even:
law_summary: Offer one lamb in the morning and one lamb in the evening as daily sacrifices
classification_reasoning: Part of the continual offering system declared perpetual in verse 42
textual_evidence: Connected to 29:42 'throughout your generations' for the continual burnt offering
interpretive_questions: Timing requirements without temple structure and priestly system
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > Daily and Communal Offerings
notes: Morning and evening timing of the daily sacrifice.

verse_text:
And with the one lamb a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil; and the fourth part of an hin of wine
for
 a drink offering.
law_summary: The daily sacrifice requires a tenth of an ephah of flour mixed with a quarter hin of beaten oil, and a quarter hin of wine for a drink offering with each lamb
classification_reasoning: Procedural component of the perpetual continual offering system
textual_evidence: Integral part of offering declared perpetual in 29:42 'throughout your generations'
interpretive_questions: Specific measurements and substitutions in modern context
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > Daily and Communal Offerings
notes: Grain and drink offering accompaniments for the daily sacrifice.

verse_text: And the other lamb thou shalt offer at even, and shalt do thereto according to the meat offering of the morning, and according to the drink offering thereof, for a sweet savour, an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
law_summary: Offer the second lamb in the evening with the same grain and drink offerings as the morning sacrifice
classification_reasoning: Evening component of the perpetual continual offering declared in verse 42
textual_evidence: Part of offering system declared 'throughout your generations' in 29:42
interpretive_questions: Evening timing determination and offering procedures without temple
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > Daily and Communal Offerings
notes: Evening sacrifice must have the same accompaniments as the morning sacrifice.

verse_text:
This shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations
at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD: where I will meet you, to speak there unto thee.
law_summary: A continual burnt offering must be maintained throughout all generations at the tabernacle entrance
classification_reasoning: Explicitly states 'throughout your generations' making it eternally binding
textual_evidence: Exodus 29:42 'throughout your generations at the door of the tabernacle'
interpretive_questions: How to maintain continual offering without physical tabernacle/temple structure
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > Daily and Communal Offerings
notes: Explicitly a 'continual burnt offering throughout your generations' -- confirms the perpetual nature of the daily sacrifice.

verse_text:
And thou shalt make an altar to burn incense upon:
of shittim wood shalt thou make it.
law_summary: Make an altar for burning incense out of shittim wood
classification_reasoning: Construction command for tabernacle/temple worship system without explicit duration language
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:1 command to 'make an altar' as part of worship system
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to future temple construction or was specific to tabernacle
notes: Altar of incense construction.

verse_text:
A cubit
shall be the length thereof, and a cubit the breadth thereof; foursquare shall it be: and two cubits
shall be the height thereof: the horns thereof
shall be of the same.
law_summary: The altar of incense must be constructed as a square cubit by cubit, two cubits high, with horns made from the same material
classification_reasoning: Specific construction specifications for worship altar without duration language
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:2 detailed measurements for altar construction
interpretive_questions: Whether exact specifications required for any future altar construction
notes: Dimensions of the incense altar.

verse_text: And thou shalt overlay it with pure gold, the top thereof, and the sides thereof round about, and the horns thereof; and thou shalt make unto it a crown of gold round about.
law_summary: The altar of incense must be overlaid with pure gold on its top, sides, and horns, and have a golden crown around it
classification_reasoning: Construction specifications for altar overlay as part of worship system
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:3 'overlay it with pure gold' - specific construction command
interpretive_questions: Material requirements and availability for future altar construction

law_summary: Make two golden rings under the crown of the altar of incense at opposite corners to hold the carrying staves
classification_reasoning: Functional construction detail for portable altar system used by priests
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:4 'they shall be for places for the staves to bear it'
interpretive_questions: Portability requirements in permanent temple structure versus tabernacle

verse_text:
And thou shalt make the staves
of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold.
law_summary: Make staves of acacia wood and overlay them with gold for the altar of incense
classification_reasoning: Construction specification for altar carrying equipment without explicit duration
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:5 'make the staves of shittim wood, and overlay them with gold'
interpretive_questions: Material specifications and necessity of carrying staves for permanent installations

verse_text:
And thou shalt put it before the vail that
is by the ark of the testimony, before the mercy seat that
is over the testimony, where I will meet with thee.
law_summary: Place the altar of incense before the veil by the ark of testimony, before the mercy seat
classification_reasoning: Physical placement instruction requiring temple infrastructure
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:6 - placement before veil by ark of testimony
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to any future temple or only the tabernacle
notes: Placement of the incense altar before the veil, near the Ark.

verse_text: And Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning: when he dresseth the lamps, he shall burn incense upon it.
law_summary: Aaron must burn sweet incense on the altar every morning when he tends to the lamps
classification_reasoning: Daily ritual requiring temple and Aaronic priesthood
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:7 - Aaron shall burn thereon sweet incense every morning
interpretive_questions: Whether Aaron refers specifically to him or the office of high priest
notes: Ongoing priestly duty: burning incense every morning when tending the lamps.

verse_text: And when Aaron lighteth the lamps at even, he shall burn incense upon it, a perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations.
law_summary: Aaron must burn incense when lighting the evening lamps as a perpetual offering
classification_reasoning: Explicitly stated as perpetual with generational language, but requires temple infrastructure
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:8 - 'a perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations'
interpretive_questions: None - clearly eternal but awaiting prerequisites
notes: Explicitly 'perpetual incense before the LORD throughout your generations.' Evening incense burning duty.

verse_text: Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon, nor burnt sacrifice, nor meat offering; neither shall ye pour drink offering thereon.
law_summary: Prohibition against offering unauthorized incense, burnt offerings, meal offerings, or drink offerings on the incense altar
classification_reasoning: Prohibition regarding proper use of incense altar
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:9 - Ye shall offer no strange incense thereon
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to any altar or only the specific incense altar
notes: Prohibition against unauthorized incense or other offerings on the incense altar. This is both a prohibited offering regulation and a priestly restriction.

verse_text:
And Aaron shall make an atonement upon the horns of it once in a year with the blood of the sin offering of atonements: once in the year shall he make atonement upon it throughout your generations: it
is most holy unto the LORD.
law_summary: Aaron must make atonement on the altar's horns once per year with sin offering blood
classification_reasoning: Yearly atonement ritual with explicit generational language
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:10 - 'throughout your generations'
interpretive_questions: Relationship to broader Day of Atonement requirements
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Yom Kippur > The Atonement Ritual
notes: Annual atonement on the altar horns with sin offering blood; explicitly stated as 'throughout your generations' making it a perpetual ordinance tied to Yom Kippur.

verse_text:
When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel after their number, then shall they give every man a ransom for his soul unto the LORD, when thou numberest them; that there be no plague among them, when
thou numberest them.
law_summary: When conducting a census of Israel, every man must give a ransom payment to the LORD to prevent plague
classification_reasoning: Conditional law triggered by conducting a census
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:12 - 'When thou takest the sum of the children of Israel'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to any census or only official governmental ones
notes: The half-shekel ransom when taking a census. Could be considered a one-time census instruction, but the conditional phrasing ('when thou takest') establishes an ongoing law for whenever a census occurs.

verse_text:
This they shall give, every one that passeth among them that are numbered, half a shekel after the shekel of the sanctuary: (a shekel
is twenty gerahs:) an half shekel
shall be the offering of the LORD.
law_summary: Every person counted in the census must give a half shekel as an offering to the LORD
addressee_in_text: every one that passeth among them that are numbered
classification_reasoning: Conditional requirement triggered by census participation
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:13 - specific to those numbered in census
interpretive_questions: Where the offering goes without temple infrastructure
notes: Specifies the amount of the census offering: half a shekel by the sanctuary standard.

verse_text: Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above, shall give an offering unto the LORD.
law_summary: Every male from twenty years old and above who is counted in the census must give an offering to the LORD
addressee_in_text: Every one that passeth among them that are numbered, from twenty years old and above
classification_reasoning: Age-specific conditional requirement for census participants
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:14 - from twenty years old and above
interpretive_questions: Same as law 2396 regarding offering destination
notes: Age threshold for the census offering: twenty years old and above.

verse_text: And thou shalt take the atonement money of the children of Israel, and shalt appoint it for the service of the tabernacle of the congregation; that it may be a memorial unto the children of Israel before the LORD, to make an atonement for your souls.
law_summary: The atonement money collected from Israel must be designated for tabernacle service as a memorial and atonement before the LORD
classification_reasoning: Administrative instruction requiring tabernacle/temple service infrastructure
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:16 - 'appoint it for the service of the tabernacle'
interpretive_questions: Whether tabernacle service can be fulfilled without physical tabernacle
notes: Designates the atonement money for tabernacle service. Dual categorization because it governs both an offering and the funding of the sanctuary.

verse_text:
Thou shalt also make a laver
of brass, and his foot
also of brass, to wash
withal: and thou shalt put it between the tabernacle of the congregation and the altar, and thou shalt put water therein.
law_summary: Make a bronze laver with its base for washing and place it between the tabernacle and altar with water
classification_reasoning: Command to make laver for tabernacle worship, no explicit duration stated but part of tabernacle system
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:18 - direct command to make bronze laver for tabernacle
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to any future temple or only the original tabernacle
notes: Instruction to construct the bronze laver for priestly washing. Part of the tabernacle furnishings.

verse_text: For Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet thereat:
law_summary: Aaron and his sons must wash their hands and feet at the bronze basin
classification_reasoning: Priestly duty with no stated end, but requires functioning priesthood and sanctuary
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:19 - Aaron and his sons shall wash hands and feet
interpretive_questions: Application to Levitical priests versus other priestly lines
notes: Ongoing priestly duty to wash hands and feet at the laver.

verse_text: When they go into the tabernacle of the congregation, they shall wash with water, that they die not; or when they come near to the altar to minister, to burn offering made by fire unto the LORD:
law_summary: Priests must wash with water before entering the tabernacle or approaching the altar to minister
classification_reasoning: Ongoing priestly obligation tied to temple service, death penalty indicates seriousness
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:20 - wash with water before entering tabernacle or approaching altar
interpretive_questions: Whether death penalty applies in all future temple contexts
notes: Expands on the washing requirement: specifically when entering the tabernacle or approaching the altar. Death penalty attached for non-compliance.

verse_text:
Take thou also unto thee principal spices, of pure myrrh five hundred
shekels, and of sweet cinnamon half so much,
even two hundred and fifty
shekels, and of sweet calamus two hundred and fifty
shekels,
law_summary: Take specific amounts of principal spices: 500 shekels of pure myrrh, 250 shekels of sweet cinnamon, and 250 shekels of sweet calamus
classification_reasoning: Specific recipe for anointing oil, part of larger perpetual system but no explicit duration stated
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:23 - specific measurements of spices for anointing oil
interpretive_questions: Whether exact measurements required or if proportions are the key element
notes: Specifies the ingredients for the holy anointing oil. Part of a sequence (vv. 23-25) establishing the sacred anointing oil recipe for perpetual use.

verse_text: And thou shalt make it an oil of holy ointment, an ointment compound after the art of the apothecary: it shall be an holy anointing oil.
law_summary: Make holy anointing oil as a compound prepared by a skilled perfumer
classification_reasoning: Process command tied to holy anointing oil system, ongoing by implication
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:25 - make holy anointing oil by apothecary's art
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'art of the apothecary' and required skill level
notes: Command to compound the holy anointing oil as a perfumer's art.

verse_text: And thou shalt anoint the tabernacle of the congregation therewith, and the ark of the testimony,
law_summary: Anoint the tabernacle of the congregation and the ark of the testimony with holy anointing oil
classification_reasoning: Anointing requirement for tabernacle and ark, part of consecration system
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:26 - anoint tabernacle and ark with holy oil
interpretive_questions: Application to future temples beyond the original tabernacle
notes: Instruction to anoint the tabernacle and ark with the holy oil. Could be viewed as a one-time initial consecration, but it establishes the principle of anointing sacred objects.

verse_text: And thou shalt sanctify them, that they may be most holy: whatsoever toucheth them shall be holy.
law_summary: Items used to anoint the altar and its vessels must be sanctified to become most holy, and anything that touches them becomes holy
classification_reasoning: Principle of sanctification and holiness transfer, ongoing spiritual law
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:29 - sanctify them to be most holy, whatever touches them becomes holy
interpretive_questions: Extent and mechanics of holiness transfer in practice
notes: Sanctification of tabernacle items to 'most holy' status, with contagious holiness principle.

verse_text:
And thou shalt anoint Aaron and his sons, and consecrate them, that
they may minister unto me in the priest’s office.
law_summary: Aaron and his sons must be anointed and consecrated to serve as priests
classification_reasoning: Priestly consecration requirement, ongoing for priestly service
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:30 - anoint Aaron and sons to minister as priests
interpretive_questions: Application to subsequent generations of priests and method of consecration
notes: Anointing and consecration of Aaron and his sons for priestly office. Ongoing institutional instruction for priestly succession.

verse_text: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations.
law_summary: The holy anointing oil shall be set apart for God throughout all generations of Israel
classification_reasoning: Explicitly generational statute about holy anointing oil being set apart for God
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:31 - 'This shall be an holy anointing oil unto me throughout your generations'
interpretive_questions: Whether this refers to the specific oil batch or the institution of holy anointing oil
notes: Declares the holy anointing oil sacred throughout all generations. Explicitly perpetual.

verse_text:
Upon man’s flesh shall it not be poured, neither shall ye make
any other like it, after the composition of it: it
is
 holy,
and it shall be holy unto you.
law_summary: The holy anointing oil must not be poured on ordinary people's flesh, and no imitation oil with the same composition may be made
requires_specific_role: holy anointing oil and proper sanctuary context
classification_reasoning: Prohibition concerning holy anointing oil with eternal character ('it shall be holy unto you') but requires temple/priesthood context
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:32 - 'it is holy, and it shall be holy unto you'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'unto you' implies perpetual obligation or contextual holiness within sanctuary system
notes: Two prohibitions: (1) do not pour the holy oil on ordinary persons, (2) do not replicate the formula.

verse_text:
Whosoever compoundeth
any like it, or whosoever putteth
any of it upon a stranger, shall even be cut off from his people.
law_summary: Anyone who makes anointing oil like the sacred oil or puts it on a foreigner shall be cut off from the people
requires_specific_role: knowledge of sacred anointing oil composition
classification_reasoning: Capital punishment statute related to sacred anointing oil, ongoing prohibition but requires temple context for full application
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:33 - 'shall even be cut off from his people'
interpretive_questions: Whether prohibition applies when no legitimate sacred oil exists, definition of 'stranger' in context
notes: Penalty clause for violating the anointing oil prohibitions: being cut off from the people. The word 'stranger' here likely means an unauthorized person, not a foreigner per se.

law_summary: Take specific sweet spices (stacte, onycha, galbanum) and pure frankincense in equal weights
classification_reasoning: Specific instruction to Moses for initial incense preparation, not ongoing statute
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:34 - 'And the LORD said unto Moses, Take unto thee'
interpretive_questions: Whether this establishes pattern for ongoing incense preparation by others
notes: Specifies the ingredients for the sacred incense. Part of a sequence (vv. 34-38) establishing the incense recipe.

verse_text:
And thou shalt make it a perfume, a confection after the art of the apothecary, tempered together, pure
and holy:
law_summary: Make the incense as a perfume using the apothecary's art, properly mixed, pure and holy
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for sacred incense preparation, ongoing standard but requires temple context
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:35 - 'thou shalt make it a perfume...pure and holy'
interpretive_questions: Whether standard applies to any future temple incense or only tabernacle context
notes: Instruction on preparation method for the sacred incense.

verse_text:
And thou shalt beat
some of it very small, and put of it before the testimony in the tabernacle of the congregation, where I will meet with thee: it shall be unto you most holy.
law_summary: Beat some of the incense very small and place it before the testimony in the tabernacle where it shall be most holy
classification_reasoning: Specific procedure for incense placement in sanctuary, eternal holiness ('it shall be unto you most holy') but requires temple
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:36 - 'it shall be unto you most holy'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'most holy' designation continues without active temple service
notes: Placement of the incense before the testimony in the tabernacle.

verse_text:
And
as for the perfume which thou shalt make, ye shall not make to yourselves according to the composition thereof: it shall be unto thee holy for the LORD.
law_summary: The sacred incense recipe must not be used for personal purposes; it is holy to the Lord
classification_reasoning: Prohibition against personal use of sacred incense recipe, can be observed without temple if recipe known
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:37 - 'it shall be unto thee holy for the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether prohibition applies when no legitimate sacred incense exists for comparison
notes: Prohibition against replicating the sacred incense formula for personal use.

verse_text: Whosoever shall make like unto that, to smell thereto, shall even be cut off from his people.
law_summary: Anyone who makes incense like the sacred incense for personal use shall be cut off from the people
classification_reasoning: Capital punishment for making sacred incense for personal use, prohibition enforceable regardless of temple status
textual_evidence: Exodus 30:38 - 'shall even be cut off from his people'
interpretive_questions: How to identify forbidden incense composition without active temple standard
notes: Penalty for making incense like the sacred formula: cut off from the people.

verse_text:
And the anointing oil, and sweet incense for the holy
place:
 according to all that I have commanded thee shall they do.
law_summary: The anointing oil and sweet incense for the holy place must be made according to God's specifications
classification_reasoning: Reaffirmation of exact specifications for anointing oil and incense, requires active sanctuary
textual_evidence: Exodus 31:11 - 'according to all that I have commanded thee shall they do'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'all that I have commanded' creates perpetual standard for future temple service
notes: Reaffirms that the anointing oil and incense must be made according to God's specifications.

verse_text:
Speak thou also unto the children of Israel, saying, Verily my sabbaths ye shall keep: for it is a sign between me and you throughout your generations; that ye may know that I
am the LORD that doth sanctify you.
law_summary: Israel must keep the Sabbaths as a sign of the covenant with God
classification_reasoning: Explicit generational language makes Sabbath observance perpetual covenant sign for Israel
textual_evidence: Exodus 31:13 - 'throughout your generations; that ye may know that I am the LORD that doth sanctify you'
interpretive_questions: None - clear perpetual covenant sign
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Weekly Sabbath > Rest and Work Prohibition
notes: Sabbath as a covenantal sign between God and Israel throughout their generations.

verse_text:
Ye shall keep the sabbath therefore; for it
is holy unto you: every one that defileth it shall surely be put to death: for whosoever doeth
any work therein, that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
law_summary: Keep the Sabbath holy; anyone who defiles it by doing work shall be put to death or cut off from the people
classification_reasoning: Continues previous verse's perpetual Sabbath command with enforcement details, inherits generational scope
textual_evidence: Exodus 31:14 - 'Ye shall keep the sabbath therefore; for it is holy unto you'
interpretive_questions: Application of death penalty without temple court system
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Weekly Sabbath > Rest and Work Prohibition
notes: Sabbath enforcement with death penalty and being cut off for desecration through work.

verse_text: Observe thou that which I command thee this day: behold, I drive out before thee the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite.
law_summary: Observe and obey all the commands God gives
classification_reasoning: General principle of obedience to God's commands, fundamental covenant obligation
textual_evidence: Command to observe God's commandments repeated throughout Torah
interpretive_questions: None - clear ongoing obligation to obey God's commands
notes: General exhortation to observe God's commands. Serves as an introduction to the covenant renewal laws that follow.

verse_text: But ye shall destroy their altars, break their images, and cut down their groves:
law_summary: Israel must destroy the altars, images, and groves of other nations
classification_reasoning: Connected to the conquest period and the specific task of removing idolatrous infrastructure from the land
textual_evidence: Exodus 34:13 - imperative to destroy 'their' altars implies specific peoples' religious sites
interpretive_questions: Whether ongoing obligation exists to remove idolatrous objects when encountered
notes: Command to destroy pagan altars, images, and sacred groves (Asherah poles) in the land.

law_summary: Do not make covenants with the inhabitants of the land or participate in their sacrificial worship
classification_reasoning: Specifically about covenants with inhabitants of the land being entered, though principle of avoiding idolatrous worship continues
textual_evidence: Exodus 34:15 - 'inhabitants of the land' refers to specific peoples in promised land
interpretive_questions: Whether prohibition on eating sacrificial food extends beyond those specific inhabitants
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Separation from Idolatrous Nations
notes: Prohibition against covenants with the land's inhabitants and participating in their sacrificial worship.

verse_text: Thou shalt make thee no molten gods.
law_summary: Prohibition against making molten (cast metal) gods or idols
classification_reasoning: Absolute prohibition on idolatry with no temporal or geographical limitations
textual_evidence: Exodus 34:17 - categorical command 'make thee no molten gods'
interpretive_questions: None - clear and absolute prohibition
notes: Prohibition against making molten/cast metal gods.

verse_text: And thou shalt observe the feast of weeks, of the firstfruits of wheat harvest, and the feast of ingathering at the year’s end.
law_summary: Observe the feast of weeks (firstfruits of wheat harvest) and the feast of ingathering at year's end
classification_reasoning: Ongoing statute for appointed times, but feast of weeks requires firstfruits of wheat harvest and feast of ingathering requires agricultural land
textual_evidence: Exodus 34:22 - commanded as ongoing observance tied to agricultural cycles
interpretive_questions: Can these be observed without being in the land with agricultural cycles?
notes: Two feasts in one verse: Feast of Weeks (Shavuot, firstfruits of wheat) and Feast of Ingathering (Sukkot). Dual categorization warranted.

verse_text: Ye shall kindle no fire throughout your habitations upon the sabbath day.
law_summary: No fire shall be kindled in any dwelling place on the Sabbath day
classification_reasoning: Specific sabbath application, part of perpetual sabbath system, no prerequisites needed
textual_evidence: Exodus 35:3 - specific sabbath restriction, connected to perpetual sabbath laws
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'kindling' in modern context?
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Weekly Sabbath > Rest and Work Prohibition
notes: Specific Sabbath prohibition: kindling fire. Unique to this verse; not repeated elsewhere in exactly this form.

verse_text:
Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, If any man of you bring an offering unto the LORD, ye shall bring your offering of the cattle,
even of the herd, and of the flock.
law_summary: When bringing an offering to the LORD, it must be from cattle of the herd or flock
classification_reasoning: Conditional law applying when bringing offerings - requires proper animals from herd/flock
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:2 - 'If any man of you bring an offering' - conditional structure
interpretive_questions: Whether offerings can be brought without temple/priesthood system
notes: General introduction to the offering laws: offerings must come from cattle, herd, or flock.

verse_text:
If his offering
be a burnt sacrifice of the herd, let him offer a male without blemish: he shall offer it of his own voluntary will at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD.
law_summary: Burnt offerings from cattle must be unblemished males offered voluntarily at the tabernacle entrance
classification_reasoning: Conditional procedural requirement for burnt offerings - specifies unblemished male at tabernacle
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:3 - 'If his offering be a burnt sacrifice' - conditional structure requiring tabernacle
interpretive_questions: Whether burnt offering requirements apply in absence of tabernacle/temple
notes: Burnt offering from the herd: must be unblemished male, offered voluntarily at the tabernacle entrance.

verse_text: And he shall put his hand upon the head of the burnt offering; and it shall be accepted for him to make atonement for him.
law_summary: The person offering a burnt offering must place his hand upon the head of the animal for it to be accepted as atonement
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for burnt offerings - hand laying for atonement acceptance
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:4 - hand placement requirement for burnt offering acceptance and atonement
interpretive_questions: Whether this procedure is valid without functioning priesthood/altar system
notes: Laying on of hands for the burnt offering. This act of identification is integral to the offering's acceptance.

verse_text:
And he shall kill the bullock before the LORD: and the priests, Aaron’s sons, shall bring the blood, and sprinkle the blood round about upon the altar that
is by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
law_summary: The person offering a bullock sacrifice must kill it before the LORD, and the priests must bring and sprinkle the blood around the altar
classification_reasoning: Procedural law requiring both offerer and Aaronic priests - involves altar at tabernacle
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:5 - requires killing before LORD and priests sprinkling blood on altar at tabernacle
interpretive_questions: Whether any aspect can be fulfilled without both priesthood and altar
notes: Procedure for slaughtering the bull and priestly blood sprinkling.

verse_text: And he shall flay the burnt offering, and cut it into his pieces.
law_summary: The one offering a burnt offering must skin it and cut it into pieces
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for burnt offering preparation - skinning and cutting into pieces
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:6 - offerer must flay and cut burnt offering into pieces
interpretive_questions: Whether this procedure has meaning without altar system for completion
notes: The offerer must skin and cut the burnt offering into pieces.

verse_text: And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar, and lay the wood in order upon the fire:
law_summary: The sons of Aaron must put fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire
classification_reasoning: Priestly obligation for altar fire maintenance - requires both Aaronic priesthood and altar
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:7 - sons of Aaron must put fire on altar and arrange wood
interpretive_questions: Whether this connects to perpetual fire requirement in Leviticus 6:13
notes: Priestly duty to put fire and arrange wood on the altar for burnt offerings.

verse_text:
And the priests, Aaron’s sons, shall lay the parts, the head, and the fat, in order upon the wood that
is on the fire which
is
 upon the altar:
law_summary: Priests must arrange the sacrificial parts, head, and fat in proper order on the altar fire
classification_reasoning: Procedural instruction for burnt offerings given as ongoing statute but requires functional temple, altar, and Aaronic priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:8 - direct command to Aaron's sons regarding altar procedures
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to any legitimate altar or specifically the tabernacle/temple altar
notes: Priestly arrangement of sacrificial parts on the altar fire.

verse_text:
But his inwards and his legs shall he wash in water: and the priest shall burn all on the altar,
to be a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
law_summary: The inwards and legs of a burnt offering must be washed in water before the priest burns the entire offering on the altar
other_torah_refs: Part of broader burnt offering procedures in Leviticus 1, Leviticus 1:13
classification_reasoning: Ongoing procedural requirement for burnt offerings requiring functional sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:9 - command for priest to wash and burn offering parts
interpretive_questions: Relationship between washing requirement and ritual purity concepts
notes: Washing of inwards and legs before burning the entire offering. Completes the burnt offering procedure for cattle.

verse_text:
And if his offering
be of the flocks,
namely, of the sheep, or of the goats, for a burnt sacrifice; he shall bring it a male without blemish.
law_summary: If offering a sheep or goat for burnt sacrifice, it must be a male without blemish
other_torah_refs: Similar requirements for unblemished animals throughout Leviticus
classification_reasoning: Conditional requirement (if offering from flocks) but depends on sacrificial system being operational
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:10 - conditional 'if his offering be of the flocks' with requirement for male without blemish
interpretive_questions: Whether the 'without blemish' standard could apply to other contexts beyond temple sacrifice
notes: Burnt offering from the flock (sheep or goats): must be an unblemished male.

verse_text: And he shall kill it on the side of the altar northward before the LORD: and the priests, Aaron’s sons, shall sprinkle his blood round about upon the altar.
law_summary: Burnt offerings must be killed on the north side of the altar, and priests must sprinkle the blood around the altar
other_torah_refs: Blood sprinkling procedures repeated throughout sacrificial laws
classification_reasoning: Specific procedural requirements for altar location and priestly duties requiring functional temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:11 - specific location 'north side of the altar' and priestly blood sprinkling duties
interpretive_questions: Significance of the north side specification and its permanent requirement
notes: Flock burnt offerings must be killed on the north side of the altar.

verse_text:
And he shall cut it into his pieces, with his head and his fat: and the priest shall lay them in order on the wood that
is on the fire which
is upon the altar:
law_summary: The offerer must cut the burnt offering into pieces including head and fat, and the priest must arrange them in order on the altar fire
other_torah_refs: Similar cutting and arranging procedures in Leviticus 1
classification_reasoning: Procedural division of labor between offerer and priest requiring functional sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:12 - offerer cuts into pieces, priest arranges on altar fire
interpretive_questions: Whether the specific cutting procedures have significance beyond the sacrificial context
notes: Cutting and arranging flock burnt offering pieces on the altar.

verse_text:
And if the burnt sacrifice for his offering to the LORD
be
 of fowls, then he shall bring his offering of turtledoves, or of young pigeons.
law_summary: If offering a bird as burnt sacrifice, it must be turtledoves or young pigeons
classification_reasoning: Conditional provision for those who cannot afford larger animals, but still requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:14 - conditional 'if the burnt sacrifice...be of fowls' specifying turtledoves or young pigeons
interpretive_questions: Whether this represents an accommodation for economic circumstances or separate category
notes: Bird burnt offerings: must be turtledoves or young pigeons.

verse_text:
And the priest shall bring it unto the altar, and wring off his head, and burn
it on the altar; and the blood thereof shall be wrung out at the side of the altar:
law_summary: The priest must bring a bird offering to the altar, wring off its head, burn it on the altar, and drain its blood at the side of the altar
other_torah_refs: Bird sacrifice procedures unique to this context
classification_reasoning: Specific priestly procedures for bird offerings requiring functional altar and priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:15 - priest brings to altar, wrings head, burns, drains blood at altar side
interpretive_questions: Why bird offerings require different procedures from animal offerings
notes: Procedure for bird burnt offering: wringing off head, burning, draining blood.

verse_text: And he shall pluck away his crop with his feathers, and cast it beside the altar on the east part, by the place of the ashes:
law_summary: When offering a bird sacrifice, the priest must remove the crop with its feathers and cast it beside the altar on the east side where the ashes are placed
classification_reasoning: Specific procedure for bird offerings requiring altar with designated ash placement area
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:16 - crop and feathers removed and cast 'beside the altar on the east part, by the place of the ashes'
interpretive_questions: Significance of the east side placement and relationship to ash removal procedures
notes: Removal of crop and feathers from bird burnt offering, cast on the east side.

verse_text:
And he shall cleave it with the wings thereof,
but shall not divide
it asunder: and the priest shall burn it upon the altar, upon the wood that
is upon the fire: it
is a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
law_summary: The bird for burnt offering must be cleaved at the wings but not completely divided, then burned by the priest on the altar
classification_reasoning: Final procedural step for bird offerings requiring precise handling and priestly burning on altar
textual_evidence: Leviticus 1:17 - cleave at wings but not divide completely, priest burns on altar wood
interpretive_questions: Symbolic significance of partial division versus complete cutting as with animal offerings
notes: Final step for bird burnt offering: cleave at wings but do not divide completely, then burn on the altar.

verse_text:
And when any will offer a meat offering unto the LORD, his offering shall be
of fine flour; and he shall pour oil upon it, and put frankincense thereon:
law_summary: When offering a grain offering to the LORD, it must be made of fine flour with oil poured on it and frankincense placed on top
classification_reasoning: Part of sacrificial system requiring altar and priesthood; no explicit temporal limitations but prerequisites not currently available
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:1 - procedural instruction for grain offerings
interpretive_questions: Whether 'when any will offer' implies ongoing validity or situational context
notes: Basic grain offering: fine flour with oil and frankincense.

verse_text:
And he shall bring it to Aaron’s sons the priests: and he shall take thereout his handful of the flour thereof, and of the oil thereof, with all the frankincense thereof; and the priest shall burn the memorial of it upon the altar,
to be an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD:
law_summary: The priest shall take a handful of flour, oil, and frankincense from the grain offering and burn it as a memorial portion on the altar
other_torah_refs: Leviticus 2:9, similar memorial portions throughout Leviticus
classification_reasoning: Priestly procedure requiring functioning priesthood and altar; no temporal limitations stated
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:2 - specific priestly duties in grain offering ritual
interpretive_questions: None significant - clear procedural instruction
notes: Memorial portion procedure: priest takes a handful of flour, oil, and all frankincense to burn on the altar.

verse_text:
And the remnant of the meat offerings
shall be Aaron’s and his sons’:
it is a thing most holy of the offerings of the LORD made by fire.
law_summary: The remainder of grain offerings belongs to Aaron and his sons as most holy portions
classification_reasoning: Priestly portion allocation requiring active priesthood and sacrificial system; described as 'most holy'
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:3 - designation of priestly portion as 'most holy'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'most holy' designation implies perpetual status
notes: Remainder of grain offerings belongs to Aaron and his sons as a most holy priestly portion.

verse_text:
And if thou bring an oblation of a meat offering baken in the oven,
it shall be unleavened cakes of fine flour mingled with oil, or unleavened wafers anointed with oil.
law_summary: Meat offerings baked in the oven must be made of fine flour with oil, either as unleavened cakes mixed with oil or unleavened wafers anointed with oil
classification_reasoning: Specific preparation method for grain offerings; conditional on offering context but no stated limitations
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:4 - 'if thou bring' indicates conditional but ongoing applicability
interpretive_questions: None - clear procedural specification
notes: Oven-baked grain offering: unleavened cakes with oil or unleavened wafers anointed with oil.

verse_text:
And if thy oblation
be a meat offering
baken in a pan, it shall be
of fine flour unleavened, mingled with oil.
law_summary: Meat offerings baked in a pan must be made of fine flour without leaven, mixed with oil
classification_reasoning: Alternative preparation method for grain offerings; part of comprehensive offering system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:5 - conditional 'if thy oblation be' suggests ongoing validity
interpretive_questions: None - straightforward procedural instruction
notes: Pan-baked grain offering: fine flour, unleavened, mixed with oil.

verse_text:
Thou shalt part it in pieces, and pour oil thereon: it
is
 a meat offering.
law_summary: Break the grain offering into pieces and pour oil on it
classification_reasoning: Specific handling procedure for prepared grain offering; part of sacrificial protocol
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:6 - imperative 'thou shalt' indicates binding instruction
interpretive_questions: None - clear procedural command
notes: Break the pan-baked grain offering into pieces and pour oil on it.

verse_text:
And if thy oblation
be a meat offering
baken in the frying pan, it shall be made
of fine flour with oil.
law_summary: Meat offerings baked in a frying pan must be made of fine flour with oil
classification_reasoning: Third cooking method option for grain offerings; completes the preparation alternatives
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:7 - conditional 'if thy oblation be' maintains consistency with other methods
interpretive_questions: None - follows established pattern
notes: Frying pan grain offering: fine flour with oil.

verse_text: And thou shalt bring the meat offering that is made of these things unto the LORD: and when it is presented unto the priest, he shall bring it unto the altar.
law_summary: Bring the grain offering made of these ingredients to the LORD, and the priest shall present it at the altar
other_torah_refs: General bringing of offerings throughout Leviticus
classification_reasoning: Transfer protocol from offerer to priest to altar; requires functioning sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:8 - 'thou shalt bring' indicates ongoing obligation when offering
interpretive_questions: None - clear procedural chain
notes: The grain offering must be brought to the LORD and presented by the priest at the altar.

verse_text: No meat offering, which ye shall bring unto the LORD, shall be made with leaven: for ye shall burn no leaven, nor any honey, in any offering of the LORD made by fire.
law_summary: Grain offerings to the LORD must not contain leaven or honey when offered by fire
classification_reasoning: Ongoing statute for grain offerings with no termination clause, but requires functional temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:11 - prohibition applies to offerings 'which ye shall bring unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to non-temple grain offerings or only fire offerings on the altar
notes: Prohibition against leaven and honey in fire offerings. This is about what is prohibited in offerings rather than the grain offering procedure itself.

verse_text: As for the oblation of the firstfruits, ye shall offer them unto the LORD: but they shall not be burnt on the altar for a sweet savour.
law_summary: Firstfruit oblations must be offered to the LORD but not burned on the altar for a sweet savor
classification_reasoning: Procedural statute for firstfruit offerings with no termination clause, requires altar and temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:12 - 'ye shall offer them unto the LORD: but they shall not be burnt on the altar'
interpretive_questions: How firstfruits are to be offered if not burned on the altar in temple context
notes: Firstfruit oblations must be offered to the LORD but not burned on the altar as a sweet savor. Distinguishes firstfruit offerings from regular fire offerings.

verse_text: And every oblation of thy meat offering shalt thou season with salt; neither shalt thou suffer the salt of the covenant of thy God to be lacking from thy meat offering: with all thine offerings thou shalt offer salt.
law_summary: All grain offerings must be seasoned with salt and salt must be included with all offerings
classification_reasoning: References 'covenant of thy God' suggesting perpetual nature, but requires offerings system. Salt covenant language implies permanence
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:13 - 'salt of the covenant of thy God' and 'with all thine offerings thou shalt offer salt'
interpretive_questions: Whether salt covenant language extends beyond temple offerings to all sacred meals or offerings
notes: Salt requirement for all offerings. Referred to as the 'salt of the covenant.' Applies broadly across all offering types.

verse_text:
And if thou offer a meat offering of thy firstfruits unto the LORD, thou shalt offer for the meat offering of thy firstfruits green ears of corn dried by the fire,
even corn beaten out of full ears.
law_summary: When offering a grain offering of firstfruits, it must be made from green ears of grain dried by fire and beaten from full ears
classification_reasoning: Explicitly conditional ('if thou offer') with specific requirements, no termination clause
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:14 - 'And if thou offer a meat offering of thy firstfruits unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to agricultural firstfruits or broader firstfruit offerings
notes: Firstfruit grain offering: green ears dried by fire, beaten from full ears.

verse_text:
And thou shalt put oil upon it, and lay frankincense thereon: it
is a meat offering.
law_summary: Oil and frankincense must be put on the meat offering
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for grain offerings with no termination clause, requires temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 2:15 - 'And thou shalt put oil upon it, and lay frankincense thereon'
interpretive_questions: Whether oil and frankincense requirements apply to all grain offerings or specific types
notes: Oil and frankincense must be added to the firstfruit grain offering.

verse_text:
And if his oblation
be a sacrifice of peace offering, if he offer
it of the herd; whether
it be a male or female, he shall offer it without blemish before the LORD.
law_summary: Peace offerings from cattle must be without blemish, whether male or female
classification_reasoning: General requirement for all peace offerings with no termination clause, requires temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:1 - 'he shall offer it without blemish before the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'before the LORD' requires temple location or any sacred context
notes: Peace offerings from the herd: male or female, without blemish.

verse_text:
And he shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering, and kill it
at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron’s sons the priests shall sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.
law_summary: The offerer must lay hands on the peace offering and kill it at the tabernacle entrance, while priests sprinkle its blood on the altar
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement specifying tabernacle location and Aaronic priests, no termination clause
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:2 - 'at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron's sons the priests'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to temple as well as tabernacle, and to all Aaronic descendants
notes: Laying on of hands, slaughtering at tabernacle entrance, priestly blood sprinkling for peace offerings.

verse_text:
And he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the LORD; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that
is upon the inwards,
law_summary: The fat covering and surrounding the inner organs must be offered as a fire offering to the LORD from peace offerings
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for peace offerings with no termination clause, requires altar and fire offerings
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:3 - 'he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether fat restrictions apply outside of sacrificial context
notes: Fat portions of peace offerings that must be offered as fire offerings.

verse_text:
And the two kidneys, and the fat that
is on them, which
is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.
law_summary: The kidneys, surrounding fat, and liver lobe must be removed from peace offerings
classification_reasoning: Priestly procedural duty with no termination clause, requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:4 - 'it shall he take away' (referring to priest's duty)
interpretive_questions: Whether 'he' refers to offerer or priest, and application to non-sacrificial slaughter
notes: Specific internal organs to be removed from peace offerings: kidneys, fat, liver lobe.

verse_text:
And Aaron’s sons shall burn it on the altar upon the burnt sacrifice, which
is upon the wood that
is on the fire:
it is
 an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
law_summary: Aaron's sons must burn the peace offering on the altar upon the burnt sacrifice
classification_reasoning: Part of ongoing sacrificial system but requires altar, temple service, and Aaronic priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:5 specifies Aaron's sons must perform this on the altar
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all future altars or only tabernacle/temple altar
notes: Priestly duty to burn the peace offering fat on the altar atop the burnt sacrifice.

verse_text:
And if his offering for a sacrifice of peace offering unto the LORD
be of the flock; male or female, he shall offer it without blemish.
law_summary: Peace offerings from the flock must be male or female animals without blemish
classification_reasoning: Standard for peace offerings whenever sacrificial system operates; no termination indicated
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:6 establishes ongoing standard for flock offerings
interpretive_questions: Whether standard applies to any future sacrificial context
notes: Peace offerings from the flock: male or female, without blemish.

verse_text: If he offer a lamb for his offering, then shall he offer it before the LORD.
law_summary: If offering a lamb as a sacrifice, it must be presented before the LORD
classification_reasoning: Conditional requirement ('if he offer') for proper presentation before the LORD
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:7 'If he offer a lamb... then shall he offer it before the LORD'
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'before the LORD' in different contexts
notes: Lamb peace offering must be presented before the LORD.

verse_text:
And the priest shall burn it upon the altar:
it is the food of the offering made by fire unto the LORD.
law_summary: The priest must burn the peace offering upon the altar as food offered by fire to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Priestly duty to burn offering as 'food' for the LORD - ongoing obligation
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:11 'the priest shall burn it... it is the food of the offering made by fire'
interpretive_questions: Theological meaning of offerings as 'food' for the LORD
notes: Priest must burn the peace offering on the altar as 'food of the offering made by fire.'

verse_text:
And if his offering
be a goat, then he shall offer it before the LORD.
law_summary: If someone offers a goat as a sacrifice, it must be presented before the LORD
classification_reasoning: Conditional requirement parallel to lamb offering - same principle applied to goats
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:12 'if his offering be a goat, then he shall offer it before the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether goats have same status as lambs for peace offerings
notes: Goat peace offering must be presented before the LORD.

verse_text:
And the priest shall burn them upon the altar:
it is the food of the offering made by fire for a sweet savour: all the fat
is
 the LORD’S.
law_summary: All fat belongs to the LORD and must not be consumed
classification_reasoning: Dietary prohibition with universal application to Israel, reinforced by perpetual statute in following verse
textual_evidence: Leviticus 3:16 - 'all the fat is the LORD'S'
interpretive_questions: Scope of 'fat' - all animal fat or specific sacrificial fat context
notes: All fat belongs to the LORD. This is a general principle applying across all offerings, not just peace offerings.

verse_text:
Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a soul shall sin through ignorance against any of the commandments of the LORD
concerning things
 which ought not to be done, and shall do against any of them:
law_summary: Instructions for handling unintentional sins against God's commandments
classification_reasoning: Framework for handling unintentional sin through sacrificial system - principle ongoing but method requires priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:2 - addresses 'children of Israel' regarding unintentional sins
interpretive_questions: Whether alternative methods for unintentional sin exist without temple system
notes: Introduction to the sin offering laws for unintentional sins. Addressed to the children of Israel.

verse_text: If the priest that is anointed do sin according to the sin of the people; then let him bring for his sin, which he hath sinned, a young bullock without blemish unto the LORD for a sin offering.
law_summary: If the anointed priest sins, he must bring a young unblemished bull as a sin offering to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Conditional law ('If the priest...sins') requiring specific priestly role and sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:3 - 'If the priest that is anointed do sin'
interpretive_questions: Identity of 'anointed priest' - high priest specifically or broader category
notes: Sin offering for the anointed priest: a young unblemished bull.

verse_text: And he shall bring the bullock unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the LORD; and shall lay his hand upon the bullock’s head, and kill the bullock before the LORD.
law_summary: The high priest must bring a bull to the tabernacle entrance, lay his hand on its head, and kill it before the LORD as a sin offering
classification_reasoning: Procedural instruction for high priest sin offering requiring tabernacle/temple structure
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:4 - specific procedure 'unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation'
interpretive_questions: Application to future temple versus tabernacle-specific instructions
notes: Procedure for the priest's sin offering: bring bull to tabernacle entrance, lay hands, kill before the LORD.

verse_text: And the priest that is anointed shall take of the bullock’s blood, and bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation:
law_summary: The anointed priest must take blood from the sacrificial bull and bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation
classification_reasoning: Continuation of sin offering procedure requiring temple structure and anointed priest
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:5 - 'bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation'
interpretive_questions: Whether procedure adapts to different temple structures or remains tabernacle-specific
notes: Blood handling for the priest's sin offering: take blood to the tabernacle.

verse_text: And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle of the blood seven times before the LORD, before the vail of the sanctuary.
law_summary: The priest must dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the LORD in front of the sanctuary veil
classification_reasoning: Specific ritual procedure requiring sanctuary veil and priestly function
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:6 - 'before the vail of the sanctuary'
interpretive_questions: Adaptation of procedure for different temple configurations
notes: Seven-fold sprinkling of blood before the LORD in front of the sanctuary veil.

verse_text:
And the priest shall put
some of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the LORD, which
is in the tabernacle of the congregation: and shall pour all the blood of the bullock at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which
is at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
law_summary: The priest must put blood on the horns of the incense altar and pour remaining blood at the base of the burnt offering altar during sin offering ritual
classification_reasoning: Detailed altar procedure requiring specific temple furniture and priestly role
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:7 - references both incense altar and burnt offering altar in tabernacle
interpretive_questions: Exact correspondence of altar specifications across different temple periods
notes: Blood application: on the incense altar horns and remaining blood poured at the base of the burnt offering altar.

verse_text:
And he shall take off from it all the fat of the bullock for the sin offering; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that
is
 upon the inwards,
law_summary: The priest must remove all the fat from the bullock used for a sin offering, including the fat covering the internal organs and all fat upon the internal organs
classification_reasoning: Sacrificial procedure continuing the sin offering ritual requiring priestly function
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:8 - procedural instruction for handling sacrificial fat
interpretive_questions: Relationship to the perpetual fat prohibition in Leviticus 3:17
notes: Fat removal from the priest's sin offering bull.

verse_text: As it was taken off from the bullock of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall burn them upon the altar of the burnt offering.
law_summary: The priest shall burn the fat portions upon the altar of burnt offering, following the same procedure as peace offerings
classification_reasoning: Ongoing procedural law for sacrificial system with cross-reference to peace offerings
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:10 - 'as it was taken off from the bullock of the sacrifice of peace offerings'
interpretive_questions: Relationship between sin offering and peace offering procedures
notes: Fat portions of the sin offering burned on the altar, following the same procedure as peace offerings.

verse_text: When the sin, which they have sinned against it, is known, then the congregation shall offer a young bullock for the sin, and bring him before the tabernacle of the congregation.
law_summary: When the congregation becomes aware of their sin, they must offer a young bull as a sin offering and bring it before the tabernacle
classification_reasoning: Conditional law triggered by recognition of sin, applies to congregation as ongoing possibility
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:14 - 'When the sin, which they have sinned against it, is known'
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'congregation' and types of sins requiring this offering
notes: Congregational sin offering: young bull brought before the tabernacle when the community becomes aware of their sin.

verse_text: And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock before the LORD: and the bullock shall be killed before the LORD.
law_summary: The elders of the congregation must lay their hands on the head of the bullock before it is killed as a sin offering
classification_reasoning: Hand-laying procedure integral to sacrificial system, no termination indicated
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:15 - 'elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock'
interpretive_questions: Role and selection of congregation elders in this context
notes: Elders lay hands on behalf of the congregation for the communal sin offering.

verse_text:
And he shall take all his fat from him, and burn
it upon the altar.
law_summary: The priest must remove all fat from the sin offering and burn it on the altar
classification_reasoning: Fat burning requirement consistent across sacrifice types, ongoing procedural obligation
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:19 - 'take all his fat from him, and burn it upon the altar'
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'all his fat' and relationship to other fat-burning requirements
notes: Fat removal and burning for the congregational sin offering.

verse_text: And he shall do with the bullock as he did with the bullock for a sin offering, so shall he do with this: and the priest shall make an atonement for them, and it shall be forgiven them.
law_summary: The priest shall perform the same ritual with the bullock for the congregation's sin offering as with the priest's sin offering, and make atonement for forgiveness
classification_reasoning: Cross-references earlier procedure establishing consistent ritual pattern for atonement
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:20 - same procedure 'and it shall be forgiven them'
interpretive_questions: Scope of atonement and forgiveness achieved through this ritual
notes: Congregational sin offering follows the same procedure as the priest's sin offering. Atonement results in forgiveness.

verse_text: Or if his sin, wherein he hath sinned, come to his knowledge; he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a male without blemish:
law_summary: A ruler who becomes aware of his sin must bring a male goat without blemish as a sin offering
other_torah_refs: Part of broader sin offering system in Leviticus 4
classification_reasoning: Conditional law triggered by awareness of sin, no temporal limitations given, but requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:23 - conditional 'if his sin...come to his knowledge'
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'ruler' in modern context and alternative atonement methods
notes: Sin offering for a ruler: male goat without blemish.

verse_text:
And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat, and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the LORD: it
is
 a sin offering.
law_summary: The one bringing a sin offering must lay his hand on the goat's head and kill it in the designated place for burnt offerings
other_torah_refs: Hand-laying procedure repeated throughout Leviticus sacrificial laws, Leviticus 4:29, Leviticus 4:33
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for sin offerings with no time limitation, but requires designated sacrificial location
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:24 - prescriptive procedure 'he shall lay his hand'
interpretive_questions: Whether procedure could be adapted without temple infrastructure
notes: Procedure for ruler's sin offering: lay hands, kill at the burnt offering location.

verse_text: Or if his sin, which he hath sinned, come to his knowledge: then he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a female without blemish, for his sin which he hath sinned.
law_summary: If a common person becomes aware of their unintentional sin, they must bring a female goat without blemish as a sin offering
other_torah_refs: Parallel to law 2819 for rulers, part of Leviticus 4 sin offering system, Numbers 15:27
classification_reasoning: Conditional obligation triggered by awareness of sin, no temporal limits, but requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:28 - 'if his sin...come to his knowledge: then he shall bring'
interpretive_questions: Modern application of sin awareness principle without temple sacrifice
notes: Sin offering for a common person: female goat without blemish.

verse_text:
And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat is taken away from off the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn
it upon the altar for a sweet savour unto the LORD; and the priest shall make an atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him.
law_summary: The priest must remove all fat from the sin offering and burn it on the altar to make atonement
addressee_in_text: priests making atonement through fat burning
other_torah_refs: Similar to law 2822, fat burning throughout Leviticus sacrifice laws, Leviticus 4:35
classification_reasoning: Priestly atonement procedure identical in principle to law 2822, requires altar for 'sweet savour unto the LORD'
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:31 - 'priest shall burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Significance of 'sweet savour' aspect in atonement process
notes: Fat removal and burning for common person's sin offering, with atonement and forgiveness.

verse_text: And if he bring a lamb for a sin offering, he shall bring it a female without blemish.
law_summary: If bringing a lamb for a sin offering, it must be a female without blemish
addressee_in_text: anyone bringing lamb for sin offering
other_torah_refs: Alternative to goat in previous laws, part of sin offering options
classification_reasoning: Conditional specification for lamb alternative, no time limits but requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 4:32 - 'if he bring a lamb...he shall bring it a female without blemish'
interpretive_questions: Choice between goat and lamb offerings and underlying principles
notes: Alternative sin offering for a common person: female lamb without blemish.

verse_text:
And if a soul sin, and hear the voice of swearing, and
is
 a witness, whether he hath seen or known
of it; if he do not utter
it, then he shall bear his iniquity.
law_summary: A witness who hears an oath or adjuration and fails to testify about what they have seen or known shall bear their iniquity
classification_reasoning: Moral/judicial obligation for witnesses with no prerequisites or temporal limitations
textual_evidence: Leviticus 5:1 - applies to 'a soul' generally, states consequence of bearing iniquity
interpretive_questions: Does this apply only in formal judicial settings or all witness situations?
notes: Obligation to testify when one is a witness. Failure to do so incurs guilt. This is about the duty to come forward as a witness.

verse_text:
Or if a soul touch any unclean thing, whether
it be a carcase of an unclean beast, or a carcase of unclean cattle, or the carcase of unclean creeping things, and
if it be hidden from him; he also shall be unclean, and guilty.
law_summary: A person who touches any unclean carcass becomes unclean and guilty, even if unaware at the time
classification_reasoning: Purity law defining uncleanness from contact with unclean carcasses, no temporal or infrastructural limitations
textual_evidence: Leviticus 5:2 - universal application to 'a soul', defines guilt condition
interpretive_questions: Are the categories of clean/unclean animals still binding for determining contamination?
categories: LOVE_GOD > PURITY_AND_HOLINESS > Clean and Unclean Animals
notes: Touching unclean carcasses renders a person unclean and guilty, even if unaware at the time. Establishes the principle of inadvertent contamination.

verse_text:
Or if he touch the uncleanness of man, whatsoever uncleanness
it be
 that a man shall be defiled withal, and it be hid from him; when he knoweth
of it, then he shall be guilty.
