Thou shalt not curse the deaf, nor put a stumblingblock before the blind, but shalt fear thy God: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Do not curse the deaf or put obstacles before the blind
classification_reasoning: Moral prohibition protecting vulnerable people with divine authority backing. No temporal or conditional limitations stated.
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:14 - specific prohibitions with 'fear thy God' and 'I am the LORD' statements
interpretive_questions: Modern applications regarding treatment of disabled individuals in various contexts

verse_text:
Thou shalt not go up and down
as a talebearer among thy people: neither shalt thou stand against the blood of thy neighbour: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Do not go about as a talebearer among your people, and do not stand by when your neighbor's life is in danger
classification_reasoning: Social/moral law protecting community harmony and human life with divine authority. No temporal limitations stated.
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:16 - dual prohibition with 'I am the LORD' authority statement
interpretive_questions: Defining scope of obligation to intervene when neighbor's life is endangered
notes: Contains two distinct commands: prohibition against tale-bearing and the obligation not to stand idly by when a neighbor's life is at risk.

verse_text: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thine heart: thou shalt in any wise rebuke thy neighbour, and not suffer sin upon him.
law_summary: Do not harbor hatred in your heart against your brother, but rebuke your neighbor directly and do not let sin remain upon him
classification_reasoning: Moral law governing interpersonal relationships and conflict resolution. Fundamental principle with no stated temporal limits.
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:17 - prohibition against hatred with positive command for direct rebuke
interpretive_questions: Practical application of rebuke obligation in various relationship contexts
notes: Contains both a prohibition (do not hate your brother) and a positive command (rebuke your neighbor).

verse_text:
Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Do not take revenge or hold grudges against fellow Israelites, but love your neighbor as yourself
classification_reasoning: Core moral law including the great commandment to love neighbor. Divine authority with no temporal limitations.
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:18 - prohibition against revenge/grudges with positive love command and 'I am the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Scope of 'children of thy people' - applies to all humanity or specifically fellow covenant people
notes: Contains three commands: no vengeance, no grudge-bearing, and the positive command to love your neighbor as yourself.

verse_text: Ye shall keep my statutes. Thou shalt not let thy cattle gender with a diverse kind: thou shalt not sow thy field with mingled seed: neither shall a garment mingled of linen and woollen come upon thee.
law_summary: Prohibition against mixing different kinds: breeding different species of cattle, sowing mixed seeds in fields, and wearing garments of mixed linen and wool
classification_reasoning: Statute explicitly called 'my statutes' with command to keep them. No temporal limitations. Garment law applies universally, agriculture laws require land.
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:19 - 'Ye shall keep my statutes' introduces three mixing prohibitions
interpretive_questions: Purpose and scope of mixing prohibitions - symbolic, practical, or ceremonial distinctions
notes: Three distinct prohibited mixtures: animal cross-breeding, mixed seeds, and mixed-fabric garments (shatnez).

verse_text:
And whosoever lieth carnally with a woman, that
is a bondmaid, betrothed to an husband, and not at all redeemed, nor freedom given her; she shall be scourged; they shall not be put to death, because she was not free.
law_summary: If a man has sexual relations with a betrothed female slave who has not been redeemed or freed, she shall be scourged but neither shall be put to death because she was not free
classification_reasoning: Specific case law for slavery context with prescribed punishment. Slavery system as described no longer exists.
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:20 - specific scenario involving betrothed bondmaid with particular legal status
interpretive_questions: Whether underlying principles about consent and legal status apply in modern contexts
notes: Addresses a specific case of sexual relations with a betrothed bondmaid. Spans both betrothal laws and servant laws.

verse_text:
And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the LORD, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation,
even a ram for a trespass offering.
law_summary: A man who has sexual relations with a betrothed slave woman must bring a ram as a trespass offering to the door of the tabernacle
addressee_in_text: he - man in the specific situation
classification_reasoning: Sacrifice law requiring tabernacle/temple system. Connected to previous case law. Prerequisites not currently available.
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:21 - trespass offering 'unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation'
interpretive_questions: Whether alternative forms of restitution apply when temple sacrifice unavailable
notes: The required guilt offering for the sin described in 19:20.

verse_text: And the priest shall make an atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering before the LORD for his sin which he hath done: and the sin which he hath done shall be forgiven him.
law_summary: The priest must make atonement with a ram trespass offering for the person's sin, and the sin will be forgiven
classification_reasoning: Requires functioning priesthood and sacrificial system, no explicit perpetual language but part of ongoing legal code
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:22 - 'the priest shall make an atonement'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to the specific context of Leviticus 19:20-21 or to trespass offerings generally
notes: Continuation of the atonement procedure from 19:21; the priest's role in the guilt offering.

verse_text: And when ye shall come into the land, and shall have planted all manner of trees for food, then ye shall count the fruit thereof as uncircumcised: three years shall it be as uncircumcised unto you: it shall not be eaten of.
law_summary: When you plant fruit trees in the land, the fruit is forbidden to eat for the first three years
classification_reasoning: Ongoing agricultural statute with conditional land reference but principle applies wherever fruit trees are planted
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:23 - 'when ye shall come into the land' - conditional on planting activity
interpretive_questions: Whether 'the land' restricts application to Israel only or applies to fruit tree planting anywhere
notes: The law of orlah -- fruit from newly planted trees is forbidden for three years. Categorized under dietary holiness as it pertains to forbidden consumption of produce.

verse_text:
But in the fourth year all the fruit thereof shall be holy to praise the LORD
withal.
law_summary: In the fourth year, all fruit from trees must be consecrated as holy to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Continuation of fruit tree law from verse 23, part of ongoing agricultural statute
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:24 - 'shall be holy to praise the LORD' - ongoing obligation
interpretive_questions: How to consecrate fruit as holy without temple system; whether applies outside the land
notes: Fourth-year fruit is holy to the LORD, consecrated as praise. Functions as a dedication/offering of produce.

verse_text:
And in the fifth year shall ye eat of the fruit thereof, that it may yield unto you the increase thereof: I
am the LORD your God.
law_summary: In the fifth year after planting a fruit tree, you may eat its fruit so that it will increase its yield
classification_reasoning: Final part of fruit tree statute, ongoing agricultural law with divine signature 'I am the LORD your God'
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:25 - 'shall ye eat' with divine authority statement
interpretive_questions: Same geographical scope questions as previous fruit tree laws
notes: Completes the orlah sequence: fifth-year fruit may be eaten freely.

verse_text: Ye shall not round the corners of your heads, neither shalt thou mar the corners of thy beard.
law_summary: Do not cut the hair at the corners of your head or trim the edges of your beard
classification_reasoning: Ongoing purity statute with no conditional requirements, universal applicability
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:27 - direct prohibition 'shall not'
interpretive_questions: Precise definition of 'corners' and extent of prohibition
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Separation from Idolatrous Nations
notes: Traditionally understood as prohibiting pagan mourning customs. Could also fit under Purity and Holiness, but the context links it to separation from idolatrous practices.

verse_text:
Ye shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead, nor print any marks upon you: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Prohibition against making cuts in the flesh for the dead or tattooing marks on the body
classification_reasoning: Universal prohibition with divine authority statement, repeated in Deuteronomy, no conditional requirements
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:28 - 'shall not' with 'I am the LORD' authority
interpretive_questions: Whether prohibition extends to all body markings or specifically pagan mourning practices
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Separation from Idolatrous Nations
notes: Prohibitions against cuttings for the dead and tattoos, associated with pagan mourning and worship practices.

verse_text: Do not prostitute thy daughter, to cause her to be a whore; lest the land fall to whoredom, and the land become full of wickedness.
law_summary: Prohibition against prostituting one's daughter
classification_reasoning: Moral prohibition with broader societal concern, ongoing applicability with divine reasoning provided
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:29 - prohibition with consequence 'lest the land fall to whoredom'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'the land' reference makes this geographically specific to Israel

verse_text:
Regard not them that have familiar spirits, neither seek after wizards, to be defiled by them: I
am the LORD your God.
law_summary: Do not consult mediums or wizards
classification_reasoning: Universal moral prohibition with divine authority, repeated in Deuteronomy, no conditional requirements
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:31 with 'I am the LORD your God' authority statement
interpretive_questions: Specific definitions of 'familiar spirits' and 'wizards' in modern context

verse_text:
Thou shalt rise up before the hoary head, and honour the face of the old man, and fear thy God: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: You shall stand up in the presence of elderly people and honor old men
classification_reasoning: Universal moral/social law with no temporal limitations or special prerequisites; part of general holiness code
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:32 - direct command with divine authority statement 'I am the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Application to non-Israelites; definition of 'hoary head' and 'old man'
notes: Command to honor the elderly. Could arguably fit under Honor Parents, but the scope is broader -- it applies to all elderly, not just one's parents.

verse_text:
But the stranger that dwelleth with you shall be unto you as one born among you, and thou shalt love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt: I
am the LORD your God.
law_summary: Treat strangers dwelling among you as native-born and love them as yourself
classification_reasoning: Fundamental moral principle with divine authority; historical rationale provided; repeatedly emphasized
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:34 - command to love stranger 'as thyself' with divine signature 'I am the LORD your God'
interpretive_questions: Distinction between 'sojourn' vs 'dwell'; practical implementation of equal treatment

verse_text: Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment, in meteyard, in weight, or in measure.
law_summary: Do not use dishonest standards in judgment, length, weight, or measure
classification_reasoning: Universal principle of honesty in commerce and judgment; no temporal or location restrictions
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:35 - comprehensive prohibition covering judgment, meteyard, weight, and measure
interpretive_questions: Modern applications to different measurement systems and commercial practices
notes: Prohibition against dishonest measures in judgment and commerce.

verse_text:
Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have: I
am the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt.
law_summary: Use accurate and honest weights and measures in commerce
classification_reasoning: Positive command for honest commerce with redemption rationale; universal moral principle
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:36 - specific requirement for 'just balances, just weights' with divine authority and historical rationale
interpretive_questions: Application to modern measurement standards and commercial instruments
notes: Positive command to maintain just weights and measures; companion to 19:35.

verse_text:
Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Observe all of God's statutes and judgments
other_torah_refs: Throughout Torah - statutes and judgments repeatedly commanded
classification_reasoning: Meta-command to observe all divine laws; reinforces perpetual obligation to Torah observance
textual_evidence: Leviticus 19:37 - comprehensive command to observe 'all my statutes, and all my judgments' with divine signature
interpretive_questions: Which statutes require temple/land vs universal application; scope of 'all'
notes: General exhortation to observe all of God's statutes and judgments.

verse_text:
Again, thou shalt say to the children of Israel, Whosoever
he be
 of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that sojourn in Israel, that giveth
any of his seed unto Molech; he shall surely be put to death: the people of the land shall stone him with stones.
law_summary: Anyone who sacrifices their child to Molech must be executed by stoning
addressee_in_text: children of Israel and strangers that sojourn in Israel
classification_reasoning: Fundamental prohibition against child sacrifice; applies to all in covenant community; capital punishment shows severity
textual_evidence: Leviticus 20:2 - death penalty for child sacrifice to Molech, applies to Israelites and sojourning strangers
interpretive_questions: Implementation of capital punishment outside theocratic state; modern equivalent practices
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Worship of False Gods
notes: Prescribes the death penalty by stoning for Molech worship. Applies to Israelites and sojourners alike.

verse_text: And if the people of the land do any ways hide their eyes from the man, when he giveth of his seed unto Molech, and kill him not:
law_summary: The community must not ignore or fail to execute someone who sacrifices their children to Molech
classification_reasoning: Community responsibility for justice; requires organized judicial system and authority to execute
textual_evidence: Leviticus 20:4 - prohibition against ignoring child sacrifice, implies community obligation for justice
interpretive_questions: Authority structure needed for implementation; community vs individual responsibility
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Worship of False Gods
notes: Prohibits the community from ignoring Molech worship; communal responsibility to execute justice.

verse_text: Then I will set my face against that man, and against his family, and will cut him off, and all that go a whoring after him, to commit whoredom with Molech, from among their people.
law_summary: God will cut off from the people anyone who worships Molech and their family
addressee_in_text: that man and his family (divine punishment)
classification_reasoning: Divine punishment for Molech worship; God's direct action, not requiring human implementation
textual_evidence: Leviticus 20:5 - 'I will set my face against' and 'will cut him off' - divine action
interpretive_questions: Nature of divine cutting off; family liability extent; timing of divine judgment
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Worship of False Gods
notes: Divine penalty for Molech worshippers and their families when the community fails to act.

verse_text:
And the man that committeth adultery with
another man’s wife,
even he that committeth adultery with his neighbour’s wife, the adulterer and the adulteress shall surely be put to death.
law_summary: Both the adulterer and adulteress shall be executed for committing adultery
classification_reasoning: Prohibition against adultery is ongoing moral law, but death penalty requires judicial authority
textual_evidence: Law appears in multiple contexts without temporal limitation, indicating permanence
interpretive_questions: Whether prohibition remains while penalty awaits proper judicial system
notes: Prescribes death penalty for both parties in adultery.

verse_text:
And the man that lieth with his father’s wife hath uncovered his father’s nakedness: both of them shall surely be put to death; their blood
shall be upon them.
law_summary: A man who has sexual relations with his father's wife must be put to death, along with the woman
classification_reasoning: Sexual prohibition is permanent moral law, but death penalty requires judicial system
textual_evidence: Prohibition repeated in Leviticus 18 and Deuteronomy, indicating ongoing moral standard
interpretive_questions: Application of penalty in absence of proper judicial authority
notes: Prescribes the death penalty for the act prohibited in Leviticus 18:8.

verse_text:
And if a man lie with his daughter in law, both of them shall surely be put to death: they have wrought confusion; their blood
shall be
 upon them.
law_summary: A man who has sexual relations with his daughter-in-law shall be put to death, along with the woman
classification_reasoning: Sexual prohibition is permanent, death penalty requires proper judicial system
textual_evidence: Also prohibited in Leviticus 18, showing consistent moral standard across Torah
interpretive_questions: Whether moral prohibition remains binding while penalty awaits proper implementation
notes: Prescribes the death penalty for the act prohibited in Leviticus 18:15.

verse_text:
And if a man take a wife and her mother, it
is wickedness: they shall be burnt with fire, both he and they; that there be no wickedness among you.
law_summary: A man who marries both a woman and her mother must be executed by burning, along with both women
classification_reasoning: Called 'wickedness' indicating permanent moral category, execution requires judicial authority
textual_evidence: Also addressed in Leviticus 18:17, and purpose stated 'that there be no wickedness among you'
interpretive_questions: Whether specific execution method (burning) is essential or if general prohibition remains primary
notes: Prescribes death by burning for marrying a woman and her mother.

verse_text:
And if a woman approach unto any beast, and lie down thereto, thou shalt kill the woman, and the beast: they shall surely be put to death; their blood
shall be upon them.
law_summary: A woman who has sexual relations with an animal must be put to death, along with the animal
classification_reasoning: Bestiality prohibition is permanent moral law, death penalty requires proper judicial system
textual_evidence: Consistent with male bestiality law and referenced in Leviticus 18:23
interpretive_questions: Implementation of penalties without established judicial authority
notes: Prescribes the death penalty for bestiality by a woman; companion penalty to 18:23.

verse_text:
And if a man shall take his sister, his father’s daughter, or his mother’s daughter, and see her nakedness, and she see his nakedness; it
is a wicked thing; and they shall be cut off in the sight of their people: he hath uncovered his sister’s nakedness; he shall bear his iniquity.
law_summary: A man who has sexual relations with his sister (either half-sister or full sister) commits a grave sin and both parties shall be cut off from their people
classification_reasoning: Moral prohibition against incest with no temporal limitations stated; part of holiness code applicable to Israel
textual_evidence: Leviticus 20:17 - prohibition with punishment clearly stated without time limitations
interpretive_questions: Whether 'cut off' requires communal action or divine punishment
notes: Prescribes karet (cutting off) for incest with a sister; penalty for the act prohibited in 18:9.

verse_text: Ye shall therefore keep all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: that the land, whither I bring you to dwell therein, spue you not out.
law_summary: Keep all God's statutes and judgments to avoid being expelled from the Promised Land
classification_reasoning: Explicitly conditional on dwelling in the land; consequence is expulsion from the land
textual_evidence: Leviticus 20:22 - 'that the land, whither I bring you to dwell therein, spue you not out'
interpretive_questions: Whether applies to individual Israelites outside the land or only to collective Israel in the land
notes: General exhortation to keep God's statutes; warns the land will expel those who violate them.

verse_text: And ye shall not walk in the manners of the nation, which I cast out before you: for they committed all these things, and therefore I abhorred them.
law_summary: Do not follow the customs and practices of the nations that God cast out before Israel
classification_reasoning: Moral prohibition against pagan practices with no temporal limitations; principle extends beyond specific nations
textual_evidence: Leviticus 20:23 - prohibition against walking in manners of cast-out nations
interpretive_questions: Scope of 'manners' - whether all customs or specifically abominable practices mentioned in context
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Separation from Idolatrous Nations

verse_text:
A man also or woman that hath a familiar spirit, or that is a wizard, shall surely be put to death: they shall stone them with stones: their blood
shall be upon them.
law_summary: Those who practice necromancy or divination must be executed by stoning
classification_reasoning: Capital punishment requires judicial system and community authority; prohibition principle remains
textual_evidence: Leviticus 20:27 - death penalty by stoning for familiar spirits and wizards
interpretive_questions: Whether prohibition remains applicable without ability to execute prescribed penalty
notes: Prescribes death by stoning for mediums and wizards.

verse_text: And the LORD said unto Moses, Speak unto the priests the sons of Aaron, and say unto them, There shall none be defiled for the dead among his people:
law_summary: Priests must not defile themselves by contact with dead bodies among their people
classification_reasoning: Part of ongoing priestly statutes without explicit temporal limitation, but requires functioning Aaronic priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:1 - 'Speak unto the priests the sons of Aaron' indicates ongoing institutional requirement
interpretive_questions: Whether Aaronic priesthood can function without temple service
notes: General prohibition on priestly corpse contamination, with exceptions in following verses.

verse_text:
But for his kin, that is near unto him,
that is, for his mother, and for his father, and for his son, and for his daughter, and for his brother,
law_summary: Priests may defile themselves for close family members (mother, father, son, daughter, brother)
classification_reasoning: Exception to general prohibition for priests, part of ongoing priestly code without temporal limitation
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:2 - Exception clause within priestly purity laws, implies ongoing application when priesthood functions
interpretive_questions: Scope of 'close family members' and application without temple
notes: Defines the exceptions to the priest's prohibition on corpse defilement: immediate family members.

verse_text: And for his sister a virgin, that is nigh unto him, which hath had no husband; for her may he be defiled.
law_summary: A priest may become ritually defiled for his unmarried virgin sister who is close to him
classification_reasoning: Continuation of family exception rules for priests, no temporal limitation specified
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:3 - Specific exception within priestly purity statutes
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'virgin sister' and 'close to him' criteria
notes: Extends the exception for the priest to defile himself for an unmarried virgin sister.

verse_text:
But he shall not defile himself,
being a chief man among his people, to profane himself.
law_summary: A priest who is a chief among his people must not defile himself in ways that would profane him
requires_specific_role: priest who is a chief man
addressee_in_text: priest as chief among his people
classification_reasoning: Higher standard for priests in leadership roles, part of ongoing priestly code
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:4 - 'chief man among his people' indicates leadership responsibility requiring higher purity
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'chief man' and what constitutes profaning oneself
notes: A priest who is a leader must not defile himself in ways that profane his status.

verse_text:
They shall not take a wife
that is a whore, or profane; neither shall they take a woman put away from her husband: for he
is holy unto his God.
law_summary: Priests are prohibited from marrying prostitutes, profaned women, or divorced women because of their holy status
classification_reasoning: Marriage restrictions for priests based on holiness, not temple-dependent
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:7 - 'for he is holy unto his God' shows permanent holy status requiring marriage restrictions
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'profaned women' and application without functioning priesthood
notes: Marriage restrictions for ordinary priests.

verse_text:
Thou shalt sanctify him therefore; for he offereth the bread of thy God: he shall be holy unto thee: for I the LORD, which sanctify you,
am
 holy.
law_summary: The people must sanctify/treat as holy the priest who offers bread to God
classification_reasoning: Command to Israel to honor priests due to their sacrificial role
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:8 - 'Thou shalt sanctify him' is command to people, 'for he offereth' shows basis in priestly function
interpretive_questions: How to sanctify priests without active temple service
notes: Obligation on the people of Israel to sanctify/honor the priest.

verse_text: And the daughter of any priest, if she profane herself by playing the whore, she profaneth her father: she shall be burnt with fire.
law_summary: A priest's daughter who becomes a prostitute must be executed by burning
classification_reasoning: Capital punishment law for specific case involving priest's family, requires both priesthood and judicial authority
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:9 - 'daughter of any priest' shows ongoing application to priestly families
interpretive_questions: Requires functioning judicial system and identified priesthood; method of execution specificity
notes: Penalty of burning for a priest's daughter who engages in prostitution, due to the profanation of her father's holiness.

verse_text:
And
he that is the high priest among his brethren, upon whose head the anointing oil was poured, and that is consecrated to put on the garments, shall not uncover his head, nor rend his clothes;
law_summary: The high priest must not uncover his head or tear his clothes
classification_reasoning: Conduct requirements for high priest related to his consecrated status and garments
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:10 - 'anointing oil was poured...consecrated to put on the garments' shows ongoing status requirements
interpretive_questions: Whether high priest position exists without temple, and meaning of 'uncover his head'
notes: Restrictions specific to the high priest: not uncovering head or tearing garments.

verse_text: Neither shall he go in to any dead body, nor defile himself for his father, or for his mother;
law_summary: The high priest must not defile himself by contact with any dead body, including his father or mother
addressee_in_text: Moses (to convey to Aaron and his sons)
classification_reasoning: Law specifically for high priest role with no explicit temporal limitation, but requires functioning priesthood and temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:11 - direct prohibition for high priest regarding dead body contact
interpretive_questions: Does this apply to any future high priest or only to Aaron's immediate lineage?
notes: Unlike ordinary priests, the high priest may not defile himself even for parents.

verse_text:
Neither shall he go out of the sanctuary, nor profane the sanctuary of his God; for the crown of the anointing oil of his God
is upon him: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: The high priest must not leave the sanctuary or profane it
addressee_in_text: Moses (to convey to Aaron and his sons)
classification_reasoning: Directly tied to sanctuary service which requires functioning temple and priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:12 - prohibition against leaving sanctuary or profaning it
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'leaving the sanctuary' in practical terms?
notes: High priest must not leave the sanctuary during service or profane it.

verse_text: And he shall take a wife in her virginity.
law_summary: The high priest must marry a virgin
addressee_in_text: Moses (to convey to Aaron and his sons)
classification_reasoning: Personal conduct law for high priest with no temporal limitation stated
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:13 - direct command for high priest to marry virgin
interpretive_questions: Does this apply independently of temple service or only while serving?
notes: Marriage requirement for the high priest: must marry a virgin.

verse_text:
A widow, or a divorced woman, or profane,
or an harlot, these shall he not take: but he shall take a virgin of his own people to wife.
law_summary: The high priest must marry only a virgin from his own people, not a widow, divorced woman, defiled woman, or harlot
addressee_in_text: Moses (to convey to Aaron and his sons)
classification_reasoning: Specific marriage restrictions for high priest with no stated time limitation
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:14 - prohibition list and positive command for virgin of his people
interpretive_questions: What defines 'his own people' in diaspora contexts?
notes: Stricter marriage restrictions for the high priest than for ordinary priests.

verse_text: Neither shall he profane his seed among his people: for I the LORD do sanctify him.
law_summary: The high priest must not profane his descendants among his people
addressee_in_text: Moses (to convey to Aaron and his sons)
classification_reasoning: Prohibition about profaning descendants tied to sanctification by the LORD
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:15 - 'for I the LORD do sanctify him' indicates ongoing divine sanctification
interpretive_questions: What specific actions constitute 'profaning his seed'?
notes: The high priest must not profane his offspring through improper marriages.

verse_text:
Speak unto Aaron, saying, Whosoever
he be of thy seed in their generations that hath
any blemish, let him not approach to offer the bread of his God.
law_summary: Any descendant of Aaron who has a physical blemish must not approach to offer the bread of God
classification_reasoning: Explicitly states 'in their generations' indicating perpetual application for all future generations of Aaron's lineage
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:17 - 'in their generations' phrase establishes ongoing temporal scope
interpretive_questions: Does this apply to all Aaronic descendants or only those actively serving?
notes: Blemished descendants of Aaron are disqualified from offering sacrifices.

verse_text:
For whatsoever man
he be that hath a blemish, he shall not approach: a blind man, or a lame, or he that hath a flat nose, or any thing superfluous,
law_summary: Men with physical blemishes or deformities shall not approach to serve as priests
addressee_in_text: Moses (to convey to Aaron and his sons)
classification_reasoning: Continuation of blemish prohibitions with no temporal limitation, part of broader generational statute
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:18 - specific physical disqualifications for priestly service
interpretive_questions: Are these absolute disqualifications or contextual to specific services?
notes: Specifies categories of blemishes that disqualify priestly service.

verse_text: Or a man that is brokenfooted, or brokenhanded,
law_summary: Men with broken feet or hands are disqualified from priestly service
addressee_in_text: Moses (to convey to Aaron and his sons)
classification_reasoning: Part of comprehensive list of physical disqualifications, inherits generational scope from context
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:19 - continuation of verse 18's prohibition list
interpretive_questions: Do temporary injuries disqualify or only permanent conditions?
notes: Continuation of the list of disqualifying physical blemishes.

verse_text: Or crookbackt, or a dwarf, or that hath a blemish in his eye, or be scurvy, or scabbed, or hath his stones broken;
law_summary: Priests with physical defects including hunchback, dwarfism, eye blemishes, skin diseases, or damaged testicles are disqualified from offering sacrifices
addressee_in_text: Moses (to convey to Aaron and his sons)
classification_reasoning: Continuation of blemish list, part of statute with generational language established in verse 17
textual_evidence: Leviticus 21:20 - additional specific physical disqualifications
interpretive_questions: How are these conditions defined medically in application?
notes: Continuation of the list of disqualifying physical blemishes for priestly service.

verse_text:
Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, that they separate themselves from the holy things of the children of Israel, and that they profane not my holy name
in those things which they hallow unto me: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Priests must separate themselves from the holy things of Israel and not profane God's holy name in consecrated offerings
other_torah_refs: Multiple references to not profaning God's name throughout Torah, Leviticus 22:15
classification_reasoning: General principle for priests regarding sanctity, but tied to holy things system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:2 - 'Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, that they separate themselves from the holy things'
interpretive_questions: Whether separation principle applies outside of formal temple service context
notes: Priests must treat holy things with reverence and not profane God's name through mishandling them.

verse_text:
Say unto them, Whosoever
he be of all your seed among your generations, that goeth unto the holy things, which the children of Israel hallow unto the LORD, having his uncleanness upon him, that soul shall be cut off from my presence: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Any descendant of Aaron who approaches holy things while ritually unclean shall be cut off from God's presence
addressee_in_text: all your seed among your generations
classification_reasoning: Explicitly states 'among your generations' indicating perpetual application, but requires holy things system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:3 - 'Whosoever he be of all your seed among your generations, that goeth unto the holy things'
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'holy things' in future temple contexts
notes: Penalty of karet for an unclean priest who approaches holy things.

verse_text:
What man soever of the seed of Aaron
is a leper, or hath a running issue; he shall not eat of the holy things, until he be clean. And whoso toucheth any thing
that is unclean
by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;
law_summary: Priests with leprosy or discharge cannot eat holy things until clean; those touching corpse-unclean things or having seminal emissions are also prohibited
classification_reasoning: Ongoing purity requirements for priests accessing holy things
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:4 - 'What man soever of the seed of Aaron is a leper, or hath a running issue; he shall not eat of the holy things'
interpretive_questions: Modern application of leprosy identification and discharge definitions
notes: Specifies conditions of impurity that prevent priests from eating holy food.

verse_text: The soul which hath touched any such shall be unclean until even, and shall not eat of the holy things, unless he wash his flesh with water.
law_summary: One who touches unclean things must remain unclean until evening and cannot eat holy things unless they wash with water
classification_reasoning: Purity requirement tied to eating holy things, which requires functioning temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:6 - 'shall be unclean until even, and shall not eat of the holy things, unless he wash his flesh with water'
interpretive_questions: Whether washing principle applies outside of holy things context
notes: Purification requirement for priests touching unclean things before they may eat holy food.

verse_text:
And when the sun is down, he shall be clean, and shall afterward eat of the holy things; because it
is his food.
law_summary: A priest who was unclean becomes clean at sunset and may then eat of the holy things
other_torah_refs: Multiple sunset purification references in Leviticus 15
classification_reasoning: Procedural law for priest purification tied to eating holy things
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:7 - 'when the sun is down, he shall be clean, and shall afterward eat of the holy things'
interpretive_questions: Sunset timing calculations and geographical considerations
notes: Priest becomes clean at sunset and may then eat holy food.

verse_text:
That which dieth of itself, or is torn
with beasts, he shall not eat to defile himself therewith: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Prohibition against eating animals that died naturally or were killed by wild beasts
classification_reasoning: Dietary prohibition with no specific prerequisites, repeated elsewhere for broader application
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:8 - 'That which dieth of itself, or is torn with beasts, he shall not eat'
interpretive_questions: Whether context limits this to priests only or applies generally
notes: Prohibition against eating nevelah (died naturally) and treifah (torn by beasts). Though in priestly context here, the prohibition applies broadly.

verse_text: They shall therefore keep mine ordinance, lest they bear sin for it, and die therefore, if they profane it: I the LORD do sanctify them.
law_summary: Priests must keep God's ordinances or face death for profaning them
other_torah_refs: Multiple warnings about priestly obligations throughout Leviticus
classification_reasoning: General obligation for priests to keep ordinances, but tied to temple service context
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:9 - 'They shall therefore keep mine ordinance, lest they bear sin for it, and die therefore'
interpretive_questions: Which specific ordinances are referenced and their scope of application
notes: General exhortation to priests to keep God's ordinances regarding holy things, with severe penalty for profaning them.

verse_text:
There shall no stranger eat
of the holy thing: a sojourner of the priest, or an hired servant, shall not eat
of the holy thing.
law_summary: Strangers, sojourners of priests, and hired servants are prohibited from eating holy things
classification_reasoning: Restriction on eating holy things requires functioning temple and priestly system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:10 - 'There shall no stranger eat of the holy thing'
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'stranger' and whether this includes righteous converts
notes: Restricts consumption of holy food to the priestly family; strangers, sojourners of priests, and hired servants are excluded.

verse_text:
But if the priest buy
any soul with his money, he shall eat of it, and he that is born in his house: they shall eat of his meat.
law_summary: A priest's purchased slaves and those born in his house may eat of the holy food
classification_reasoning: Part of ongoing priestly statute regarding holy food consumption, but requires functioning priesthood and temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:11 - refers to 'holy meat' which implies temple sacrificial system
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'holy meat' outside temple context? Does this apply to all priestly families or only those serving?
notes: Exception: purchased servants and those born in the priest's household may eat holy food.

verse_text:
If the priest’s daughter also be
married unto a stranger, she may not eat of an offering of the holy things.
law_summary: A priest's daughter who marries outside the priestly family cannot eat the holy offerings
classification_reasoning: Ongoing statute about priestly family eating privileges, but depends on temple system providing holy offerings
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:12 - refers to 'offering of the holy things' which requires temple
interpretive_questions: What defines a 'stranger' in this context? Does this apply to marriage to non-Israelites or just non-priests?
notes: A priest's daughter who marries a non-priest loses her right to eat holy offerings.

verse_text: But if the priest’s daughter be a widow, or divorced, and have no child, and is returned unto her father’s house, as in her youth, she shall eat of her father’s meat: but there shall no stranger eat thereof.
law_summary: A priest's daughter who is widowed or divorced with no children may return to her father's house and eat of the holy food, but no stranger may eat it
classification_reasoning: Conditional ongoing statute for priestly family restoration rights, but requires temple system for holy food
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:13 - 'her father's meat' refers to priestly portions from temple
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'returned to father's house'? Does 'no child' mean never had children or currently childless?
notes: A widowed or divorced priest's daughter without children may return to eating holy food in her father's house.

verse_text:
And if a man eat
of the holy thing unwittingly, then he shall put the fifth
part thereof unto it, and shall give
it
 unto the priest with the holy thing.
law_summary: If someone accidentally eats sacred food, they must repay the priest with the value plus an additional fifth (20% penalty)
classification_reasoning: Ongoing restitution law for accidental consumption, but requires temple system to have holy food available
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:14 - 'holy thing' and giving to 'the priest' implies active temple system
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'unwittingly'? How is the value determined without temple pricing system?
notes: Restitution plus one-fifth penalty for inadvertent consumption of holy food.

verse_text: Or suffer them to bear the iniquity of trespass, when they eat their holy things: for I the LORD do sanctify them.
law_summary: Priests must not allow unauthorized persons to eat holy things, lest they bear guilt for trespass
classification_reasoning: Ongoing responsibility for priests to prevent unauthorized access, but requires temple system with holy things
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:16 - 'I the LORD do sanctify them' refers to the holy things from temple
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'suffering them to bear iniquity'? Is this active permission or passive negligence?
notes: Priests bear responsibility for preventing unauthorized consumption of holy things.

verse_text: Ye shall offer at your own will a male without blemish, of the beeves, of the sheep, or of the goats.
law_summary: Voluntary offerings must be unblemished males from cattle, sheep, or goats
classification_reasoning: Ongoing statute for voluntary offerings with specific standards, but requires temple system for offering
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:19 - 'ye shall offer' implies ongoing practice with established system
interpretive_questions: Can voluntary offerings be made outside temple system? What constitutes 'at your own will'?
notes: Offerings must be unblemished males from cattle, sheep, or goats.

verse_text:
But whatsoever hath a blemish,
that shall ye not offer: for it shall not be acceptable for you.
law_summary: Do not offer any animal with a blemish as a sacrifice, as it will not be acceptable
classification_reasoning: Ongoing prohibition with repeated emphasis throughout Torah, but requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:20 - prohibition stated as ongoing principle repeated multiple times
interpretive_questions: Does this principle extend to other offerings to God outside formal sacrifice? What defines 'blemish'?

verse_text: Blind, or broken, or maimed, or having a wen, or scurvy, or scabbed, ye shall not offer these unto the LORD, nor make an offering by fire of them upon the altar unto the LORD.
law_summary: Animals with physical defects (blind, broken, maimed, or having skin conditions) must not be offered as sacrifices to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Detailed ongoing prohibition specifying unacceptable defects, but requires temple altar system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:22 - 'upon the altar unto the LORD' specifically requires temple altar
interpretive_questions: Do these specific defects represent exhaustive list or examples? Can the principle apply outside formal sacrifice?
notes: Detailed specification of which blemishes disqualify an animal from being offered.

verse_text:
Either a bullock or a lamb that hath any thing superfluous or lacking in his parts, that mayest thou offer
for a freewill offering; but for a vow it shall not be accepted.
law_summary: Animals with physical defects may be offered as freewill offerings but not as vow offerings
classification_reasoning: Part of sacrificial system regulations with no temporal limitations stated, but requires functioning temple/priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:23 - distinguishes between freewill offerings and vow offerings regarding defective animals
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all animal offerings or only temple sacrifices
notes: Partial exception: animals with minor defects may be used for freewill offerings but not for vows.

verse_text:
Ye shall not offer unto the LORD that which is bruised, or crushed, or broken, or cut; neither shall ye make
any offering thereof in your land.
law_summary: Prohibition against offering animals with damaged or mutilated reproductive organs to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Prohibition on offering defective animals with no time limit, but requires temple system for offerings
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:24 - 'Ye shall not offer unto the LORD' - direct prohibition to Israel
interpretive_questions: Scope of 'offering' - whether limited to formal temple sacrifices
notes: Prohibits offering animals with damaged reproductive organs.

verse_text:
Neither from a stranger’s hand shall ye offer the bread of your God of any of these; because their corruption
is in them,
and
 blemishes
be in them: they shall not be accepted for you.
law_summary: Do not offer sacrificial animals obtained from foreigners that have defects or corruption
classification_reasoning: Prohibition regarding sacrificial animals from foreigners, no temporal limitation but requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:25 - 'shall ye offer the bread of your God' indicates ongoing sacrificial context
interpretive_questions: Whether 'corruption' refers only to physical defects or broader impurity
notes: Blemished animals from foreigners are also unacceptable as offerings.

verse_text:
And
whether it be cow or ewe, ye shall not kill it and her young both in one day.
law_summary: Do not slaughter a mother animal and her offspring on the same day

textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:28 - 'ye shall not kill it and her young both in one day' - general prohibition
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to ritual slaughter or all slaughter
notes: Prohibition against slaughtering a mother and offspring on the same day. Reflects both offering law and compassionate treatment of animals.

verse_text:
And when ye will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto the LORD, offer
it at your own will.
law_summary: Thanksgiving sacrifices must be offered voluntarily and willingly
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for thanksgiving sacrifices, no time limit but requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:29 - 'when ye will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Meaning of 'at your own will' - whether referring to voluntary timing or attitude
notes: Thanksgiving offering is a subcategory of peace offerings.

verse_text:
On the same day it shall be eaten up; ye shall leave none of it until the morrow: I
am the LORD.
law_summary: Sacrificial meat must be eaten on the same day it is offered and none left until the next morning
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for consuming sacrificial meat, no time limit but requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:30 - 'On the same day it shall be eaten up' with divine authority 'I am the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all sacrificial meat or only thanksgiving offerings
notes: Time restriction on eating the thanksgiving offering: must be consumed the same day.

verse_text:
Therefore shall ye keep my commandments, and do them: I
am
 the LORD.
law_summary: Keep and observe God's commandments

textual_evidence: Leviticus 22:31 - 'Therefore shall ye keep my commandments, and do them: I am the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Scope of 'my commandments' - whether all Torah or contextual sacrificial laws
notes: General exhortation to keep God's commandments.

verse_text:
Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them,
Concerning
 the feasts of the LORD, which ye shall proclaim
to be holy convocations,
even these
are my feasts.
law_summary: Israel must proclaim the LORD's feasts as holy convocations
classification_reasoning: Command to proclaim LORD's feasts as holy convocations, can be observed without temple though some aspects require it
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:2 - 'ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, even these are my feasts'
interpretive_questions: Whether proclamation requires formal authority or can be done by individuals/communities
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Weekly Sabbath > Holy Convocation
notes: General introductory command to proclaim the appointed feasts as holy convocations. Could apply to all feasts, but serves as the header statement for the sacred times calendar.

verse_text:
In the fourteenth
day of the first month at even
is
 the LORD’S passover.
law_summary: The LORD's Passover must be observed on the fourteenth day of the first month at evening
classification_reasoning: Passover timing established as permanent calendar appointment, confirmed by repetition and 'forever' language in related passages
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:5 - definitive statement 'is the LORD'S passover' establishes permanent timing
interpretive_questions: Calendar system - whether lunar/solar calendar differences affect precise timing
notes: Establishes the date for Passover.

verse_text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come into the land which I give unto you, and shall reap the harvest thereof, then ye shall bring a sheaf of the firstfruits of your harvest unto the priest:
law_summary: Bring a sheaf of the firstfruits of the harvest to the priest when reaping in the Promised Land
classification_reasoning: Explicitly conditional on being in the land and having harvest; requires priesthood for fulfillment
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:10 - 'When ye be come into the land which I give unto you, and shall reap the harvest thereof'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all agricultural areas or specifically the Promised Land boundaries
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Unleavened Bread > Firstfruits and Omer

verse_text: And he shall wave the sheaf before the LORD, to be accepted for you: on the morrow after the sabbath the priest shall wave it.
law_summary: The priest must wave the sheaf offering before the LORD on the day after the sabbath for acceptance
classification_reasoning: Requires functioning priesthood and harvest context; depends on fulfillment of previous law
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:11 - 'the priest shall wave it' - specific priestly function required
interpretive_questions: Whether waving can occur without temple or requires temple courtyard
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Unleavened Bread > Firstfruits and Omer
notes: The priest waves the sheaf on the day after the sabbath.

verse_text: And ye shall offer that day when ye wave the sheaf an he lamb without blemish of the first year for a burnt offering unto the LORD.
law_summary: Offer a yearling male lamb without blemish as a burnt offering on the day of waving the sheaf
classification_reasoning: Burnt offering requires altar and priesthood; connected to harvest/land conditions of previous laws
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:12 - 'burnt offering unto the LORD' requires sacrificial altar system
interpretive_questions: None - clearly requires full temple sacrificial system
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Unleavened Bread > Firstfruits and Omer
notes: Burnt offering accompanying the wave sheaf offering.

verse_text:
And the meat offering thereof
shall be two tenth deals of fine flour mingled with oil, an offering made by fire unto the LORD
for
 a sweet savour: and the drink offering thereof
shall be of wine, the fourth
part of an hin.
law_summary: The grain offering must be two-tenths of fine flour mixed with oil, and the drink offering must be one-fourth hin of wine
classification_reasoning: Grain and drink offerings are part of temple sacrificial system; procedural details for priestly service
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:13 - 'offering made by fire unto the LORD' indicates altar-based sacrifice
interpretive_questions: None - clearly procedural instructions for temple-based offerings
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Unleavened Bread > Firstfruits and Omer
notes: Grain and drink offerings accompanying the omer wave sheaf offering.

verse_text:
And ye shall eat neither bread, nor parched corn, nor green ears, until the selfsame day that ye have brought an offering unto your God:
it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
law_summary: Prohibition against eating bread, parched grain, or fresh grain until bringing an offering to God on the appointed day
forever_phrase: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations
generational_phrase: throughout your generations in all your dwellings
requires_specific_role: bringing an offering unto your God
classification_reasoning: Explicit perpetual language but requires temple offering system which is not currently operational
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:14 - 'it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'bringing an offering' can be fulfilled without temple system
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Unleavened Bread > Firstfruits and Omer
notes: Prohibition on eating new grain until the omer offering is brought; a perpetual statute.

verse_text: And ye shall count unto you from the morrow after the sabbath, from the day that ye brought the sheaf of the wave offering; seven sabbaths shall be complete:
law_summary: Count seven complete weeks from the day after the sabbath when the wave offering sheaf is brought
requires_specific_role: bringing the sheaf of wave offering
classification_reasoning: No explicit duration stated but part of broader perpetual feast context; requires wave offering system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:15 - counting depends on 'day that ye brought the sheaf of the wave offering'
interpretive_questions: Whether counting can begin without actual wave offering being brought
notes: The counting of the omer, leading to Shavuot.

verse_text: Even unto the morrow after the seventh sabbath shall ye number fifty days; and ye shall offer a new meat offering unto the LORD.
law_summary: Count fifty days after the seventh sabbath and offer a new grain offering to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Part of feast sequence with implicit ongoing nature but requires temple offering system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:16 - 'ye shall offer a new meat offering unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether the counting aspect can be observed separately from the offering requirement
notes: Count fifty days and bring a new grain offering.

verse_text:
Ye shall bring out of your habitations two wave loaves of two tenth deals: they shall be of fine flour; they shall be baken with leaven;
they are the firstfruits unto the LORD.
law_summary: Bring two leavened wave loaves made of fine flour as firstfruits offering to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Part of perpetual feast system but requires functioning temple and priesthood for wave offering
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:17 - 'they are the firstfruits unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether firstfruits principle can be observed without formal temple wave offering
notes: The two leavened wave loaves as firstfruits for Shavuot.

verse_text:
And ye shall offer with the bread seven lambs without blemish of the first year, and one young bullock, and two rams: they shall be
for
 a burnt offering unto the LORD, with their meat offering, and their drink offerings,
even an offering made by fire, of sweet savour unto the LORD.
law_summary: Offer seven unblemished yearling lambs, one young bull, and two rams as burnt offerings with grain and drink offerings during the feast
classification_reasoning: Sacrificial requirements that are part of perpetual feast system but require temple and priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:18 - burnt offering with grain and drink offerings
interpretive_questions: None - clearly requires temple sacrificial system
notes: Burnt offerings for Shavuot: seven lambs, one bull, two rams.

verse_text: Then ye shall sacrifice one kid of the goats for a sin offering, and two lambs of the first year for a sacrifice of peace offerings.
law_summary: Sacrifice one male goat as a sin offering and two yearling lambs as peace offerings
classification_reasoning: Sacrificial law within perpetual feast context but requires temple system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:19 - 'sacrifice one kid of the goats for a sin offering'
interpretive_questions: None - explicitly requires sacrifice system
notes: Sin offering and peace offerings for Shavuot.

verse_text:
And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the first fruits
for a wave offering before the LORD, with the two lambs: they shall be holy to the LORD for the priest.
law_summary: The priest must wave the firstfruits bread and two lambs as a wave offering before the LORD, and these offerings become holy to the LORD for the priest
classification_reasoning: Priestly procedure within perpetual feast system requiring temple and functioning priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:20 - 'the priest shall wave them...for a wave offering before the LORD'
interpretive_questions: None - explicitly requires priest and temple context
notes: The priest waves the firstfruits bread with the lambs; these become holy to the LORD for the priest.

verse_text:
And ye shall proclaim on the selfsame day,
that it may be an holy convocation unto you: ye shall do no servile work
therein: it shall be a statute for ever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
law_summary: Proclaim a holy convocation and do no servile work on this day, as a perpetual statute
forever_phrase: it shall be a statute for ever
classification_reasoning: Explicit perpetual language for holy convocation and sabbath rest, requires no temple infrastructure
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:21 - 'it shall be a statute for ever in all your dwellings throughout your generations'
interpretive_questions: Which specific day this refers to in the feast sequence
notes: Holy convocation and rest from work on Shavuot; perpetual statute.

verse_text:
Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, In the seventh month, in the first
day of the month, shall ye have a sabbath, a memorial of blowing of trumpets, an holy convocation.
law_summary: Observe the first day of the seventh month as a sabbath with trumpet blowing and holy convocation
classification_reasoning: Calendar observance requiring no temple infrastructure, part of appointed times system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:24 - sabbath observance with trumpet memorial and holy convocation
interpretive_questions: Whether trumpet blowing requires specific priestly role or can be done by community
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Yom Teruah > Sacred Assembly
LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Yom Teruah > The Teruah
notes: Establishes Yom Teruah: sabbath rest, memorial of blowing, holy convocation.

verse_text:
Ye shall do no servile work
therein: but ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
law_summary: Prohibition against servile work and requirement to offer fire offerings on a holy day
classification_reasoning: Part of holy day observance requiring fire offerings which necessitate temple and priesthood
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:25 - 'ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether the prohibition on servile work applies independently of the fire offering requirement
notes: No servile work and fire offerings on Yom Teruah.

verse_text:
For whatsoever soul
it be that shall not be afflicted in that same day, he shall be cut off from among his people.
law_summary: Anyone who does not afflict their soul on the Day of Atonement shall be cut off from the people
classification_reasoning: Soul affliction requirement with divine punishment does not require temple infrastructure
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:29 - 'whatsoever soul it be that shall not be afflicted... shall be cut off from among his people'
interpretive_questions: Specific definition of 'afflict your souls' and mechanism of divine cutting off
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Yom Kippur > Affliction and Fasting
notes: Penalty of karet for not afflicting one's soul on Yom Kippur.

verse_text:
And whatsoever soul
it be that doeth any work in that same day, the same soul will I destroy from among his people.
law_summary: Anyone who does work on the Day of Atonement will be cut off from their people
classification_reasoning: Work prohibition with divine punishment requires no temple infrastructure, reinforced by perpetual language in surrounding context
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:30 - 'whatsoever soul it be that doeth any work... the same soul will I destroy'
interpretive_questions: Distinction between divine cutting off versus human cutting off from the people
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Yom Kippur > Sacred Assembly
notes: Penalty for doing work on Yom Kippur: destruction from among the people.

verse_text:
On the first day
shall be an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work
therein.
law_summary: On the first day of the feast, hold a holy convocation and do no servile work
classification_reasoning: Holy convocation and work prohibition require no temple infrastructure
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:35 - 'first day shall be an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work'
interpretive_questions: Distinction between 'servile work' versus 'any work' in other contexts
notes: Holy convocation and rest from work on the first day of Sukkot.

verse_text:
Seven days ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD: on the eighth day shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the LORD: it
is a solemn assembly;
and ye shall do no servile work
therein.
law_summary: During the seven-day festival, offer fire offerings to the LORD daily, hold a holy convocation on the eighth day with another fire offering, and do no regular work on that day
classification_reasoning: Mixed requirements: fire offerings need temple/priesthood, but holy convocation and work prohibition do not
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:36 - 'offer an offering made by fire... holy convocation... do no servile work'
interpretive_questions: Whether the non-sacrificial elements can be observed independently of the fire offering requirements
notes: Covers both the seven days of Sukkot offerings and the eighth day (Shemini Atzeret) as a solemn assembly.

verse_text: And ye shall take you on the first day the boughs of goodly trees, branches of palm trees, and the boughs of thick trees, and willows of the brook; and ye shall rejoice before the LORD your God seven days.
law_summary: Take branches of specific trees on the first day and rejoice before the LORD for seven days
classification_reasoning: Taking branches and rejoicing, no temple or land prerequisite stated
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:40 - 'ye shall take...and ye shall rejoice before the LORD'
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'before the LORD' without temple presence
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Sukkot > The Four Species
notes: Taking branches of four types of trees and rejoicing for seven days.

verse_text: Ye shall dwell in booths seven days; all that are Israelites born shall dwell in booths:
law_summary: All native-born Israelites must dwell in booths for seven days during the feast
addressee_in_text: Israelites born ('all that are Israelites born')
classification_reasoning: Part of feast context, dwelling in booths requires no temple or land
textual_evidence: Leviticus 23:42 - 'all that are Israelites born shall dwell in booths'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to native-born or extends to grafted-in believers
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Sukkot > Dwelling in Booths

verse_text: And thou shalt take fine flour, and bake twelve cakes thereof: two tenth deals shall be in one cake.
law_summary: Take fine flour and bake twelve cakes, with two tenth deals in each cake
classification_reasoning: Showbread preparation requires sanctuary context
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:5 - 'take fine flour, and bake twelve cakes'
interpretive_questions: Whether this is part of perpetual showbread statute in following verses
notes: Instructions for preparing the showbread: twelve cakes of fine flour.

verse_text: And thou shalt set them in two rows, six on a row, upon the pure table before the LORD.
law_summary: Set the showbread in two rows of six loaves each on the pure table before the LORD
classification_reasoning: Showbread arrangement requires pure table in sanctuary
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:6 - 'set them...upon the pure table before the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether this receives perpetual status from subsequent verses about showbread
notes: Arrangement of the showbread on the pure table.

verse_text:
And thou shalt put pure frankincense upon
each row, that it may be on the bread for a memorial,
even an offering made by fire unto the LORD.
law_summary: Place pure frankincense on each row of showbread as a memorial offering to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Specific procedure for showbread preparation with no explicit perpetual language, but part of larger showbread system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:7 - specific instruction for frankincense placement
interpretive_questions: Duration tied to the perpetual covenant mentioned in verse 8
notes: Frankincense placed on the showbread rows as a memorial offering.

verse_text:
And it shall be Aaron’s and his sons’; and they shall eat it in the holy place: for it
is most holy unto him of the offerings of the LORD made by fire by a perpetual statute.
law_summary: Aaron and his sons must eat the showbread in the holy place as it is most holy
classification_reasoning: Explicitly called 'perpetual statute' making it eternal, but requires holy place and showbread system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:9 - 'by a perpetual statute'
interpretive_questions: None - clearly perpetual but awaiting temple and priesthood
notes: The showbread belongs to Aaron and his sons and must be eaten in the holy place.

verse_text:
Bring forth him that hath cursed without the camp; and let all that heard
him lay their hands upon his head, and let all the congregation stone him.
law_summary: The blasphemer must be taken outside the camp, witnesses must lay hands on his head, and the congregation must stone him to death
classification_reasoning: Specific procedure for blasphemy case with no perpetual language, but follows from preceding eternal laws
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:14 - procedural command for specific case
interpretive_questions: Whether this procedure applies beyond the immediate case or establishes ongoing protocol
notes: Procedure for executing a blasphemer: taken outside the camp, witnesses lay hands on head, congregation stones.

verse_text: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, Whosoever curseth his God shall bear his sin.
law_summary: Anyone who curses God shall bear the consequences of their sin
classification_reasoning: General moral principle addressed to children of Israel with no temporal limitations
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:15 - universal principle about bearing sin for cursing God
interpretive_questions: Relationship between bearing sin and specific penalties outlined in following verses
notes: Anyone who curses God bears their sin.

verse_text:
And he that blasphemeth the name of the LORD, he shall surely be put to death,
and all the congregation shall certainly stone him: as well the stranger, as he that is born in the land, when he blasphemeth the name
of the LORD, shall be put to death.
law_summary: Anyone who blasphemes the name of the LORD must be put to death by stoning
classification_reasoning: Universal law applying to all people but requiring congregational authority for execution
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:16 - applies to 'stranger' and 'born in the land'
interpretive_questions: Authority structure needed for congregational stoning in diaspora
notes: Death penalty by stoning for blaspheming the name of the LORD; applies equally to native-born and stranger.

verse_text: And he that killeth a beast shall make it good; beast for beast.
law_summary: One who kills another person's animal must compensate with an equivalent animal
classification_reasoning: Civil restitution law with no temporal or geographical limitations
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:18 - straightforward restitution principle
interpretive_questions: Modern equivalent values for different types of animals
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > NO_STEALING > Property Damage and Liability
notes: Restitution for killing another's animal: beast for beast.

verse_text: And he that killeth a beast, he shall restore it: and he that killeth a man, he shall be put to death.
law_summary: One who kills an animal must restore it; one who kills a person must be put to death
classification_reasoning: Basic justice principle stated as ongoing law but requires judicial system to implement
textual_evidence: Leviticus 24:21 states principle within broader legal context
interpretive_questions: How does this apply without Israelite judicial authority?
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > NO_STEALING > Property Damage and Liability
notes: Two principles: restitution for killing an animal, and death penalty for killing a person.

verse_text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land which I give you, then shall the land keep a sabbath unto the LORD.
law_summary: The land must observe a sabbath rest unto the LORD when Israel enters the promised land
classification_reasoning: Explicitly conditional on 'when ye come into the land which I give you'
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:2 - 'When ye come into the land which I give you' sets clear condition
interpretive_questions: None - clearly conditional on being in the promised land
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Sabbatical Year > Land Rest

verse_text:
That which groweth of its own accord of thy harvest thou shalt not reap, neither gather the grapes of thy vine undressed:
for it is a year of rest unto the land.
law_summary: Do not reap volunteer crops or gather grapes from unpruned vines during the sabbatical year
classification_reasoning: Specific prohibition within sabbatical year laws requiring land
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:5 specifies sabbatical year restrictions on 'thy harvest' and 'thy vine'
interpretive_questions: None - requires owning agricultural land
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Sabbatical Year > What Grows Freely
notes: Prohibition on reaping volunteer crops or gathering grapes from unpruned vines during the sabbatical year.

verse_text: And the sabbath of the land shall be meat for you; for thee, and for thy servant, and for thy maid, and for thy hired servant, and for thy stranger that sojourneth with thee,
law_summary: During the sabbatical year, the natural produce of the land may be eaten by all inhabitants including servants and strangers
classification_reasoning: Provision for sabbatical year requiring land and agricultural context
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:6 - 'sabbath of the land' requires being in the land per context
interpretive_questions: None - clearly requires agricultural land context
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Sabbatical Year > What Grows Freely
notes: What grows naturally during the sabbatical year is available to all: owners, servants, hired workers, and sojourners.

verse_text:
And for thy cattle, and for the beast that
are in thy land, shall all the increase thereof be meat.
law_summary: During the sabbatical year, all produce that grows naturally shall serve as food for livestock and wild animals in the land
classification_reasoning: Extension of sabbatical year provision requiring land and livestock
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:7 - 'thy cattle' and 'in thy land' requires land possession
interpretive_questions: None - requires owning land and livestock
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Sabbatical Year > What Grows Freely
notes: Sabbatical year produce also serves as food for cattle and wild animals.

verse_text: And thou shalt number seven sabbaths of years unto thee, seven times seven years; and the space of the seven sabbaths of years shall be unto thee forty and nine years.
law_summary: Count seven cycles of seven years, totaling forty-nine years, in preparation for the jubilee year
classification_reasoning: Preparation for jubilee year system requiring land-based agricultural cycle
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:8 begins jubilee laws which require land possession per verse 2 context
interpretive_questions: None - clearly requires being established in the land for multi-generational counting
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Jubilee > Counting and Proclamation
notes: Count seven cycles of seven years (49 years) in preparation for the jubilee.

verse_text:
Then shalt thou cause the trumpet of the jubile to sound on the tenth
day of the seventh month, in the day of atonement shall ye make the trumpet sound throughout all your land.
law_summary: Sound the jubilee trumpet on the tenth day of the seventh month (Day of Atonement) throughout all the land
classification_reasoning: Command to sound trumpet throughout 'all your land' requires territorial control of the land of Israel and ability to coordinate nationwide proclamation
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:9 - 'throughout all your land' indicates territorial requirement
interpretive_questions: Who specifically sounds the trumpet - priests, officials, or others? What constitutes 'all your land'?
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Jubilee > Counting and Proclamation
notes: Sound the jubilee trumpet on the Day of Atonement throughout the land.

verse_text:
And ye shall hallow the fiftieth year, and proclaim liberty throughout
all the land unto all the inhabitants thereof: it shall be a jubile unto you; and ye shall return every man unto his possession, and ye shall return every man unto his family.
law_summary: Hallow the fiftieth year as jubilee, proclaim liberty throughout the land, and return every person to their ancestral possession and family
classification_reasoning: Returning to ancestral possessions requires established tribal land allocations and functioning land tenure system in Israel
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:10 - 'return every man unto his possession' presupposes established ancestral land holdings
interpretive_questions: How are ancestral possessions determined after centuries of diaspora? Does this apply to converts to Judaism?
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Jubilee > Counting and Proclamation
LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Jubilee > Liberty for Servants
notes: Comprehensive jubilee law: hallow the fiftieth year, proclaim liberty, return property and persons to ancestral holdings.

verse_text:
A jubile shall that fiftieth year be unto you: ye shall not sow, neither reap that which groweth of itself in it, nor gather
the grapes
 in it of thy vine undressed.
law_summary: During the jubilee year (every 50th year), do not sow crops, reap volunteer growth, or harvest grapes from unpruned vines
classification_reasoning: Agricultural sabbath requires coordinated observance across the land of Israel as part of the jubilee system
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:11 - part of jubilee cycle which requires territorial control for proper implementation
interpretive_questions: Does this apply to all agricultural land or only inherited tribal portions? What about modern agricultural methods?
notes: Agricultural restrictions during the jubilee year parallel to the sabbatical year.

verse_text:
For it
is the jubile; it shall be holy unto you: ye shall eat the increase thereof out of the field.
law_summary: During the jubilee year, you shall eat only what the field produces naturally without cultivation
classification_reasoning: Connected to jubilee agricultural sabbath, requires land-based agricultural system in Israel
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:12 - 'eat the increase thereof out of the field' presupposes agricultural land in Israel
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'natural increase' in modern agricultural contexts? Does this include all food sources?
notes: During jubilee, eat only what the field produces naturally.

verse_text:
And if thou sell
ought unto thy neighbour, or buyest ought of thy neighbour’s hand, ye shall not oppress one another:
law_summary: Do not oppress one another in buying and selling transactions
classification_reasoning: General moral principle against oppression in commerce applies universally without geographic or institutional requirements
textual_evidence: Leviticus 25:14 - prohibition on oppression in buying/selling is universally applicable moral law
interpretive_questions: What specific actions constitute 'oppression' in modern commercial contexts?
notes: Prohibition against oppression in commercial transactions; placed in the context of jubilee land sales.

verse_text:
According to the number of years after the jubile thou shalt buy of thy neighbour,
and according unto the number of years of the fruits he shall sell unto thee:
