verse_text: All the days of his separation shall he eat nothing that is made of the vine tree, from the kernels even to the husk.
law_summary: A Nazirite must not eat anything from the grapevine, including grapes and raisins, during their vow period
classification_reasoning: Specific dietary restriction applying only during active Nazirite separation period
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:4 - 'All the days of his separation shall he eat nothing that is made of the vine tree'
interpretive_questions: Temporal limitation explicit to separation period only
notes: Extension of the grape prohibition to include anything from the vine, from seeds to skin

verse_text:
All the days of the vow of his separation there shall no razor come upon his head: until the days be fulfilled, in the which he separateth
himself
 unto the LORD, he shall be holy,
and shall let the locks of the hair of his head grow.
law_summary: During a Nazirite vow, no razor may touch the head and hair must be allowed to grow
classification_reasoning: Grooming prohibition with explicit temporal limitation to vow duration
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:5 - 'All the days of the vow of his separation there shall no razor come upon his head: until the days be fulfilled'
interpretive_questions: Clear temporal boundary set by completion of vow period
notes: No razor may touch the Nazirite's head; hair must grow freely during the vow

verse_text:
All the days that he separateth
himself unto the LORD he shall come at no dead body.
law_summary: A Nazirite must not come near any dead body during the period of their vow
classification_reasoning: Purity restriction explicitly limited to separation period only
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:6 - 'All the days that he separateth himself unto the LORD he shall come at no dead body'
interpretive_questions: Clear temporal limitation to active separation period
notes: Prohibition on contact with dead bodies during the Nazirite vow

verse_text:
He shall not make himself unclean for his father, or for his mother, for his brother, or for his sister, when they die: because the consecration of his God
is upon his head.
law_summary: A Nazirite must not defile himself by contact with dead bodies, even of close family members
classification_reasoning: Enhanced purity standard for Nazirites during vow period, stricter than normal family obligations
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:7 - 'He shall not make himself unclean for his father, or for his mother... because the consecration of his God is upon his head'
interpretive_questions: Overrides normal family purity obligations during active consecration
notes: Even close family members' deaths do not permit defilement; parallels the restriction on the High Priest

verse_text:
All the days of his separation he
is holy unto the LORD.
law_summary: A Nazirite must remain holy to the LORD throughout the entire duration of their vow of separation
classification_reasoning: General holiness obligation during separation period
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:8 - 'All the days of his separation he is holy unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Holiness status limited to duration of separation period
notes: Statement of the Nazirite's holy status throughout the vow period

verse_text: And if any man die very suddenly by him, and he hath defiled the head of his consecration; then he shall shave his head in the day of his cleansing, on the seventh day shall he shave it.
law_summary: A Nazarite who becomes defiled by sudden death nearby must shave his head on the seventh day of cleansing
classification_reasoning: Conditional purification procedure applying only if defilement occurs
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:9 - 'And if any man die very suddenly by him, and he hath defiled the head of his consecration; then he shall shave his head'
interpretive_questions: Procedural remedy for accidental defilement during active vow
notes: Purification procedure for a Nazirite defiled by sudden death: shave head on the seventh day

verse_text: And on the eighth day he shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons, to the priest, to the door of the tabernacle of the congregation:
law_summary: A Nazirite who becomes defiled must bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the tabernacle door on the eighth day
classification_reasoning: Sacrificial requirement dependent on functioning tabernacle/temple and priesthood
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:10 - 'he shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons, to the priest, to the door of the tabernacle'
interpretive_questions: Requires functioning sanctuary system currently not available
notes: Offering requirements on the eighth day after defilement: two turtledoves or two young pigeons

verse_text: And the priest shall offer the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering, and make an atonement for him, for that he sinned by the dead, and shall hallow his head that same day.
law_summary: The priest must offer a sin offering and burnt offering for a Nazirite who became defiled by contact with the dead, and consecrate his head on the same day
classification_reasoning: Priestly procedure requiring functioning sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:11 - 'And the priest shall offer the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering'
interpretive_questions: Priestly function dependent on restoration of temple service
notes: The priest offers one bird as a sin offering and one as a burnt offering to atone for the Nazirite's defilement

verse_text: And he shall consecrate unto the LORD the days of his separation, and shall bring a lamb of the first year for a trespass offering: but the days that were before shall be lost, because his separation was defiled.
law_summary: A defiled Nazirite must bring a year-old lamb as a trespass offering and restart their vow period
classification_reasoning: Part of ongoing Nazirite law but requires temple/priesthood for trespass offering
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:12 - 'shall bring a lamb of the first year for a trespass offering'
interpretive_questions: Whether Nazirite vows can be taken without temple for completion
notes: A defiled Nazirite must restart their vow and bring a lamb as a trespass offering; prior days are forfeited

verse_text:
And this
is the law of the Nazarite, when the days of his separation are fulfilled: he shall be brought unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation:
law_summary: A Nazarite must be brought to the entrance of the tabernacle when their vow period ends
classification_reasoning: Part of ongoing Nazirite completion procedure requiring tabernacle/temple
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:13 - 'he shall be brought unto the door of the tabernacle'
interpretive_questions: Whether alternative completion procedures exist without temple
notes: Completion procedure: the Nazirite must be brought to the tabernacle entrance

verse_text: And he shall offer his offering unto the LORD, one he lamb of the first year without blemish for a burnt offering, and one ewe lamb of the first year without blemish for a sin offering, and one ram without blemish for peace offerings,
law_summary: A Nazirite must offer specific sacrifices when completing their vow: a male lamb for burnt offering, a female lamb for sin offering, and a ram for peace offerings, all without blemish
classification_reasoning: Ongoing law for Nazirite completion requiring sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:14 - specific sacrifices 'unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether intent to offer suffices when temple unavailable
notes: Three required offerings at vow completion: male lamb (burnt offering), female lamb (sin offering), and ram (peace offering)

verse_text:
And the priest shall bring
them before the LORD, and shall offer his sin offering, and his burnt offering:
law_summary: The priest must bring the Nazirite's offerings before the LORD and offer the sin offering and burnt offering
classification_reasoning: Priestly duty as part of ongoing Nazirite law requiring temple function
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:16 - 'priest shall bring them before the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Status of priestly duties without functioning temple
notes: Priestly duty to present and offer the Nazirite's sin and burnt offerings

verse_text:
And he shall offer the ram
for a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the LORD, with the basket of unleavened bread: the priest shall offer also his meat offering, and his drink offering.
law_summary: The priest must offer a ram as a peace offering along with unleavened bread, meat offering, and drink offering
classification_reasoning: Priestly procedure for peace offerings requiring temple/altar
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:17 - 'he shall offer the ram for a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether peace offerings can be offered outside temple system
notes: The peace offering with unleavened bread, grain offering, and drink offering

verse_text:
And the Nazarite shall shave the head of his separation
at
 the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall take the hair of the head of his separation, and put
it in the fire which
is under the sacrifice of the peace offerings.
law_summary: A Nazarite must shave their head at the tabernacle entrance and burn the hair in the fire under the peace offering sacrifice
classification_reasoning: Nazirite completion ritual requiring tabernacle location and sacrificial fire
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:18 - 'at the door of the tabernacle' and 'fire which is under the sacrifice'
interpretive_questions: Whether hair burning ritual can be modified without temple
notes: Shaving the head at the tabernacle entrance and burning the hair under the peace offering

verse_text:
And the priest shall take the sodden shoulder of the ram, and one unleavened cake out of the basket, and one unleavened wafer, and shall put
them upon the hands of the Nazarite, after
the hair of
 his separation is shaven:
law_summary: The priest must take specific offerings (sodden shoulder of ram, unleavened cake, and unleavened wafer) and place them in the hands of the Nazarite after their hair is shaven
classification_reasoning: Priestly procedure in Nazirite completion requiring sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:19 - priest takes offerings from sacrifice and places in Nazirite's hands
interpretive_questions: Whether symbolic performance possible without actual sacrifices
notes: Priestly ritual of placing the boiled shoulder, unleavened cake, and wafer in the Nazirite's hands

verse_text:
And the priest shall wave them
for a wave offering before the LORD: this
is holy for the priest, with the wave breast and heave shoulder: and after that the Nazarite may drink wine.
law_summary: The priest must perform a wave offering with the Nazirite's offerings, after which the Nazirite may drink wine
classification_reasoning: Priestly wave offering procedure requiring temple system and priestly portion allocation
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:20 - 'priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the LORD: this is holy for the priest'
interpretive_questions: Status of wave offerings and priestly portions without temple
notes: The wave offering concludes the Nazirite vow; afterward the Nazirite may drink wine

verse_text:
This
is the law of the Nazarite who hath vowed,
and of
 his offering unto the LORD for his separation, beside that
that
 his hand shall get: according to the vow which he vowed, so he must do after the law of his separation.
law_summary: A Nazarite must fulfill their vow of separation according to the prescribed law, including required offerings, plus any additional voluntary offerings they can afford
classification_reasoning: Summary law of Nazirite obligations requiring temple system for completion
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:21 - 'according to the vow which he vowed, so he must do after the law of his separation'
interpretive_questions: Whether partial Nazirite observance valid without completion ceremony
notes: Summary statement: the Nazirite must fulfill all prescribed obligations plus any additional voluntary offerings

verse_text: Speak unto Aaron and unto his sons, saying, On this wise ye shall bless the children of Israel, saying unto them,
law_summary: Aaron and his sons (the priests) are commanded to bless the children of Israel in a specific manner
classification_reasoning: Priestly duty to bless Israel, ongoing but requires legitimate priesthood
textual_evidence: Numbers 6:23 - 'ye shall bless the children of Israel'
interpretive_questions: Whether Aaronic priesthood authority continues without temple service
notes: The priestly blessing (birkat kohanim); an ongoing institutional command to the priestly class to bless Israel

verse_text:
But unto the sons of Kohath he gave none: because the service of the sanctuary belonging unto them
was that they should bear upon their shoulders.
law_summary: The sons of Kohath must carry the sanctuary items on their shoulders rather than using carts
classification_reasoning: Defines how Kohathites must transport sacred items - ongoing statute for whenever sanctuary items need transport
textual_evidence: Numbers 7:9 - states permanent method of transport for sanctuary service
interpretive_questions: Application when temple/tabernacle is relocated or reconstructed
notes: Establishes the ongoing principle that Kohathites must carry sanctuary items on their shoulders, not by cart

verse_text: Take the Levites from among the children of Israel, and cleanse them.
law_summary: Take the Levites from among the Israelites and purify them for service
classification_reasoning: Command to take and purify the Levites for initial consecration - appears to be one-time establishment
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:6 - context suggests initial selection and purification of Levites for service
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to initial Levitical consecration or to each generation of Levites entering service
notes: While the initial consecration is a one-time event, this establishes the institutional requirement for Levitical purification and consecration

verse_text:
And thus shalt thou do unto them, to cleanse them: Sprinkle water of purifying upon them, and let them shave all their flesh, and let them wash their clothes, and
so make themselves clean.
law_summary: Instructions for cleansing the Levites through sprinkling purifying water, shaving their bodies, and washing their clothes
classification_reasoning: Procedural instructions for Levitical purification - establishes method for whenever Levites need cleansing for service
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:7 - detailed procedure suggests standard method rather than one-time event
interpretive_questions: Frequency of this purification and whether required for each service period or only initial consecration
notes: Prescribed purification ritual for Levites: sprinkling, shaving, and washing

verse_text:
Then let them take a young bullock with his meat offering,
even
 fine flour mingled with oil, and another young bullock shalt thou take for a sin offering.
law_summary: Take a young bull with a grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil, and another young bull for a sin offering
classification_reasoning: Specific sacrificial requirements for the Levitical consecration ceremony - appears tied to initial consecration event
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:8 - specific sacrificial instructions within context of Levitical consecration ceremony
interpretive_questions: Whether these same offerings required for future Levitical consecrations or unique to initial ceremony
notes: Offerings required for Levitical consecration: a bull with grain offering and a bull for sin offering

verse_text: And thou shalt bring the Levites before the LORD: and the children of Israel shall put their hands upon the Levites:
law_summary: The children of Israel must lay their hands upon the Levites when they are brought before the LORD
classification_reasoning: Laying hands on Levites establishes representative principle - likely required procedure for Levitical consecration
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:10 - procedural requirement for Israel to lay hands on Levites suggests ongoing pattern
interpretive_questions: Whether all Israel must physically participate or representatives can act, and application to future Levitical consecrations
notes: The children of Israel lay hands on the Levites as part of the consecration, symbolizing their representation

verse_text:
And the Levites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bullocks: and thou shalt offer the one
for a sin offering, and the other
for a burnt offering, unto the LORD, to make an atonement for the Levites.
law_summary: The Levites must lay their hands on the heads of the sacrificial bulls, and one bull shall be offered as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to make atonement for the Levites
classification_reasoning: Requires sacrificial system and temple for atonement procedure
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:12 - Levites lay hands on bulls for sin and burnt offerings
interpretive_questions: Whether this was a one-time consecration or ongoing requirement
notes: Levites lay hands on the sacrificial bulls; one is a sin offering and one a burnt offering for atonement

verse_text: Thus shalt thou separate the Levites from among the children of Israel: and the Levites shall be mine.
law_summary: The Levites must be separated from the other Israelites and consecrated to God's service
classification_reasoning: Divine declaration of Levite separation and ownership by God has no stated temporal limit
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:14 - 'the Levites shall be mine' - divine claim of ownership
interpretive_questions: How this separation manifests without temple service
notes: Reinforces the permanent separation of Levites from the rest of Israel for divine service

verse_text:
And after that shall the Levites go in to do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation: and thou shalt cleanse them, and offer them
for an offering.
law_summary: The Levites must be cleansed and offered as an offering before they can perform tabernacle service
classification_reasoning: Specifically requires tabernacle service as prerequisite for law's operation
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:15 - cleansing required before tabernacle service begins
interpretive_questions: Whether cleansing procedures apply to future temple service
notes: Levites must be cleansed and presented as an offering before entering tabernacle service

verse_text:
This
is it that
belongeth unto the Levites: from twenty and five years old and upward they shall go in to wait upon the service of the tabernacle of the congregation:
law_summary: Levites must begin service in the tabernacle at age twenty-five years and older
classification_reasoning: Age requirement for tabernacle service has no temporal limit but requires functioning tabernacle
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:24 - 'from twenty and five years old and upward they shall go in'
interpretive_questions: Whether age requirements apply to all future temple service
notes: Sets the starting age for Levitical service at 25; note tension with Numbers 4:3 which says 30. Traditionally understood as 25-30 being an apprenticeship period

verse_text:
And from the age of fifty years they shall cease waiting upon the service
thereof, and shall serve no more:
law_summary: Levites must retire from active service at age fifty
classification_reasoning: Mandatory retirement age for temple service has no stated temporal limit but requires functioning temple
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:25 - 'from the age of fifty years they shall cease waiting upon the service'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all forms of Levitical service or only active tabernacle work
notes: Mandatory retirement from active Levitical service at age 50

verse_text: But shall minister with their brethren in the tabernacle of the congregation, to keep the charge, and shall do no service. Thus shalt thou do unto the Levites touching their charge.
law_summary: Levites past working age may assist their brethren in keeping charge of the tabernacle but shall perform no active service
classification_reasoning: Post-retirement service rules have no temporal limit but require functioning tabernacle context
textual_evidence: Numbers 8:26 - 'minister with their brethren...to keep the charge'
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'keeping charge' versus active service in temple context
notes: Retired Levites may assist their brethren in guard duty but not perform active service

verse_text:
Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If any man of you or of your posterity shall be unclean by reason of a dead body, or
be in a journey afar off, yet he shall keep the passover unto the LORD.
law_summary: Men who are ritually unclean from contact with a dead body or on a distant journey must still observe Passover
classification_reasoning: Generational language indicates ongoing applicability, but refers to sacrificial Passover requiring temple
textual_evidence: Numbers 9:10 - 'you or of your posterity' extends to future generations
interpretive_questions: How do uncleanness laws apply without temple system?
notes: Establishes the second Passover provision for those unclean or on a distant journey

verse_text:
The fourteenth day of the second month at even they shall keep it,
and eat it with unleavened bread and bitter
herbs.
law_summary: Those who missed Passover due to uncleanness or travel must observe it on the fourteenth day of the second month with unleavened bread and bitter herbs
classification_reasoning: Second Passover provision for those who missed first, requires sacrificial lamb thus temple system
textual_evidence: Numbers 9:11 - specific timing and elements for makeup Passover
interpretive_questions: Can second Passover be observed without sacrifice?
notes: The second Passover date (14th of second month) and manner of observance (unleavened bread and bitter herbs)

verse_text: They shall leave none of it unto the morning, nor break any bone of it: according to all the ordinances of the passover they shall keep it.
law_summary: When observing the second Passover, leave no meat until morning and do not break any bones of the sacrificial lamb
classification_reasoning: Regulations about sacrificial lamb require temple system, but principle remains as ongoing ordinance
textual_evidence: Numbers 9:12 - 'according to all the ordinances of the passover' references broader system
interpretive_questions: Do bone-breaking prohibitions apply to non-sacrificial Passover meals?
notes: Same rules apply to the second Passover as the first: no leftovers until morning, no broken bones

verse_text:
But the man that
is clean, and is not in a journey, and forbeareth to keep the passover, even the same soul shall be cut off from among his people: because he brought not the offering of the LORD in his appointed season, that man shall bear his sin.
law_summary: A ritually clean person not on a journey who fails to keep the Passover shall be cut off from the people for not bringing the LORD's offering at its appointed time
classification_reasoning: Penalty for failing to bring LORD's offering at appointed time requires sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 9:13 - 'brought not the offering of the LORD' specifically refers to temple sacrifice
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'keeping Passover' without ability to bring offering?
notes: Penalty of being cut off for a clean, non-traveling person who neglects the Passover offering; relates to the general Passover obligation rather than specifically to the second Passover

verse_text: Make thee two trumpets of silver; of a whole piece shalt thou make them: that thou mayest use them for the calling of the assembly, and for the journeying of the camps.
law_summary: Make two silver trumpets from hammered work for calling assemblies and signaling camp movements
classification_reasoning: Specific command to Moses for making trumpets, lacks perpetual language but related to perpetual priestly function in v.8
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:2 - direct command to Moses for specific construction
interpretive_questions: Whether this is one-time construction or ongoing pattern for trumpet making
notes: While the initial making is a one-time event, the silver trumpets become a permanent institution for assembly and camp movements (see v.8: perpetual ordinance)

verse_text: And when they shall blow with them, all the assembly shall assemble themselves to thee at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
law_summary: When the silver trumpets are blown, all the assembly must gather at the entrance of the tabernacle
classification_reasoning: Procedural law contingent on tabernacle and trumpet system, gains perpetual status through v.8
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:3 - assembly gathering at tabernacle door when trumpets blown
interpretive_questions: Application with temple instead of tabernacle
notes: Ongoing instruction for using trumpets to gather the full assembly

verse_text:
And if they blow
but with one
trumpet, then the princes,
which are heads of the thousands of Israel, shall gather themselves unto thee.
law_summary: When one trumpet is blown, the princes who are heads of thousands of Israel shall gather to Moses
classification_reasoning: Leadership structure procedure connected to perpetual trumpet ordinance in v.8
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:4 - princes gather when one trumpet blown
interpretive_questions: Modern equivalent of 'princes who are heads of thousands'
notes: Signal protocol: one trumpet gathers the tribal princes only

verse_text: When ye blow an alarm, then the camps that lie on the east parts shall go forward.
law_summary: When an alarm is blown, the camps on the east side shall move forward first
classification_reasoning: Movement procedure tied to perpetual trumpet ordinance but requires camp organization
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:5 - east camps move forward at alarm
interpretive_questions: Application when not in organized camp formation
notes: Alarm signal for the eastern camps to advance; part of the perpetual trumpet ordinance

verse_text: When ye blow an alarm the second time, then the camps that lie on the south side shall take their journey: they shall blow an alarm for their journeys.
law_summary: When the second trumpet alarm is blown, the camps on the south side shall begin their journey
classification_reasoning: Sequential movement procedure tied to perpetual trumpet ordinance but requires camp organization
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:6 - south camps journey at second alarm
interpretive_questions: Application in settled land versus journey context
notes: Second alarm signal for the southern camps to advance

verse_text: But when the congregation is to be gathered together, ye shall blow, but ye shall not sound an alarm.
law_summary: When gathering the congregation, blow the trumpets but do not sound an alarm
classification_reasoning: Distinction between gathering and alarm signals, part of perpetual trumpet system from v.8
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:7 - blow but do not sound alarm for congregation gathering
interpretive_questions: Exact distinction between regular blow and alarm sound
notes: Distinction between the assembly call (long blast, no alarm) and the marching signal (alarm)

verse_text: And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow with the trumpets; and they shall be to you for an ordinance for ever throughout your generations.
law_summary: The sons of Aaron (priests) shall blow the trumpets as a perpetual ordinance for all generations
classification_reasoning: Explicit perpetual language making this eternal ordinance, but requires Aaronic priesthood
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:8 - 'they shall be to you for an ordinance for ever throughout your generations'
interpretive_questions: Whether trumpets are required in all contexts or specific situations
notes: Explicitly declared a perpetual ordinance for all generations; only the sons of Aaron may blow the trumpets

verse_text: And if ye go to war in your land against the enemy that oppresseth you, then ye shall blow an alarm with the trumpets; and ye shall be remembered before the LORD your God, and ye shall be saved from your enemies.
law_summary: When going to war against oppressors in your land, blow an alarm with trumpets to be remembered before the LORD and saved from enemies
classification_reasoning: Conditional law triggered by warfare in 'your land' - requires being established in the land of Israel with enemies oppressing within it
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:9 - 'if ye go to war in your land against the enemy that oppresseth you'
interpretive_questions: Does 'your land' specifically require the Promised Land or could apply to any land where Israel dwells?
notes: Trumpets to be blown in wartime as a perpetual practice for divine remembrance

verse_text:
Also in the day of your gladness, and in your solemn days, and in the beginnings of your months, ye shall blow with the trumpets over your burnt offerings, and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings; that they may be to you for a memorial before your God: I
am the LORD your God.
law_summary: Blow trumpets over burnt offerings and peace offerings during times of gladness, solemn days, and new months as a memorial before God
classification_reasoning: Ongoing obligation tied to sacrificial system - requires burnt offerings, peace offerings, and priesthood to function
textual_evidence: Numbers 10:10 - 'ye shall blow with the trumpets over your burnt offerings, and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings'
interpretive_questions: Could this apply to future temple service or only to the wilderness/first temple period?
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > Daily and Communal Offerings
notes: Trumpets over offerings on joyful occasions, appointed feasts, and new moons as a perpetual memorial

verse_text: And will make an offering by fire unto the LORD, a burnt offering, or a sacrifice in performing a vow, or in a freewill offering, or in your solemn feasts, to make a sweet savour unto the LORD, of the herd, or of the flock:
law_summary: Israelites must offer fire offerings to the LORD from cattle or sheep for burnt offerings, vow fulfillments, freewill offerings, or solemn feasts
classification_reasoning: Fire offerings are part of ongoing sacrificial system but require altar/priesthood infrastructure
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:3 - prescribed for 'burnt offering, or a sacrifice in performing a vow, or in a freewill offering, or in your solemn feasts'
interpretive_questions: Whether all offerings require centralized altar or some could be offered elsewhere
notes: General statement on the types of fire offerings from the herd or flock

verse_text:
Then shall he that offereth his offering unto the LORD bring a meat offering of a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth
part
 of an hin of oil.
law_summary: When making an offering to the LORD, bring a grain offering of one-tenth ephah of flour mixed with one-fourth hin of oil
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for grain offerings, part of sacrificial system requiring altar and priesthood
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:4 - 'Then shall he that offereth his offering unto the LORD bring a meat offering'
interpretive_questions: Specific measurements and whether modern equivalents would suffice
notes: Specifies the grain offering accompanying an animal sacrifice: 1/10 ephah flour with 1/4 hin oil

verse_text:
And the fourth
part of an hin of wine for a drink offering shalt thou prepare with the burnt offering or sacrifice, for one lamb.
law_summary: Prepare one-fourth hin of wine as a drink offering with each burnt offering or sacrifice of a lamb
classification_reasoning: Drink offering requirements are procedural elements of ongoing sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:5 - 'shalt thou prepare with the burnt offering or sacrifice'
interpretive_questions: Whether drink offerings could be made without animal sacrifice
notes: Drink offering of 1/4 hin of wine per lamb; drink offerings are not separately categorized in the schema

verse_text:
Or for a ram, thou shalt prepare
for a meat offering two tenth deals of flour mingled with the third
part of an hin of oil.
law_summary: For a ram sacrifice, prepare a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of flour mixed with one-third of a hin of oil
classification_reasoning: Specific procedural requirements for ram sacrifices, part of broader sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:6 - 'Or for a ram, thou shalt prepare for a meat offering'
interpretive_questions: Precise measurements and modern application methods
notes: Grain offering for a ram: 2/10 ephah flour with 1/3 hin oil

verse_text:
And for a drink offering thou shalt offer the third
part of an hin of wine,
for a sweet savour unto the LORD.
law_summary: Offer one-third hin of wine as a drink offering to accompany sacrifices as a sweet savor to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Drink offering requirements are part of sacrificial system designed to be 'sweet savour unto the LORD'
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:7 - 'for a sweet savour unto the LORD' indicates ongoing purpose
interpretive_questions: Relationship between drink offerings and animal sacrifices
notes: Drink offering of 1/3 hin of wine for a ram sacrifice

verse_text: Then shall he bring with a bullock a meat offering of three tenth deals of flour mingled with half an hin of oil.
law_summary: When offering a bullock, bring a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of flour mixed with half a hin of oil
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for bullock offerings, highest level of sacrifice requiring specific grain offering proportions
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:9 - 'Then shall he bring with a bullock a meat offering'
interpretive_questions: Whether proportional increases reflect greater significance of bullock sacrifices
notes: Grain offering for a bull: 3/10 ephah flour with 1/2 hin oil

verse_text: And thou shalt bring for a drink offering half an hin of wine, for an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
law_summary: Requirement to bring half a hin of wine as a drink offering accompanying fire offerings to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Drink offering for largest sacrifices, part of fire offering system designed as 'sweet savour unto the LORD'
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:10 - 'for an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Maximum amount of drink offering suggests this is for most significant sacrificial occasions
notes: Drink offering of 1/2 hin of wine for a bull sacrifice

verse_text: Thus shall it be done for one bullock, or for one ram, or for a lamb, or a kid.
law_summary: Standardized procedure for offering grain offerings with each type of animal sacrifice
classification_reasoning: Standardization command ensuring uniform procedures across all animal sacrifice types
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:11 - 'Thus shall it be done' indicates standardized, ongoing procedure
interpretive_questions: Whether 'kid' refers to young goat specifically or includes other young animals
notes: Confirms the standardized procedure applies to all animal types (bull, ram, lamb, or kid)

verse_text: According to the number that ye shall prepare, so shall ye do to every one according to their number.
law_summary: For each animal sacrificed, the corresponding grain and drink offerings must be provided in the prescribed proportions
classification_reasoning: Procedural law for sacrificial system with no explicit duration language, but part of ongoing sacrificial statutes
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:12 - procedural instruction for proportional offerings
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all sacrifice contexts or only specific offerings mentioned in this chapter
notes: Each animal sacrificed must have its corresponding grain and drink offering in proper proportion

verse_text: All that are born of the country shall do these things after this manner, in offering an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.
law_summary: All native-born Israelites must follow the prescribed manner when offering fire offerings to the LORD
addressee_in_text: all that are born of the country
classification_reasoning: Obligation for native-born Israelites regarding sacrificial procedures with no temporal limitations stated
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:13 - 'All that are born of the country shall do these things after this manner'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'country' refers specifically to the land of Israel or anywhere Israelites dwell
notes: Confirms these offering rules apply to all native-born Israelites

verse_text:
And if a stranger sojourn with you, or whosoever
be among you in your generations, and will offer an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD; as ye do, so he shall do.
law_summary: Foreigners living among Israel who wish to offer burnt offerings must follow the same procedures as Israelites
addressee_in_text: stranger sojourn with you, or whosoever be among you
classification_reasoning: Contains generational language indicating perpetual application for foreigners offering sacrifices
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:14 - 'in your generations' indicates ongoing application
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only when living among Israelites in the land or in any context
notes: Foreigners who wish to offer sacrifices must follow the same procedures as native Israelites

verse_text: Then it shall be, that, when ye eat of the bread of the land, ye shall offer up an heave offering unto the LORD.
law_summary: When eating bread from the land, must offer a heave offering to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Conditional law dependent on eating bread from the land, requiring land possession and sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:19 - 'when ye eat of the bread of the land' establishes condition
interpretive_questions: Whether 'bread of the land' refers to any bread eaten in the land or specifically bread from land-grown grain
notes: Introduction to the challah (dough) offering; a heave offering from the bread of the land

verse_text:
Ye shall offer up a cake of the first of your dough
for an heave offering: as
ye do the heave offering of the threshingfloor, so shall ye heave it.
law_summary: Must offer a cake made from the first portion of dough as a heave offering, similar to the heave offering from the threshing floor
classification_reasoning: Procedural instruction for dough offering with no temporal limitations but requiring sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:20 - specific procedure for heave offering from first dough
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all dough preparation or only in specific contexts
notes: The first dough offering (challah) must be a cake offered as a heave offering, like the threshing floor offering

verse_text: Of the first of your dough ye shall give unto the LORD an heave offering in your generations.
law_summary: Give the first portion of dough as a heave offering to the LORD throughout all generations
classification_reasoning: Contains generational language indicating perpetual obligation for dough heave offering
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:21 - 'in your generations' establishes ongoing temporal scope
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies wherever Israelites make dough or only in the land
notes: Explicitly declared as a perpetual obligation throughout all generations

verse_text:
Then it shall be, if
ought be committed by ignorance without the knowledge of the congregation, that all the congregation shall offer one young bullock for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour unto the LORD, with his meat offering, and his drink offering, according to the manner, and one kid of the goats for a sin offering.
law_summary: If a sin is committed unknowingly by the congregation, they must offer a young bull as a burnt offering with its grain and drink offerings, plus a male goat as a sin offering
classification_reasoning: Conditional procedure for congregational sin offering with no temporal limitations stated
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:24 - 'if ought be committed by ignorance' establishes conditional application
interpretive_questions: How 'ignorance without the knowledge of the congregation' is determined
notes: Community sin offering for unintentional sins: a bull for burnt offering and a goat for sin offering

verse_text: And the priest shall make an atonement for the soul that sinneth ignorantly, when he sinneth by ignorance before the LORD, to make an atonement for him; and it shall be forgiven him.
law_summary: The priest must make atonement for a person who sins unintentionally, and the person will be forgiven
classification_reasoning: Priestly duty for atonement system requiring functioning priesthood and sacrificial system
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:28 - 'the priest shall make an atonement... and it shall be forgiven him'
interpretive_questions: Authority and mechanism of priestly atonement without temple
notes: Atonement for unintentional individual sins through priestly intercession

verse_text:
But the soul that doeth
ought presumptuously,
whether he be born in the land, or a stranger, the same reproacheth the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
law_summary: Anyone who sins presumptuously shall be cut off from the people
addressee_in_text: any soul - born in land or stranger
classification_reasoning: Principle remains valid but community execution mechanism requires organized authority
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:30 - 'the soul that doeth ought presumptuously... shall be cut off from among his people'
interpretive_questions: Mechanism and authority for cutting off in diaspora
notes: Presumptuous (high-handed) sin has no offering; the person is cut off. Applies equally to native and stranger

verse_text:
Because he hath despised the word of the LORD, and hath broken his commandment, that soul shall utterly be cut off; his iniquity
shall be
 upon him.
law_summary: A person who despises the LORD's word and breaks his commandment shall be cut off from the people and bear their iniquity
classification_reasoning: Divine principle of consequence but requires community structure for implementation
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:31 - 'he hath despised the word of the LORD... that soul shall utterly be cut off'
interpretive_questions: Divine vs. community execution of cutting off
notes: Reinforces the punishment for despising God's word and breaking His commandment

verse_text: And the LORD said unto Moses, The man shall be surely put to death: all the congregation shall stone him with stones without the camp.
law_summary: A man who gathers sticks on the Sabbath shall be put to death by stoning outside the camp
classification_reasoning: Specific divine judgment for particular case of Sabbath violation, not general procedural law
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:35 - 'The man shall be surely put to death' (referring to specific stick-gatherer)
interpretive_questions: Whether this establishes precedent for all Sabbath violations
categories: LOVE_GOD > SACRED_TIMES > Weekly Sabbath > Rest and Work Prohibition
notes: While the immediate context is a specific individual (the wood-gatherer), this establishes/confirms the death penalty for Sabbath violation, which is a universal law

verse_text: Speak unto the children of Israel, and bid them that they make them fringes in the borders of their garments throughout their generations, and that they put upon the fringe of the borders a ribband of blue:
law_summary: Israelites must make fringes on the borders of their garments and attach a blue cord to each fringe
classification_reasoning: Explicit generational language with no prerequisites, directly portable commandment
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:38 - 'throughout their generations... make them fringes in the borders of their garments'
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'borders of garments' in modern clothing
notes: The commandment of tzitzit (fringes) with a blue thread; explicitly addressed to the children of Israel for all generations

verse_text: And it shall be unto you for a fringe, that ye may look upon it, and remember all the commandments of the LORD, and do them; and that ye seek not after your own heart and your own eyes, after which ye use to go a whoring:
law_summary: Wear fringes as a reminder to observe God's commandments and avoid following personal desires
classification_reasoning: Continuation of fringe commandment with explicit purpose, shares generational language from verse 38
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:39 - 'ye may look upon it, and remember all the commandments of the LORD'
interpretive_questions: None - clear purpose and application
notes: Purpose of tzitzit: to remember the commandments, obey them, and not follow one's own heart and eyes

verse_text: That ye may remember, and do all my commandments, and be holy unto your God.
law_summary: Remember and obey all of God's commandments to be holy
classification_reasoning: General obligation to remember and obey commandments and be holy, fundamental to covenant relationship
textual_evidence: Numbers 15:40 - 'That ye may remember, and do all my commandments, and be holy unto your God'
interpretive_questions: None - clear ongoing obligation
notes: Summary exhortation linked to the tzitzit: remember and do all commandments, be holy to God

verse_text: To be a memorial unto the children of Israel, that no stranger, which is not of the seed of Aaron, come near to offer incense before the LORD; that he be not as Korah, and as his company: as the LORD said to him by the hand of Moses.
law_summary: No one who is not a descendant of Aaron may offer incense before the LORD
classification_reasoning: Memorial statute establishing perpetual restriction on who may offer incense, transcending the specific incident
textual_evidence: Numbers 16:40 - 'memorial unto the children of Israel' and ongoing restriction language
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all incense offering or only formal temple service
notes: Establishes the permanent principle that non-Aaronide persons may not offer incense before the LORD

verse_text: And the LORD said unto Aaron, Thou and thy sons and thy father’s house with thee shall bear the iniquity of the sanctuary: and thou and thy sons with thee shall bear the iniquity of your priesthood.
law_summary: Aaron and his sons must bear responsibility for any wrongdoing related to the sanctuary and their priesthood
classification_reasoning: Priestly responsibility for sanctuary continues as long as sanctuary and priesthood exist
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:1 - direct divine assignment of ongoing responsibility
interpretive_questions: How this responsibility transfers through priestly generations
notes: Aaron and his sons bear institutional responsibility for sanctuary wrongdoing and priestly misconduct

verse_text: And they shall keep thy charge, and the charge of all the tabernacle: only they shall not come nigh the vessels of the sanctuary and the altar, that neither they, nor ye also, die.
law_summary: Levites must perform their duties at the tabernacle but are prohibited from approaching the sacred vessels of the sanctuary and altar under penalty of death
classification_reasoning: Death penalty indicates this is permanent boundary law for sanctuary service
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:3 - death penalty shows ongoing serious restriction
interpretive_questions: Exact definition of sanctuary vessels in different temple periods
notes: Levites serve at the tabernacle but must not approach the sacred vessels or altar, under penalty of death for both Levites and priests

verse_text: And ye shall keep the charge of the sanctuary, and the charge of the altar: that there be no wrath any more upon the children of Israel.
law_summary: Priests must maintain the sanctuary and altar to prevent God's wrath on Israel
classification_reasoning: Preventing divine wrath through proper sanctuary maintenance is ongoing priestly duty
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:5 - 'that there be no wrath any more' shows purpose is ongoing protection
interpretive_questions: Specific maintenance requirements and their application to future temples
notes: Priests must guard the sanctuary and altar to prevent divine wrath upon Israel

verse_text: And the LORD spake unto Aaron, Behold, I also have given thee the charge of mine heave offerings of all the hallowed things of the children of Israel; unto thee have I given them by reason of the anointing, and to thy sons, by an ordinance for ever.
law_summary: Aaron and his sons are given the charge of heave offerings and hallowed things of Israel as a perpetual ordinance
classification_reasoning: Explicitly states 'ordinance for ever' making it eternal, but requires functioning priesthood and offering system.
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:8 - 'unto thee have I given them by reason of the anointing, and to thy sons, by an ordinance for ever'
interpretive_questions: Can heave offerings exist without temple system? Does 'anointing' refer only to original anointing or ongoing priestly anointing?
notes: Explicitly a perpetual ordinance giving heave offerings and hallowed things to the priests

verse_text:
This shall be thine of the most holy things,
reserved from the fire: every oblation of theirs, every meat offering of theirs, and every sin offering of theirs, and every trespass offering of theirs, which they shall render unto me,
shall be most holy for thee and for thy sons.
law_summary: The priests shall receive portions of the most holy offerings (meal offerings, sin offerings, and trespass offerings) that are not burned on the altar
classification_reasoning: Ongoing statute for priestly portions but requires active sacrificial system and temple service.
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:9 - 'every oblation of theirs, every meat offering of theirs, and every sin offering of theirs, and every trespass offering of theirs'
interpretive_questions: Are these offerings possible without temple altar? What constitutes 'most holy' designation outside temple context?
notes: Specifies priestly portions from the most holy offerings: grain, sin, and trespass offerings

verse_text:
And this
is thine; the heave offering of their gift, with all the wave offerings of the children of Israel: I have given them unto thee, and to thy sons and to thy daughters with thee, by a statute for ever: every one that is clean in thy house shall eat of it.
law_summary: The heave offerings and wave offerings from the children of Israel are given to Aaron and his family as a perpetual statute, to be eaten by anyone clean in his household
classification_reasoning: Explicitly eternal statute but depends on Israel bringing offerings and functioning priesthood to receive them.
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:11 - 'I have given them unto thee, and to thy sons and to thy daughters with thee, by a statute for ever'
interpretive_questions: Can wave and heave offerings exist without temple? Does 'clean in thy house' include non-priestly family members?
notes: Heave and wave offerings belong to Aaron's family (including daughters); explicitly a perpetual statute; only the ritually clean may eat

verse_text: All the best of the oil, and all the best of the wine, and of the wheat, the firstfruits of them which they shall offer unto the LORD, them have I given thee.
law_summary: The best firstfruits of oil, wine, and wheat offered to the LORD are given to the priests
classification_reasoning: Ongoing statute for firstfruits but requires agricultural society in the land and functioning priesthood to receive them.
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:12 - 'All the best of the oil, and all the best of the wine, and of the wheat, the firstfruits of them which they shall offer unto the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Must firstfruits be brought to temple or can they be given to priests elsewhere? What constitutes 'the best' portion?
notes: The best firstfruits of oil, wine, and wheat are given to the priests

verse_text:
And whatsoever is first ripe in the land, which they shall bring unto the LORD, shall be thine; every one that is clean in thine house shall eat
of it.
law_summary: The first ripe produce brought to the LORD belongs to the priests, and anyone ritually clean in the priest's household may eat it
classification_reasoning: Ongoing provision for priests from first ripe produce, requires agricultural context in the land and functioning priesthood.
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:13 - 'whatsoever is first ripe in the land, which they shall bring unto the LORD, shall be thine'
interpretive_questions: Does 'the land' specifically refer to Israel or any land? Can first ripe produce be brought without temple?
notes: First ripe produce belongs to the priests; ritually clean household members may eat it

verse_text: Every thing devoted in Israel shall be thine.
law_summary: Everything devoted to God in Israel belongs to the priests
classification_reasoning: Ongoing statute regarding devoted things but requires context of Israel in the land and functioning priesthood.
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:14 - 'Every thing devoted in Israel shall be thine'
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'devoted' items? Does this require formal temple context or can devotion occur elsewhere?
notes: Everything devoted (cherem) in Israel belongs to the priests

verse_text:
And those that are to be redeemed from a month old shalt thou redeem, according to thine estimation, for the money of five shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, which
is twenty gerahs.
law_summary: Firstborn humans must be redeemed at one month old for five sanctuary shekels
addressee_in_text: Aaron, but applies to all Israel
classification_reasoning: Specific procedure for firstborn redemption that could theoretically function without temple, but requires priests to receive payment.
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:16 - 'And those that are to be redeemed from a month old shalt thou redeem, according to thine estimation, for the money of five shekels'
interpretive_questions: What is current equivalent of sanctuary shekel? Can redemption be performed without functioning priesthood to receive payment?
notes: Firstborn humans redeemed at one month old for five sanctuary shekels (20 gerahs per shekel)

verse_text:
But the firstling of a cow, or the firstling of a sheep, or the firstling of a goat, thou shalt not redeem; they
are holy: thou shalt sprinkle their blood upon the altar, and shalt burn their fat
for an offering made by fire, for a sweet savour unto the LORD.
law_summary: Firstborn cattle, sheep, and goats cannot be redeemed but must be sacrificed - their blood sprinkled on the altar and fat burned as an offering
classification_reasoning: Requires altar, priesthood, and sacrificial system - no explicit perpetual language but part of ongoing priestly duties
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:17 - commands sprinkling blood 'upon the altar' and burning fat 'for an offering'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to any altar or specifically the tabernacle/temple altar
notes: Firstborn of cattle, sheep, and goats are holy; their blood is sprinkled on the altar and fat burned as a fire offering

verse_text: And the flesh of them shall be thine, as the wave breast and as the right shoulder are thine.
law_summary: The flesh of firstborn animals belongs to the priests, like the wave breast and right shoulder
classification_reasoning: Dependent on sacrificial system and references other priestly portions (wave breast, right shoulder) indicating ongoing priestly rights
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:18 - 'the flesh of them shall be thine, as the wave breast and as the right shoulder are thine'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to temple sacrifices or any legitimate sacrificial context
notes: The flesh of firstborn animals is the priests' portion, comparable to the wave breast and right shoulder

verse_text:
And the LORD spake unto Aaron, Thou shalt have no inheritance in their land, neither shalt thou have any part among them: I
am thy part and thine inheritance among the children of Israel.
law_summary: Aaron and his priestly descendants shall have no land inheritance among the tribes of Israel
classification_reasoning: Prohibition on land inheritance among tribes - requires identifiable Aaronic priesthood and tribal land divisions in Israel
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:20 - 'Thou shalt have no inheritance in their land, neither shalt thou have any part among them'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to individual property ownership or only tribal territorial inheritance
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: The priestly line of Aaron receives no land inheritance; God Himself is their portion and inheritance among Israel

verse_text:
And, behold, I have given the children of Levi all the tenth in Israel for an inheritance, for their service which they serve,
even the service of the tabernacle of the congregation.
law_summary: The tithe (tenth) of Israel is given to the Levites as inheritance for their tabernacle service
classification_reasoning: Tithe given specifically 'for their service which they serve, even the service of the tabernacle' - tied to tabernacle/temple service
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:21 - 'for their service which they serve, even the service of the tabernacle of the congregation'
interpretive_questions: Whether Levitical tithe applies when tabernacle/temple service is not operational
notes: Establishes the Levitical tithe as a permanent institution; also touches on Levitical inheritance

verse_text:
But the Levites shall do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation, and they shall bear their iniquity:
it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations, that among the children of Israel they have no inheritance.
law_summary: Levites must perform tabernacle service, bear responsibility for violations, and receive no territorial inheritance among Israel
classification_reasoning: Contains both 'statute for ever' and 'throughout your generations' - explicit eternal language but requires tabernacle service
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:23 - 'it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations' regarding Levites doing tabernacle service
interpretive_questions: Whether Levitical service extends beyond tabernacle to any legitimate temple service
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Combines two ongoing rules: Levites serve the tabernacle and they receive no territorial inheritance. Declared a perpetual statute.

verse_text:
Thus speak unto the Levites, and say unto them, When ye take of the children of Israel the tithes which I have given you from them for your inheritance, then ye shall offer up an heave offering of it for the LORD,
even a tenth
part of the tithe.
law_summary: Levites must give a tenth of the tithes they receive from Israel as a heave offering to the LORD
requires_specific_role: Levites receiving tithes to give heave offering
classification_reasoning: Dependent on Levites receiving tithes from Israel - part of the tithing system structure
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:26 - 'When ye take of the children of Israel the tithes... then ye shall offer up an heave offering'
interpretive_questions: To whom the heave offering should be given without functioning priesthood
notes: The tithe of the tithe — Levites must give a tenth of their tithes as a heave offering to the LORD

verse_text:
And
this your heave offering shall be reckoned unto you, as though
it were the corn of the threshingfloor, and as the fulness of the winepress.
law_summary: The Levites' heave offering shall be counted as if it were grain from the threshing floor and wine from the winepress
classification_reasoning: Procedural instruction for how Levitical heave offering should be reckoned - depends on preceding tithing system
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:27 - 'this your heave offering shall be reckoned unto you, as though it were the corn of the threshingfloor'
interpretive_questions: Whether this is merely an accounting principle or has deeper significance for Levitical obligations
notes: Clarifies how the Levites' heave offering is reckoned — as though it were produce from their own fields

verse_text:
Out of all your gifts ye shall offer every heave offering of the LORD, of all the best thereof,
even the hallowed part thereof out of it.
law_summary: From all gifts received, offer every heave offering to the LORD, giving the best and holiest portion
classification_reasoning: Ongoing obligation for Levites regarding offerings, no temporal restrictions stated
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:29 - imperative command structure without time limits
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'gifts' in absence of formal temple system
notes: The best portion of received gifts must be offered as the heave offering

verse_text:
And ye shall eat it in every place, ye and your households: for it
is your reward for your service in the tabernacle of the congregation.
law_summary: Levites may eat their portion anywhere with their households as reward for tabernacle service
classification_reasoning: Permission/reward structure for Levitical service, tied to tabernacle service
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:31 - 'for it is your reward for your service in the tabernacle'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'tabernacle service' extends to other forms of priestly service
notes: Levites may eat their portion anywhere — establishes where and how their compensation may be consumed

verse_text: And ye shall bear no sin by reason of it, when ye have heaved from it the best of it: neither shall ye pollute the holy things of the children of Israel, lest ye die.
law_summary: Levites must not pollute the holy things of Israel after giving the best portion as a heave offering, or they will die
classification_reasoning: Prohibition with death penalty, no temporal limits, requires holy things system
textual_evidence: Numbers 18:32 - 'lest ye die' indicates ongoing serious obligation
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'holy things of Israel' without temple system
notes: Levites must not pollute the holy things of Israel; penalty is death

verse_text:
This
is the ordinance of the law which the LORD hath commanded, saying, Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring thee a red heifer without spot, wherein
is no blemish,
and upon which never came yoke:
law_summary: Bring a red heifer without spot or blemish that has never been yoked for the purification ritual
classification_reasoning: Called 'ordinance of the law' suggesting permanence, but requires priestly system
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:2 - 'This is the ordinance of the law which the LORD hath commanded'
interpretive_questions: Whether red heifer ritual can be performed without temple proximity
notes: Begins the red heifer ordinance for purification from corpse contamination. Though given through Eleazar, it is an ordinance of the law for all Israel.

verse_text:
And ye shall give her unto Eleazar the priest, that he may bring her forth without the camp, and
one shall slay her before his face:
law_summary: The red heifer must be given to Eleazar the priest to be taken outside the camp and slaughtered in his presence
classification_reasoning: Procedural part of red heifer ordinance, requires specific priestly function
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:3 - procedural command within larger ordinance
interpretive_questions: Whether 'Eleazar' is specific person or priestly role designation
notes: Though Eleazar is named, this establishes the ongoing priestly procedure for the red heifer

verse_text: And Eleazar the priest shall take of her blood with his finger, and sprinkle of her blood directly before the tabernacle of the congregation seven times:
law_summary: The priest Eleazar must take blood from the red heifer with his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the tabernacle
classification_reasoning: Requires tabernacle for directional sprinkling, part of ongoing ordinance
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:4 - 'sprinkle...directly before the tabernacle of the congregation'
interpretive_questions: Whether tabernacle reference is essential or directional principle applies elsewhere
notes: Priestly procedure: sprinkling blood seven times toward the tabernacle. Part of the red heifer ritual.

verse_text:
And
one shall burn the heifer in his sight; her skin, and her flesh, and her blood, with her dung, shall he burn:
law_summary: The red heifer must be burned completely including skin, flesh, blood, and dung in the presence of the priest
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement within red heifer ordinance, requires priestly oversight
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:5 - 'in his sight' indicates priestly supervision requirement
interpretive_questions: Who constitutes the 'one' who performs the burning
notes: Complete burning of the red heifer — part of the ongoing purification ordinance

verse_text:
And the priest shall take cedar wood, and hyssop, and scarlet, and cast
it into the midst of the burning of the heifer.
law_summary: The priest must take cedar wood, hyssop, and scarlet and cast them into the burning red heifer
classification_reasoning: Specific procedural requirement within red heifer ordinance, requires priestly function
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:6 - direct priestly command within larger ordinance
interpretive_questions: Symbolic significance and necessity of specific materials (cedar, hyssop, scarlet)
notes: Cedar wood, hyssop, and scarlet cast into the burning — ritual elements of the red heifer

verse_text: Then the priest shall wash his clothes, and he shall bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he shall come into the camp, and the priest shall be unclean until the even.
law_summary: The priest must wash his clothes and bathe in water after handling the red heifer sacrifice, remaining unclean until evening before entering the camp
classification_reasoning: Specific procedural requirement for priest handling red heifer sacrifice, lacks explicit perpetual language but connected to broader red heifer statute
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:7 - specific procedure for 'the priest' after handling sacrifice
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all priests or only those performing red heifer ritual; connection to verse 10's 'statute for ever'
notes: Purification requirement for the priest who handles the red heifer ritual

verse_text: And he that burneth her shall wash his clothes in water, and bathe his flesh in water, and shall be unclean until the even.
law_summary: The person who burns the red heifer must wash his clothes and bathe in water, and remains unclean until evening
requires_specific_role: person who burns the red heifer
classification_reasoning: Specific procedural requirement for burning red heifer, lacks explicit perpetual language but part of broader statute system
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:8 - procedure for 'he that burneth her'
interpretive_questions: Whether this role is priestly or can be performed by others; relationship to overall red heifer statute
notes: Purification requirement for the person who burns the red heifer

verse_text:
And a man
that is clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer, and lay
them up without the camp in a clean place, and it shall be kept for the congregation of the children of Israel for a water of separation: it
is a purification for sin.
law_summary: A clean man must gather the ashes of the red heifer and store them outside the camp in a clean place for the congregation's purification water
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for gathering ashes, lacks explicit perpetual language but serves ongoing purification system
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:9 - 'kept for the congregation of the children of Israel for a water of separation'
interpretive_questions: Duration tied to whether red heifer system is perpetual; scope of 'congregation of children of Israel'
notes: Storage of the ashes for the water of separation — ongoing purification resource for all Israel

verse_text: And he that gathereth the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: and it shall be unto the children of Israel, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among them, for a statute for ever.
law_summary: The person who gathers the ashes of the red heifer must wash his clothes and remains unclean until evening
addressee_in_text: the children of Israel, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among them
classification_reasoning: Explicitly stated as 'statute for ever' applying to Israel and sojourning strangers
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:10 - 'it shall be unto the children of Israel, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among them, for a statute for ever'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'statute for ever' applies to the washing requirement specifically or the broader red heifer system
notes: Declared a perpetual statute for Israel and the stranger. The gatherer of ashes becomes unclean until evening.

verse_text: He that toucheth the dead body of any man shall be unclean seven days.
law_summary: Anyone who touches a dead human body becomes ritually unclean for seven days
addressee_in_text: He that toucheth the dead body
classification_reasoning: Part of broader purity system, repeated multiple times, no explicit end stated
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:11 - universal principle about touching dead bodies
interpretive_questions: Whether ritual uncleanness laws apply without temple/tabernacle system; duration of purity requirements
notes: Core law of corpse contamination: seven days of uncleanness

verse_text: He shall purify himself with it on the third day, and on the seventh day he shall be clean: but if he purify not himself the third day, then the seventh day he shall not be clean.
law_summary: A person who becomes unclean must purify himself on the third and seventh days to become clean
classification_reasoning: Conditional purification process, part of ongoing purity system, requires water of separation
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:12 - conditional purification timeline
interpretive_questions: Whether purification can occur without water of separation from red heifer ashes
notes: Purification timeline: third and seventh days required for cleansing from corpse contamination

verse_text:
Whosoever toucheth the dead body of any man that is dead, and purifieth not himself, defileth the tabernacle of the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from Israel: because the water of separation was not sprinkled upon him, he shall be unclean; his uncleanness
is yet upon him.
law_summary: Anyone who touches a dead body must purify themselves with water of separation or be cut off from Israel for defiling the tabernacle
classification_reasoning: Consequence for defiling tabernacle, requires water of separation system, penalty of being cut off
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:13 - 'defileth the tabernacle of the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from Israel'
interpretive_questions: Application when no tabernacle/temple present; whether cut-off penalty remains without central sanctuary
notes: Penalty of being cut off for failure to purify after corpse contact — defiles the tabernacle

verse_text:
This
is the law, when a man dieth in a tent: all that come into the tent, and all that
is in the tent, shall be unclean seven days.
law_summary: Anyone who enters a tent where someone has died, and everything in that tent, becomes ritually unclean for seven days
addressee_in_text: all that come into the tent, and all that is in the tent
classification_reasoning: Stated as 'the law' for specific circumstance, part of purity system, no explicit end
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:14 - 'This is the law, when a man dieth in a tent'
interpretive_questions: Whether 'the law' implies perpetual application; modern application to houses/buildings
notes: Law of tent contamination from death — extends uncleanness to all who enter and all contents

verse_text:
And every open vessel, which hath no covering bound upon it,
is
 unclean.
law_summary: Any open vessel without a covering is considered unclean
classification_reasoning: Continuation of tent death law, part of broader purity system, no explicit limitation
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:15 - universal principle about open vessels in death context
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only in tent-death scenario or generally; practical application today
notes: Open vessels without a bound covering become unclean in the presence of death

verse_text: And whosoever toucheth one that is slain with a sword in the open fields, or a dead body, or a bone of a man, or a grave, shall be unclean seven days.
law_summary: Anyone who touches a person killed by sword, a dead body, human bone, or grave becomes unclean for seven days
classification_reasoning: Expansion of corpse contamination law, part of ongoing purity system, no stated expiration
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:16 - comprehensive list of death-related contamination sources
interpretive_questions: Relationship to earlier corpse touching laws; whether different contamination sources require different purification
notes: Extends corpse contamination to touching someone slain in a field, a bone, or a grave

verse_text:
And for an unclean
person they shall take of the ashes of the burnt heifer of purification for sin, and running water shall be put thereto in a vessel:
law_summary: For purifying an unclean person, take ashes from the burnt red heifer and mix with running water in a vessel
classification_reasoning: Part of red heifer purification system, lacks explicit perpetual language but connected to v21 perpetual statute
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:17 - procedural instruction for purification ritual
interpretive_questions: Whether red heifer can be prepared without temple; if ashes can be stored/preserved
notes: Preparation of the purification water: ashes of the red heifer mixed with running water in a vessel

verse_text:
And a clean person shall take hyssop, and dip
it in the water, and sprinkle
it upon the tent, and upon all the vessels, and upon the persons that were there, and upon him that touched a bone, or one slain, or one dead, or a grave:
law_summary: A ritually clean person must use hyssop dipped in purification water to sprinkle the tent, vessels, and persons that have become unclean through contact with death
classification_reasoning: Part of red heifer purification system, procedural detail under broader perpetual statute framework
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:18 - specific sprinkling procedures for death contamination
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'clean person' qualified to perform sprinkling; tent application in modern context
notes: Application of purification water using hyssop — covers tent, vessels, and persons

verse_text:
And the clean
person shall sprinkle upon the unclean on the third day, and on the seventh day: and on the seventh day he shall purify himself, and wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and shall be clean at even.
law_summary: A clean person must sprinkle purification water on an unclean person on the third and seventh days, and the unclean person must purify himself, wash clothes, and bathe on the seventh day to become clean
classification_reasoning: Detailed timing and procedures for purification, part of broader death contamination laws
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:19 - third and seventh day purification process
interpretive_questions: Whether washing/bathing requirements apply even without purification water availability
notes: Sprinkling on third and seventh days, followed by washing clothes and bathing

verse_text: And it shall be a perpetual statute unto them, that he that sprinkleth the water of separation shall wash his clothes; and he that toucheth the water of separation shall be unclean until even.
law_summary: Those who sprinkle the water of separation must wash their clothes, and anyone who touches the water becomes unclean until evening
classification_reasoning: Explicitly declared perpetual statute regarding water of separation handlers
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:21 - 'And it shall be a perpetual statute unto them'
interpretive_questions: Whether perpetual nature extends beyond availability of purification water
notes: Declared a perpetual statute. The sprinkler must wash clothes; anyone touching the water becomes unclean until evening.

verse_text:
And whatsoever the unclean
person toucheth shall be unclean; and the soul that toucheth
it shall be unclean until even.
law_summary: Anything touched by an unclean person becomes unclean, and anyone who touches those contaminated objects becomes unclean until evening
classification_reasoning: General principle of contamination transmission, no prerequisites required
textual_evidence: Numbers 19:22 - contamination transfer principle
interpretive_questions: Scope of 'unclean person' in absence of formal purification system
notes: General principle of transmissible uncleanness from a person contaminated by corpse contact

verse_text: Unto these the land shall be divided for an inheritance according to the number of names.

verse_text: The daughters of Zelophehad speak right: thou shalt surely give them a possession of an inheritance among their father’s brethren; and thou shalt cause the inheritance of their father to pass unto them.

verse_text: And if he have no daughter, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his brethren.

interpretive_questions: None - clear conditional inheritance rule
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance

verse_text: And if he have no brethren, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his father’s brethren.

verse_text: And if his father have no brethren, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his kinsman that is next to him of his family, and he shall possess it: and it shall be unto the children of Israel a statute of judgment, as the LORD commanded Moses.

textual_evidence: Numbers 27:11 - 'statute of judgment' language indicates permanent legal statute
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'nearest kinsman' in various cultural contexts
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Final step in inheritance succession: nearest kinsman. Explicitly declared a statute of judgment for Israel.

verse_text:

counsel
 for him after the judgment of Urim before the LORD: at his word shall they go out, and at his word they shall come in,
