things ye shall do unto the LORD in your set feasts, beside your vows, and your freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings, and for your meat offerings, and for your drink offerings, and for your peace offerings.
law_summary: Israel must perform specified sacrificial offerings during appointed feasts, in addition to any voluntary vows and freewill offerings
other_torah_refs: Set feasts established in Leviticus 23, vow laws in Numbers 30
classification_reasoning: Summary command covering both sacrificial and non-sacrificial festival obligations, plus vows and freewill offerings
textual_evidence: Numbers 29:39 - 'in your set feasts, beside your vows, and your freewill offerings'
interpretive_questions: How to fulfill festival obligations when sacrifice prerequisites unavailable, scope of vows and freewill offerings
notes: Summary statement: all prescribed festival offerings are in addition to voluntary vows and freewill offerings

verse_text: If a man vow a vow unto the LORD, or swear an oath to bind his soul with a bond; he shall not break his word, he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth.
law_summary: A man who makes a vow or oath to the LORD must not break his word and must fulfill everything he promised
other_torah_refs: Deuteronomy 23:21-23, Ecclesiastes 5:4-5 references this principle, Deuteronomy 23:23
classification_reasoning: Universal moral principle about vow integrity requiring no infrastructure
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:2 - 'he shall not break his word, he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth'
interpretive_questions: Scope of vows that can be made to the LORD, procedures for impossible vows
notes: A man must not break his vow or oath; he must fulfill all that he said

verse_text:
If a woman also vow a vow unto the LORD, and bind
herself
 by a bond,
being in her father’s house in her youth;
law_summary: A woman in her father's house may make vows and binding obligations to the Lord
requires_specific_role: woman in father's house in her youth
classification_reasoning: Part of comprehensive vow statute with no temporal limitations; addresses ongoing family relationships
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:3 establishes the principle within broader vow regulations
interpretive_questions: Definition of 'youth' and transition points in authority
notes: Introduces the category of vows by a young woman in her father's house

verse_text: And her father hear her vow, and her bond wherewith she hath bound her soul, and her father shall hold his peace at her: then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she hath bound her soul shall stand.
law_summary: If a father hears his daughter's vow and remains silent, her vows and obligations become binding
classification_reasoning: Procedural component of ongoing vow statute; silence equals consent principle
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:4 establishes binding nature through paternal silence
interpretive_questions: Time frame for hearing and responding; what constitutes 'hearing'
notes: Father's silence confirms his daughter's vows

verse_text: But if her father disallow her in the day that he heareth; not any of her vows, or of her bonds wherewith she hath bound her soul, shall stand: and the LORD shall forgive her, because her father disallowed her.
law_summary: A father may nullify his daughter's vows on the day he hears them, and the LORD will forgive her
classification_reasoning: Active nullification procedure within ongoing vow statute; includes divine forgiveness
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:5 - father's authority to disallow with LORD's forgiveness
interpretive_questions: Precise timing of 'the day that he heareth'; scope of paternal authority
notes: Father may nullify his daughter's vows on the day he hears them

verse_text:
And her husband heard
it, and held his peace at her in the day that he heard
it: then her vows shall stand, and her bonds wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
law_summary: If a husband hears his wife's vow and says nothing on the day he hears it, her vows and obligations remain valid and binding
classification_reasoning: Marital authority principle within ongoing vow statute; silence equals consent
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:7 - husband's silence establishes wife's vows
interpretive_questions: Application in modern marriage structures; timing requirements
notes: Husband's silence on the day of hearing confirms his wife's vows

law_summary: A husband may nullify his wife's vows on the day he hears them, making them void and ensuring the LORD's forgiveness for her
classification_reasoning: Active nullification procedure for husbands; includes divine forgiveness mechanism
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:8 - husband's authority to nullify with LORD's forgiveness
interpretive_questions: Same-day requirement interpretation; scope of husband's authority
notes: Husband may nullify his wife's vows on the day he hears them

verse_text: But every vow of a widow, and of her that is divorced, wherewith they have bound their souls, shall stand against her.
law_summary: Vows made by widows and divorced women are binding and cannot be annulled
classification_reasoning: Establishes autonomous vow authority for women without male covering
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:9 - vows of widows and divorced women are binding
interpretive_questions: Status transitions and when independence begins
notes: Widows and divorced women bear full responsibility for their own vows — no male authority to annul

verse_text:
And her husband heard
it, and held his peace at her,
and disallowed her not: then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
law_summary: If a husband hears his wife's vow and does not object, all her vows and bonds shall stand
other_torah_refs: Part of Numbers 30:1-16 vow regulations; similar to 30:7
classification_reasoning: Restatement of silence equals consent principle for marital vow authority
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:11 - husband's silence confirms wife's vows
interpretive_questions: Relationship to verse 7; contextual differences if any
notes: Restatement that husband's silence confirms wife's vows

verse_text:
But if her husband hath utterly made them void on the day he heard
them; then whatsoever proceeded out of her lips concerning her vows, or concerning the bond of her soul, shall not stand: her husband hath made them void; and the LORD shall forgive her.
law_summary: If a husband nullifies his wife's vows on the day he hears them, the vows are void and the LORD will forgive her
other_torah_refs: Part of Numbers 30:1-16 vow regulations; similar to 30:8
classification_reasoning: Restatement of husband's nullification authority with divine forgiveness
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:12 - husband's authority to void vows with LORD's forgiveness
interpretive_questions: Emphasis on 'utterly made void'; relationship to previous similar laws
notes: Restatement that husband's nullification on the day of hearing voids the vows

verse_text: Every vow, and every binding oath to afflict the soul, her husband may establish it, or her husband may make it void.
law_summary: A husband has authority to either confirm or nullify his wife's vows and binding oaths that involve self-affliction
classification_reasoning: Specific authority over vows involving self-affliction; dual option to confirm or void
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:13 - husband may establish or void affliction vows
interpretive_questions: Definition and scope of 'afflict the soul'; distinction from other vows
notes: Summary principle: husband may establish or void wife's vows of self-affliction

verse_text:
But if her husband altogether hold his peace at her from day to day; then he establisheth all her vows, or all her bonds, which
are
 upon her: he confirmeth them, because he held his peace at her in the day that he heard
them.
law_summary: If a husband remains silent about his wife's vows from day to day, he confirms and establishes all her vows and obligations
classification_reasoning: Extended silence principle - day-to-day silence equals confirmation
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:14 - continued silence confirms vows because he heard and said nothing
interpretive_questions: Time frame of 'from day to day'; when does opportunity to object expire
notes: Day-to-day silence by the husband is interpreted as confirmation

verse_text:
But if he shall any ways make them void after that he hath heard
them; then he shall bear her iniquity.
law_summary: A husband who nullifies his wife's vows after initially hearing and allowing them must bear the consequences of her iniquity
classification_reasoning: Part of ongoing marriage and vow regulations with no temporal limitations stated
textual_evidence: Numbers 30:15 establishes principle of husband's responsibility for vow nullification
interpretive_questions: Application in modern marriage contexts and legal systems
notes: If a husband nullifies vows after initially allowing them, he bears the guilt for her broken vow

verse_text:
And do ye abide without the camp seven days: whosoever hath killed any person, and whosoever hath touched any slain, purify
both yourselves and your captives on the third day, and on the seventh day.
law_summary: Those who killed in battle or touched the dead must remain outside the camp for seven days and purify themselves and their captives on the third and seventh days
addressee_in_text: those who killed or touched dead
classification_reasoning: Applies broader corpse contamination principles, but requires water of separation
textual_evidence: Numbers 31:19 applies existing purity laws to warfare context
interpretive_questions: Application without access to proper water of separation and priestly oversight
categories: LOVE_GOD > PURITY_AND_HOLINESS > Holiness of the Camp
notes: Though given in the context of the Midianite war, this applies the ongoing purification laws of Numbers 19 to warfare. Warriors who kill or touch the dead must purify on the third and seventh days.

verse_text:
And purify all
your raiment, and all that is made of skins, and all work of goats’
hair, and all things made of wood.
law_summary: Purify all clothing, leather items, goat hair products, and wooden objects
classification_reasoning: Extension of general object purification laws, requires proper water of separation
textual_evidence: Numbers 31:20 applies broader purification principles to war spoils
interpretive_questions: Purification methods available without temple system
notes: Purification of garments and items after contact with death — applies the ongoing purity laws

verse_text:
Every thing that may abide the fire, ye shall make
it go through the fire, and it shall be clean: nevertheless it shall be purified with the water of separation: and all that abideth not the fire ye shall make go through the water.
law_summary: Metal objects captured in war must be purified by fire and water of separation; non-metal objects must be purified by water only
classification_reasoning: Procedural purification law requiring water of separation not currently available
textual_evidence: Numbers 31:23 specifies fire and water of separation purification method
interpretive_questions: Alternative purification methods in absence of proper water of separation
notes: Establishes ongoing principle of purifying captured metal objects by fire and non-metal by water. Though given in the Midianite war context, the principle of purification by fire and water extends to all similar situations.

verse_text: And ye shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and ye shall be clean, and afterward ye shall come into the camp.
law_summary: Warriors must wash their clothes on the seventh day of purification and be clean before returning to camp
classification_reasoning: Final step in corpse contamination purification process, part of broader ongoing law
textual_evidence: Numbers 31:24 completes the seven-day purification cycle from Numbers 19
interpretive_questions: Community re-entry protocols without proper priestly oversight of purification
categories: LOVE_GOD > PURITY_AND_HOLINESS > Holiness of the Camp
notes: Washing clothes on the seventh day before re-entering the camp — consistent with Numbers 19 purification laws

verse_text: And divide the prey into two parts; between them that took the war upon them, who went out to battle, and between all the congregation:
law_summary: Divide war spoils equally between the soldiers who fought and the rest of the congregation
requires_specific_role: leaders with authority over spoil distribution
classification_reasoning: Procedural instruction for dividing war spoils, could be applicable principle for future warfare but given in specific historical context
textual_evidence: Numbers 31:27 - procedural command for dividing prey from this specific battle
interpretive_questions: Whether this division method applies to all future warfare or was specific to this Midianite campaign
notes: Establishes the principle of dividing war spoils equally between fighters and the congregation. Though given in the Midianite war context, it appears to set a precedent for dividing spoils.

verse_text:
And levy a tribute unto the LORD of the men of war which went out to battle: one soul of five hundred,
both of the persons, and of the beeves, and of the asses, and of the sheep:
law_summary: Warriors must give one out of every five hundred captives and livestock as tribute to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Tribute requirement for warriors from spoils, given in specific context but could establish ongoing principle for warfare
textual_evidence: Numbers 31:28 - levy tribute from warriors' spoils at specific ratio
interpretive_questions: Whether this tribute rate and requirement extends to all future warfare or was specific to Midianite war
notes: Tribute to the LORD from the warriors' share: one out of five hundred. Establishes a principle of dedicating a portion of war spoils to God.

verse_text:
Take
it of their half, and give
it unto Eleazar the priest,
for an heave offering of the LORD.
law_summary: Take a portion from the warriors' half of the spoil and give it to Eleazar the priest as a heave offering to the LORD
classification_reasoning: Specific instruction to give portion to Eleazar the priest from this particular war's spoils
textual_evidence: Numbers 31:29 - 'give it unto Eleazar the priest' - names specific individual
interpretive_questions: Whether this establishes ongoing heave offering principle for war spoils or was specific to Eleazar and this war
notes: The warriors' tribute goes to the priest as a heave offering to the LORD

verse_text: And of the children of Israel’s half, thou shalt take one portion of fifty, of the persons, of the beeves, of the asses, and of the flocks, of all manner of beasts, and give them unto the Levites, which keep the charge of the tabernacle of the LORD.
law_summary: From Israel's half of the war spoils, take one portion of every fifty persons and animals and give them to the Levites who serve the tabernacle
requires_specific_role: Levites who keep charge of tabernacle
classification_reasoning: Portion for Levites from war spoils, tied to tabernacle service which suggests ongoing principle but given in specific context
textual_evidence: Numbers 31:30 - give to 'Levites, which keep the charge of the tabernacle of the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Whether this rate and requirement for Levites applies to all future warfare or was specific to this campaign
notes: From the congregation's share: one of every fifty goes to the Levites. Establishes Levites' share of war spoils.

verse_text: Then ye shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and destroy all their pictures, and destroy all their molten images, and quite pluck down all their high places:
law_summary: Drive out all inhabitants of the land and destroy their religious images and high places
classification_reasoning: Command tied to initial conquest period, though principle of avoiding idolatry remains
textual_evidence: Numbers 33:52 - 'Then ye shall drive out' indicates temporal context of conquest
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to future returns to the land or was specific to initial conquest
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Separation from Idolatrous Nations
notes: Three-part command: drive out inhabitants, destroy their religious images, demolish their high places

verse_text:
But if ye will not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you; then it shall come to pass, that those which ye let remain of them
shall be pricks in your eyes, and thorns in your sides, and shall vex you in the land wherein ye dwell.
law_summary: Israel must drive out all the inhabitants of the land or face consequences from those who remain
classification_reasoning: Conditional warning about consequences of incomplete conquest, historically fulfilled
textual_evidence: Numbers 33:55 - 'But if ye will not drive out' establishes clear conditional framework
interpretive_questions: Whether this principle extends beyond the initial conquest period
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Separation from Idolatrous Nations
notes: Warning that reinforces the command to drive out the inhabitants; functions as a motivating reason for the command in v.52

verse_text:
Command the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land of Canaan; (this is the land that shall fall unto you for an inheritance,
even the land of Canaan with the coasts thereof:)
law_summary: God commands Moses to instruct the Israelites about the land of Canaan that will be their inheritance
classification_reasoning: Specific command to Moses for instructing about the land inheritance boundaries
textual_evidence: Numbers 34:2 - 'When ye come into the land of Canaan' indicates specific historical timing
interpretive_questions: Whether boundary definitions have perpetual significance beyond initial inheritance
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Introduction to the national boundary definition — establishes Canaan as the inheritance

verse_text: Then your south quarter shall be from the wilderness of Zin along by the coast of Edom, and your south border shall be the outmost coast of the salt sea eastward:
law_summary: The southern border of Israel's territory shall extend from the wilderness of Zin along Edom to the eastern shore of the Salt Sea
classification_reasoning: Geographic boundary definition for tribal inheritance, part of initial land allocation
textual_evidence: Numbers 34:3 - 'Then your south quarter shall be' defines specific territorial boundaries
interpretive_questions: Whether these boundaries represent eternal territorial definitions or historical allocations
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Southern border definition

verse_text: And this shall be your north border: from the great sea ye shall point out for you mount Hor:
law_summary: The northern border of the Promised Land shall extend from the Mediterranean Sea to Mount Hor
classification_reasoning: Geographic boundary specification for initial land inheritance distribution
textual_evidence: Numbers 34:7 - 'this shall be your north border' establishes territorial demarcation
interpretive_questions: Significance of these boundaries for future prophetic fulfillment or restoration
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Northern border definition

verse_text:
From mount Hor ye shall point out
your border unto the entrance of Hamath; and the goings forth of the border shall be to Zedad:
law_summary: Define the northern border of the Promised Land from Mount Hor to the entrance of Hamath, extending to Zedad
classification_reasoning: Continuation of northern boundary definition for initial tribal land allocation
textual_evidence: Numbers 34:8 - geographical progression of border demarcation from Mount Hor to Hamath
interpretive_questions: Whether detailed boundary descriptions indicate eternal territorial claims

notes: Northern border continuation

verse_text: And the border shall go on to Ziphron, and the goings out of it shall be at Hazar-enan: this shall be your north border.
law_summary: The northern border of the Promised Land shall extend to Ziphron and end at Hazar-enan

interpretive_questions: Whether these precise geographical markers have ongoing significance beyond historical allocation
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Northern border endpoint at Hazar-enan

verse_text: And ye shall point out your east border from Hazar-enan to Shepham:
law_summary: Israel must establish their eastern border from Hazar-enan to Shepham

textual_evidence: Numbers 34:10 - direct command about border establishment
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to initial conquest or to any future restoration to the land
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Eastern border definition

verse_text: And the border shall go down to Jordan, and the goings out of it shall be at the salt sea: this shall be your land with the coasts thereof round about.
law_summary: The eastern border of Israel's land shall extend down to the Jordan River and end at the Salt Sea
classification_reasoning: Procedural command defining land boundaries requires possession of the land
textual_evidence: Numbers 34:12 - 'this shall be your land with the coasts thereof round about'
interpretive_questions: Whether these boundaries are permanent divine designations regardless of current possession
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Eastern border endpoint at the Salt Sea

verse_text:
And Moses commanded the children of Israel, saying, This
is
 the land which ye shall inherit by lot, which the LORD commanded to give unto the nine tribes, and to the half tribe:
law_summary: The land shall be inherited by lot among the nine and a half tribes
classification_reasoning: Land inheritance by lot requires possession of the land to distribute
textual_evidence: Numbers 34:13 - 'This is the land which ye shall inherit by lot'
interpretive_questions: Whether this procedure applies only to initial distribution or future redistributions
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Land to be inherited by lot among nine and a half tribes

verse_text: And ye shall take one prince of every tribe, to divide the land by inheritance.
law_summary: One prince from every tribe shall be taken to divide the land by inheritance
classification_reasoning: Procedural requirement for land division requiring both land possession and tribal structure
textual_evidence: Numbers 34:18 - 'ye shall take one prince of every tribe, to divide the land'
interpretive_questions: Whether this principle applies to any future land redistributions or only initial conquest
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: One prince from every tribe shall participate in land division — establishes tribal representation in inheritance distribution

verse_text:
Command the children of Israel, that they give unto the Levites of the inheritance of their possession cities to dwell in; and ye shall give
also unto the Levites suburbs for the cities round about them.
law_summary: The children of Israel must give cities and surrounding suburbs from their inheritance to the Levites for dwelling
classification_reasoning: Ongoing obligation to provide for Levites when in the land, connected to their perpetual service role
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:2 - 'Command the children of Israel, that they give unto the Levites'
interpretive_questions: Whether this obligation continues whenever Israel possesses the land regardless of temple status
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Israel must give Levitical cities from their tribal inheritances

verse_text: And the cities shall they have to dwell in; and the suburbs of them shall be for their cattle, and for their goods, and for all their beasts.
law_summary: The Levites shall have cities to dwell in and suburbs for their livestock and possessions
classification_reasoning: Describes ongoing Levitical rights when cities are provided, connected to their ongoing tribal status
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:3 - 'And the cities shall they have to dwell in'
interpretive_questions: Whether Levitical inheritance rights persist independently of active temple service
notes: Purpose of Levitical suburbs: for cattle and possessions

verse_text:
And the suburbs of the cities, which ye shall give unto the Levites,
shall reach from the wall of the city and outward a thousand cubits round about.
law_summary: The suburbs given to Levitical cities must extend 1,000 cubits outward from the city wall in all directions
classification_reasoning: Specific measurement requirement for Levitical suburbs, part of ongoing provision system
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:4 - specific measurement 'a thousand cubits round about'
interpretive_questions: Whether these exact measurements are binding or represent minimum standards
notes: Minimum suburb measurement: 1,000 cubits from city wall

verse_text:
And ye shall measure from without the city on the east side two thousand cubits, and on the south side two thousand cubits, and on the west side two thousand cubits, and on the north side two thousand cubits and the city
shall be in the midst: this shall be to them the suburbs of the cities.
law_summary: Cities of refuge must have suburbs measuring 2,000 cubits in each direction from the city walls
classification_reasoning: Specific procedural requirement for city planning, part of ongoing civil justice system
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:5 - specific measurements 'two thousand cubits' in each direction
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to cities of refuge or all Levitical cities, given context
notes: Extended measurement: 2,000 cubits in each direction from city walls

verse_text:
And among the cities which ye shall give unto the Levites
there shall be six cities for refuge, which ye shall appoint for the manslayer, that he may flee thither: and to them ye shall add forty and two cities.
law_summary: Israel must give the Levites 48 cities total: 6 cities of refuge for manslayers and 42 additional cities
classification_reasoning: Ongoing civil justice requirement for cities of refuge and Levitical cities when in the land
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:6 - 'six cities for refuge...and to them ye shall add forty and two cities'
interpretive_questions: Whether the cities of refuge system operates independently of other Levitical functions
notes: Six cities of refuge among the forty-eight Levitical cities

verse_text:
And the cities which ye shall give
shall be of the possession of the children of Israel: from
them that have many ye shall give many; but from
them that have few ye shall give few: every one shall give of his cities unto the Levites according to his inheritance which he inheriteth.
law_summary: Each tribe must give cities to the Levites in proportion to the size of their inheritance
classification_reasoning: Proportional giving principle tied to tribal inheritance system and identifiable Levites in the land
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:8 - 'every one shall give of his cities unto the Levites according to his inheritance which he inheriteth'
interpretive_questions: How does proportional principle apply when tribal boundaries and inheritances are not clearly established?
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Proportional allocation: tribes with more land give more cities to the Levites

verse_text: And they shall be unto you cities for refuge from the avenger; that the manslayer die not, until he stand before the congregation in judgment.
law_summary: Cities of refuge shall protect manslayers from avengers until they receive judgment before the congregation
classification_reasoning: Justice principle requiring organized congregation with judicial authority to provide due process
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:12 - 'that the manslayer die not, until he stand before the congregation in judgment'
interpretive_questions: What constitutes the 'congregation' with proper judicial authority? Can this function outside established cities of refuge?
notes: Cities of refuge protect the manslayer from the avenger until judgment by the congregation

verse_text:
Ye shall give three cities on this side Jordan, and three cities shall ye give in the land of Canaan,
which shall be cities of refuge.
law_summary: Israel must designate six cities of refuge: three on the east side of the Jordan River and three in Canaan
classification_reasoning: Geographic specification tied to specific land inheritance; may reflect principle of accessibility rather than absolute geographic requirement
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:14 - 'Ye shall give three cities on this side Jordan, and three cities shall ye give in the land of Canaan'
interpretive_questions: Does geographic specification reflect eternal requirement or principle of accessibility? How does this apply if land boundaries change?
notes: Three cities of refuge on each side of the Jordan

verse_text:
These six cities shall be a refuge,
both for the children of Israel, and for the stranger, and for the sojourner among them: that every one that killeth any person unawares may flee thither.
law_summary: Six cities of refuge shall provide sanctuary for anyone who kills someone accidentally, including Israelites, strangers, and sojourners
classification_reasoning: Justice principle applying to all people groups; requires functioning refuge city system but principle transcends specific implementation
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:15 - 'both for the children of Israel, and for the stranger, and for the sojourner among them'
interpretive_questions: Can the principle of equal justice be implemented through other means when formal refuge cities are not established?
notes: Cities of refuge available to Israelites, strangers, and sojourners alike

verse_text:
Or
if he smite him with an hand weapon of wood, wherewith he may die, and he die, he
is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death.
law_summary: If someone kills another person by striking them with a wooden hand weapon, the killer is a murderer and must be executed
classification_reasoning: Fundamental justice principle defining murder by wooden weapon; principle universally applicable but requires judicial system for implementation
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:18 - 'he is a murderer: the murderer shall surely be put to death'
interpretive_questions: Are these specific weapon categories exhaustive or illustrative? What constitutes proper judicial authority and procedure for capital cases?
notes: Killing with a wooden weapon constitutes murder; death penalty required

verse_text: Then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the revenger of blood according to these judgments:
law_summary: The congregation must judge between a slayer and the avenger of blood according to prescribed legal standards
classification_reasoning: Judicial procedure under perpetual statute, requires organized congregation/court system
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:29 establishes these judgments as perpetual statute
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'the congregation' for judicial purposes in different eras?
notes: The congregation must serve as judge between the slayer and the blood avenger

verse_text: But if the slayer shall at any time come without the border of the city of his refuge, whither he was fled;
law_summary: A person who has fled to a city of refuge must not leave its borders
classification_reasoning: Perpetual law but requires cities of refuge system to be operational
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:29 establishes perpetual nature; depends on refuge city system
interpretive_questions: What constitutes a city of refuge in the absence of the original designated cities?
notes: Manslayer must remain within the borders of the city of refuge

verse_text: And the revenger of blood find him without the borders of the city of his refuge, and the revenger of blood kill the slayer; he shall not be guilty of blood:
law_summary: A blood avenger who kills a manslayer found outside the city of refuge is not guilty of murder
classification_reasoning: Perpetual statute but requires cities of refuge system for context
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:29 makes these judgments perpetual throughout generations
interpretive_questions: How does this principle apply without the original refuge city boundaries?
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > NO_MURDER > Bloodguilt and the Land
notes: The blood avenger who kills a manslayer found outside the city of refuge bears no bloodguilt

verse_text: Because he should have remained in the city of his refuge until the death of the high priest: but after the death of the high priest the slayer shall return into the land of his possession.
law_summary: A manslayer must remain in the city of refuge until the high priest dies, after which they may return to their own land
classification_reasoning: Perpetual statute requiring high priest's death as legal marker and refuge city system
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:29 establishes perpetuity; specifically mentions 'high priest' as key element
interpretive_questions: What constitutes the equivalent of high priest's death in different dispensations?
notes: Duration of city of refuge protection: until death of the high priest, then the manslayer may return home

verse_text:
So these
things shall be for a statute of judgment unto you throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
law_summary: These regulations shall be a permanent statute of judgment for all generations in all dwelling places
classification_reasoning: Explicit perpetual declaration for all preceding judicial statutes in this chapter
textual_evidence: Text explicitly states 'throughout your generations in all your dwellings' - clear perpetual language
interpretive_questions: How do these statutes apply 'in all your dwellings' outside the land of Israel?
notes: Declared a permanent statute of judgment throughout all generations

verse_text:
Whoso killeth any person, the murderer shall be put to death by the mouth of witnesses: but one witness shall not testify against any person
to cause him to die.
law_summary: A murderer shall be executed only on the testimony of multiple witnesses; one witness alone is insufficient for a death sentence
classification_reasoning: Fundamental witness requirement under perpetual judicial statute, widely applicable principle
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:29 makes this perpetual; principle repeated in Deuteronomy 17:6, 19:15
interpretive_questions: This principle seems most universally applicable - what judicial authority is needed for implementation?
notes: Requires multiple witnesses for a death sentence; one witness is insufficient

verse_text:
Moreover ye shall take no satisfaction for the life of a murderer, which
is guilty of death: but he shall be surely put to death.
law_summary: No ransom or payment may be accepted for a murderer's life; the murderer must be executed
classification_reasoning: Moral principle against accepting ransom for murder, but requires judicial system and authority to execute
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:31 - absolute prohibition with no time limitations given
interpretive_questions: What constitutes proper judicial authority to implement this law outside of ancient Israel's theocracy?
notes: No ransom may substitute for the death penalty for a murderer

verse_text: And ye shall take no satisfaction for him that is fled to the city of his refuge, that he should come again to dwell in the land, until the death of the priest.
law_summary: No ransom or payment may be accepted to allow a person who fled to a city of refuge to return to their land before the high priest dies
classification_reasoning: Requires cities of refuge, functioning priesthood, and land allocation system
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:32 - depends on death of priest and cities of refuge system
interpretive_questions: How does this apply without Levitical cities of refuge and high priest succession?
notes: No ransom to allow early return from the city of refuge before the high priest's death

verse_text:
So ye shall not pollute the land wherein ye
are: for blood it defileth the land: and the land cannot be cleansed of the blood that is shed therein, but by the blood of him that shed it.
law_summary: Do not pollute the land with bloodshed, as land can only be cleansed by the blood of the murderer
classification_reasoning: Universal moral principle about bloodshed defiling land, applicable wherever people dwell
textual_evidence: Numbers 35:33 - states fundamental principle about blood defiling land
interpretive_questions: Does this apply only to the specific promised land or to any land where God's people dwell?
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > NO_MURDER > Bloodguilt and the Land
notes: The land is polluted by shed blood and can only be cleansed by the blood of the murderer

verse_text: Defile not therefore the land which ye shall inhabit, wherein I dwell: for I the LORD dwell among the children of Israel.
law_summary: Do not defile the land where you live, because the LORD dwells among Israel
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > NO_MURDER > Bloodguilt and the Land
notes: Do not defile the land where God dwells among Israel

verse_text: And when the jubile of the children of Israel shall be, then shall their inheritance be put unto the inheritance of the tribe whereunto they are received: so shall their inheritance be taken away from the inheritance of the tribe of our fathers.
law_summary: At the jubilee year, inherited land that passes through marriage will permanently transfer to the recipient tribe's inheritance
classification_reasoning: Requires functioning jubilee system and tribal land inheritance structure
textual_evidence: Numbers 36:4 - refers to ongoing jubilee cycles
interpretive_questions: How does jubilee function without clear tribal boundaries and land allocation?
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Concern that jubilee would permanently transfer inherited land to a different tribe through marriage

verse_text: This is the thing which the LORD doth command concerning the daughters of Zelophehad, saying, Let them marry to whom they think best; only to the family of the tribe of their father shall they marry.
law_summary: Daughters who inherit land must marry within their father's tribe
classification_reasoning: Addresses specific case but establishes principle for daughters who inherit land
textual_evidence: Numbers 36:6 - specific command concerning daughters of Zelophehad
interpretive_questions: Does this apply to all inheriting daughters or only this specific case?
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: Daughters who inherit must marry within their father's tribe — established as ongoing law from the Zelophehad case

verse_text: So shall not the inheritance of the children of Israel remove from tribe to tribe: for every one of the children of Israel shall keep himself to the inheritance of the tribe of his fathers.
law_summary: Israelites must maintain their inheritance within their ancestral tribe and not transfer it to other tribes
classification_reasoning: Requires established tribal inheritance system and land boundaries
textual_evidence: Numbers 36:7 - inheritance shall not remove from tribe to tribe
interpretive_questions: How does this apply without clear tribal land divisions and inheritance records?
categories: LOVE_NEIGHBOR > WARFARE_AND_NATIONAL_LIFE > National Boundaries and Tribal Inheritance
notes: General principle: tribal inheritances must not transfer between tribes

verse_text: Ye shall not fear them: for the LORD your God he shall fight for you.
law_summary: Do not fear your enemies because the LORD your God will fight for you
classification_reasoning: General moral principle about trusting God in conflicts, no temporal limitations stated
textual_evidence: Command given to 'Israel' broadly without contextual restrictions
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies to all conflicts or specifically conquest-related battles
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: While given in a specific context, the command not to fear enemies because God fights for Israel is repeated as a general principle throughout Deuteronomy and applies to all warfare scenarios

verse_text:
Ye shall not add unto the word which I command you, neither shall ye diminish
ought from it, that ye may keep the commandments of the LORD your God which I command you.
law_summary: Do not add to or subtract from God's commandments
classification_reasoning: Fundamental principle about Torah's completeness and integrity, no temporal limitations
textual_evidence: Prohibition against adding or diminishing from commandments applies to keeping them
interpretive_questions: Scope of what constitutes 'adding' or 'diminishing' in practical application
notes: Prohibition against adding to or diminishing from God's commandments — a foundational principle of Torah fidelity

verse_text: Only take heed to thyself, and keep thy soul diligently, lest thou forget the things which thine eyes have seen, and lest they depart from thy heart all the days of thy life: but teach them thy sons, and thy sons’ sons;
law_summary: Remember and teach to children and grandchildren the things witnessed from God
forever_phrase: all the days of thy life
generational_phrase: teach them thy sons, and thy sons' sons
classification_reasoning: Explicit lifetime duration with generational transmission requirement
textual_evidence: 'all the days of thy life' and 'teach them thy sons, and thy sons' sons' provides explicit perpetual language
interpretive_questions: Specific scope of 'the things which thine eyes have seen' for later generations
notes: Command to remember and not forget God's deeds, and to teach them to children and grandchildren

verse_text:
Take ye therefore good heed unto yourselves; for ye saw no manner of similitude on the day
that the LORD spake unto you in Horeb out of the midst of the fire:
law_summary: Take careful heed to yourselves because you saw no form when the LORD spoke from the fire at Horeb
classification_reasoning: Foundational warning against idolatry based on Horeb revelation, ongoing principle
textual_evidence: Command to 'take heed' based on permanent theological principle from Horeb experience
interpretive_questions: Whether this is preparatory to next verse's prohibition or standalone command
notes: Warning against making any image of God since no form was seen at Horeb — the reasoning behind the prohibition of images

verse_text:
And lest thou lift up thine eyes unto heaven, and when thou seest the sun, and the moon, and the stars,
even all the host of heaven, shouldest be driven to worship them, and serve them, which the LORD thy God hath divided unto all nations under the whole heaven.
law_summary: Do not worship the sun, moon, stars, or heavenly host
classification_reasoning: Fundamental prohibition against idolatry with no temporal limitations or prerequisites
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 4:19 - direct prohibition without conditions
interpretive_questions: None - clear prohibition
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Worship of False Gods
notes: Prohibition against worship of celestial bodies — sun, moon, stars, and the host of heaven

verse_text:
Know therefore this day, and consider
it in thine heart, that the LORD he
is God in heaven above, and upon the earth beneath:
there is none else.
law_summary: Know and consider in your heart that the LORD is the only God in heaven and earth
classification_reasoning: Fundamental theological truth about God's nature - no temporal limitations
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 4:39 - know that LORD is the only God
interpretive_questions: None - clear ongoing obligation to acknowledge God's uniqueness
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: Command to know and internalize that the LORD alone is God in heaven and earth

verse_text:
Thou shalt keep therefore his statutes, and his commandments, which I command thee this day, that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee, and that thou mayest prolong
thy days upon the earth, which the LORD thy God giveth thee, for ever.
law_summary: Keep God's statutes and commandments for blessing and longevity in the land
classification_reasoning: Explicit 'forever' language regarding keeping God's statutes and commandments
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 4:40 - 'Thou shalt keep therefore his statutes...for ever'
interpretive_questions: Whether land blessing is conditional but commandment-keeping remains eternal
notes: General command to keep God's statutes and commandments

verse_text: And Moses called all Israel, and said unto them, Hear, O Israel, the statutes and judgments which I speak in your ears this day, that ye may learn them, and keep, and do them.
law_summary: Israel must hear, learn, keep, and do the statutes and judgments that Moses speaks
classification_reasoning: Obligation to hear, learn, keep and do God's statutes has no temporal limitations
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 5:1 - command to hear, learn, keep and do statutes
interpretive_questions: None - clear ongoing obligation
notes: Command to hear, learn, keep, and do the statutes and judgments

verse_text: Ye shall observe to do therefore as the LORD your God hath commanded you: ye shall not turn aside to the right hand or to the left.
law_summary: Observe all of God's commandments exactly without deviation
classification_reasoning: General principle of exact obedience to God's commandments, repeated throughout Torah as ongoing obligation
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 5:32 - command to observe exactly as commanded without deviation
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'turning aside' in practical application
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Walking in His Ways
notes: Observe God's commands without turning aside to the right or left — a principle of faithful obedience

verse_text: And thou shalt love the LORD thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might.
law_summary: You shall love the LORD your God with all your heart, soul, and strength
classification_reasoning: Fundamental commandment to love God with totality of being, repeated throughout Torah as core obligation
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:5 - love the LORD your God with all heart, soul, and might
interpretive_questions: None - clear ongoing moral obligation
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: The Great Commandment: love God with all heart, soul, and might

verse_text: And these words, which I command thee this day, shall be in thine heart:
law_summary: The words of God's commandments must be kept in one's heart
classification_reasoning: Ongoing obligation to internalize God's commandments, foundational to relationship with God
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:6 - these words which I command thee this day, shall be in thine heart
interpretive_questions: What specifically constitutes having words 'in the heart'
notes: Keep God's words in your heart — internalization of Torah

verse_text: And thou shalt teach them diligently unto thy children, and shalt talk of them when thou sittest in thine house, and when thou walkest by the way, and when thou liest down, and when thou risest up.
law_summary: Parents must diligently teach God's commandments to their children and discuss them throughout daily life
generational_phrase: teach them diligently unto thy children
classification_reasoning: Parental obligation with inherent generational continuity, practical instruction for transmitting commandments
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:7 - teach them diligently unto thy children, talk of them in all life situations
interpretive_questions: Specific methods of teaching and discussion required
notes: Diligent teaching of Torah to children and constant discussion — foundational educational command

verse_text: And thou shalt bind them for a sign upon thine hand, and they shall be as frontlets between thine eyes.
law_summary: Bind God's commandments as a sign on your hand and as frontlets between your eyes
classification_reasoning: Physical reminder system for keeping commandments, repeated in multiple contexts including Passover
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:8 - bind them for a sign upon thine hand, and as frontlets between thine eyes
interpretive_questions: Whether literal physical binding is required or symbolic representation, specific manner of implementation
notes: Bind God's words as a sign on the hand and frontlets between the eyes — basis for tefillin in Jewish tradition

verse_text: And thou shalt write them upon the posts of thy house, and on thy gates.
law_summary: Write God's commandments on the doorposts of your house and on your gates
classification_reasoning: Command to write on doorposts and gates implies having houses and gates, suggesting land possession required
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:9 - 'write them upon the posts of thy house, and on thy gates'
interpretive_questions: Can this be fulfilled in diaspora housing or requires specific land inheritance?
notes: Write God's words on doorposts and gates — basis for mezuzah in Jewish tradition

verse_text: Then beware lest thou forget the LORD, which brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage.
law_summary: Do not forget the LORD who brought you out of Egypt
classification_reasoning: Prohibition against forgetting the LORD is a fundamental moral/spiritual command with no prerequisites
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:12 - 'beware lest thou forget the LORD'
interpretive_questions: None - clear ongoing prohibition
notes: Warning not to forget God when prosperity comes — especially after entering the land

verse_text:
Ye shall not tempt the LORD your God, as ye tempted
him in Massah.
law_summary: Do not test or tempt the LORD your God
classification_reasoning: Prohibition against testing God is a moral principle requiring no prerequisites
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:16 - 'Ye shall not tempt the LORD your God'
interpretive_questions: None - clear ongoing prohibition
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: Do not test God as at Massah

verse_text: Ye shall diligently keep the commandments of the LORD your God, and his testimonies, and his statutes, which he hath commanded thee.
law_summary: Israel must diligently keep all of God's commandments, testimonies, and statutes
classification_reasoning: Meta-command to keep all of God's commandments is foundational and ongoing
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:17 - 'Ye shall diligently keep the commandments of the LORD'
interpretive_questions: None - overarching obligation to obey
notes: Diligently keep all commandments, testimonies, and statutes

verse_text:
And thou shalt do
that which is right and good in the sight of the LORD: that it may be well with thee, and that thou mayest go in and possess the good land which the LORD sware unto thy fathers,
law_summary: Do what is right and good in the sight of the LORD
classification_reasoning: General moral principle to do right and good applies universally, though land blessing is conditional
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:18 - 'do that which is right and good in the sight of the LORD'
interpretive_questions: Does the land promise affect the applicability of the moral command itself?
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Walking in His Ways
notes: Do what is right and good in God's sight — a general moral imperative beyond specific commandments

verse_text:
And when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying, What
mean the testimonies, and the statutes, and the judgments, which the LORD our God hath commanded you?
law_summary: Parents must be prepared to explain God's laws and commandments to their children when asked
generational_phrase: when thy son asketh thee in time to come
classification_reasoning: Explicitly conditional - only applies when children ask questions about God's laws
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 6:20 - 'when thy son asketh thee'
interpretive_questions: None - clearly conditional on children asking
notes: Parents must be prepared to explain the meaning of God's laws when children ask — part of the intergenerational teaching mandate

verse_text:
Thou shalt not be afraid of them:
but shalt well remember what the LORD thy God did unto Pharaoh, and unto all Egypt;
law_summary: Do not be afraid of enemy nations, but remember God's deliverance from Egypt
classification_reasoning: Command to trust God and not fear enemies appears to be ongoing principle, not limited to conquest period
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 7:18 - principle of remembering God's deliverance as basis for courage applies beyond conquest
interpretive_questions: Specific application to military contexts versus general life challenges
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: Do not fear enemy nations; remember God's acts against Egypt. Combines the command against fear with the command to remember.

verse_text: And the LORD thy God will put out those nations before thee by little and little: thou mayest not consume them at once, lest the beasts of the field increase upon thee.
law_summary: Israel must not destroy the nations all at once but gradually
classification_reasoning: Specific tactical instruction for conquest period with explicit rationale tied to that context
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 7:22 - specific concern about beasts increasing indicates temporary tactical consideration
interpretive_questions: Whether gradual approach principle applies to other contexts of dealing with opposition
notes: Gradual conquest — not all at once, to prevent wild animals from proliferating. This is presented more as a description of God's plan, but includes a directive element.

verse_text: And he shall deliver their kings into thine hand, and thou shalt destroy their name from under heaven: there shall no man be able to stand before thee, until thou have destroyed them.
law_summary: You must destroy the names of conquered kings from under heaven
classification_reasoning: Specific to conquest period kings, historical context indicates completion of this particular obligation
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 7:24 - 'he shall deliver their kings into thine hand' indicates specific historical context
interpretive_questions: Whether principle of opposing idolatrous leadership extends beyond conquest context
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Separation from Idolatrous Nations
notes: Destroy the names of Canaanite kings — part of the conquest mandate. Borderline between one-time conquest directive and ongoing principle, but erring on the side of inclusion.

verse_text:
The graven images of their gods shall ye burn with fire: thou shalt not desire the silver or gold
that is on them, nor take
it
 unto thee, lest thou be snared therein: for it
is an abomination to the LORD thy God.
law_summary: Burn idols with fire and do not take their silver or gold for yourself
classification_reasoning: Prohibition against idolatrous objects and their materials appears ongoing, rationale ('abomination to the LORD') indicates permanent principle
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 7:25 - 'for it is an abomination to the LORD thy God' provides permanent theological basis
interpretive_questions: Scope of application to modern contexts where idolatrous objects are encountered
notes: Burn graven images and do not covet or take their silver and gold — ongoing prohibition

verse_text:
Neither shalt thou bring an abomination into thine house, lest thou be a cursed thing like it:
but thou shalt utterly detest it, and thou shalt utterly abhor it; for it
is a cursed thing.
law_summary: Do not bring abominable items into your house; utterly detest and abhor such cursed things
classification_reasoning: General moral prohibition against bringing detestable things into dwelling places, with no time limitations or specific prerequisites
textual_evidence: Command uses imperative language 'shalt not bring' and 'shalt utterly detest' indicating ongoing obligation
interpretive_questions: What constitutes 'abomination' may require broader Torah context for definition
notes: Do not bring abominable idolatrous items into your house; utterly detest and abhor them

verse_text:
And thou shalt remember all the way which the LORD thy God led thee these forty years in the wilderness, to humble thee,
and to prove thee, to know what
was in thine heart, whether thou wouldest keep his commandments, or no.
law_summary: Remember all the way the LORD led you forty years in the wilderness
classification_reasoning: Command to remember God's historical acts serves ongoing spiritual purpose beyond the specific wilderness generation
textual_evidence: 'thou shalt remember' uses imperative indicating continuing obligation to recall God's faithfulness
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to wilderness generation or extends to all subsequent generations
notes: Command to remember the forty years of wilderness wandering — the purpose was humbling and testing

verse_text: Therefore thou shalt keep the commandments of the LORD thy God, to walk in his ways, and to fear him.
law_summary: Keep the LORD's commandments, walk in His ways, and fear Him
classification_reasoning: Fundamental obligation to keep commandments, walk in God's ways, and fear Him represents core covenant relationship without time limits
textual_evidence: 'thou shalt keep the commandments' with no temporal restrictions indicates ongoing obligation
interpretive_questions: None - straightforward perpetual moral obligation
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Walking in His Ways
LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: Keep God's commandments, walk in His ways, and fear Him — comprehensive summary of covenant fidelity

verse_text: When thou hast eaten and art full, then thou shalt bless the LORD thy God for the good land which he hath given thee.
law_summary: You must bless the LORD your God after eating and being satisfied
classification_reasoning: Obligation to bless God after eating applies universally; land reference appears contextual rather than restrictive
textual_evidence: 'thou shalt bless the LORD thy God' establishes obligation triggered by eating and satisfaction
interpretive_questions: Whether blessing formula requires specific land context or applies to eating anywhere
notes: Bless (give thanks to) the LORD after eating — basis for the Jewish grace after meals (Birkat HaMazon)

verse_text:
But thou shalt remember the LORD thy God: for
it is he that giveth thee power to get wealth, that he may establish his covenant which he sware unto thy fathers, as
it is this day.
law_summary: Remember the LORD your God as the source of your ability to acquire wealth
classification_reasoning: Command to remember God as source of wealth-generating ability applies whenever prosperity occurs, establishing ongoing acknowledgment obligation
textual_evidence: 'thou shalt remember the LORD thy God' connected to covenant establishment indicates perpetual remembrance duty
interpretive_questions: None - clear ongoing obligation to acknowledge God's provision
notes: Remember God as the source of the power to gain wealth — a teaching and remembering command to prevent pride

verse_text: Speak not thou in thine heart, after that the LORD thy God hath cast them out from before thee, saying, For my righteousness the LORD hath brought me in to possess this land: but for the wickedness of these nations the LORD doth drive them out from before thee.
law_summary: Do not attribute the conquest of the land to your own righteousness
classification_reasoning: Prohibition against spiritual pride and self-righteousness applies beyond conquest context to ongoing relationship with God
textual_evidence: 'Speak not thou in thine heart' regarding self-righteousness represents ongoing character instruction
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to land conquest context or extends to all of God's blessings
notes: Prohibition against attributing the conquest to one's own righteousness — a command against self-righteous thinking

verse_text:
Remember,
and forget not, how thou provokedst the LORD thy God to wrath in the wilderness: from the day that thou didst depart out of the land of Egypt, until ye came unto this place, ye have been rebellious against the LORD.
law_summary: Remember and do not forget how you provoked the LORD to wrath in the wilderness
classification_reasoning: Command to remember God's past actions with Israel as perpetual reminder of human rebellion and God's faithfulness
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 9:7 - imperative 'Remember, and forget not'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to wilderness generation or extends to all subsequent generations
notes: Command to remember Israel's rebelliousness in the wilderness — serves as a corrective against self-righteousness

verse_text: Circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart, and be no more stiffnecked.
law_summary: Circumcise your heart and do not be stubborn
classification_reasoning: Spiritual/moral transformation command - internal heart condition required of God's people
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 10:16 - imperative 'Circumcise therefore' - ongoing spiritual requirement
interpretive_questions: Relationship between physical and spiritual circumcision requirements
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: Metaphorical command for inner spiritual devotion and humility before God; 'circumcise your heart' parallels physical circumcision with spiritual surrender.

verse_text: Therefore thou shalt love the LORD thy God, and keep his charge, and his statutes, and his judgments, and his commandments, alway.
law_summary: Love the LORD your God and keep his charge, statutes, judgments, and commandments always
classification_reasoning: Contains explicit perpetual language 'alway' and requires no external prerequisites
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 11:1 - 'keep his charge, and his statutes, and his judgments, and his commandments, alway'
interpretive_questions: None - clear perpetual command
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: General exhortation to love God and keep all His commandments; encompasses the whole Torah obligation.

verse_text: And it shall come to pass, when the LORD thy God hath brought thee in unto the land whither thou goest to possess it, that thou shalt put the blessing upon mount Gerizim, and the curse upon mount Ebal.
law_summary: Place the blessing on Mount Gerizim and the curse on Mount Ebal when entering the Promised Land
classification_reasoning: Specific ceremonial command for entering the land - historically completed as recorded in Joshua
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 11:29 - 'when the LORD thy God hath brought thee in unto the land... thou shalt put the blessing upon mount Gerizim, and the curse upon mount Ebal'
interpretive_questions: Whether this was a one-time ceremony or has ongoing applications
notes: Though this has a one-time historical fulfillment at entry to the land, it establishes a national covenant ceremony. The ceremony on Gerizim and Ebal is codified as instruction in Deuteronomy 27. Included on the side of inclusion.

verse_text: Ye shall utterly destroy all the places, wherein the nations which ye shall possess served their gods, upon the high mountains, and upon the hills, and under every green tree:
law_summary: Completely destroy all places where the nations you will dispossess worshiped their gods
classification_reasoning: Command specifically for initial conquest period - 'nations which ye shall possess' indicates historical conquest context
textual_evidence: wherein the nations which ye shall possess - past tense completion implied
interpretive_questions: Could this apply to future reconquest scenarios or ongoing removal of idolatrous sites?

verse_text: Ye shall not do so unto the LORD your God.
law_summary: Do not worship the LORD your God in the same manner as the pagan nations worship their gods
classification_reasoning: Fundamental worship principle with no time limitations - prohibition against pagan worship methods
textual_evidence: Ye shall not do so unto the LORD your God - clear ongoing prohibition
interpretive_questions: What specific practices constitute 'doing so' - how broad is this prohibition?
categories: LOVE_GOD > NO_IDOLATRY > Worship of False Gods
LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > The Altar and Place of Worship
notes: Prohibits worshiping the LORD in the manner of pagan nations; touches both the rejection of idolatrous worship forms and proper worship practice.

verse_text: And there ye shall eat before the LORD your God, and ye shall rejoice in all that ye put your hand unto, ye and your households, wherein the LORD thy God hath blessed thee.
law_summary: Israelites must eat and rejoice before the LORD at the designated place of worship, celebrating God's blessings with their households
classification_reasoning: Eating 'before the LORD' requires the designated place of worship
textual_evidence: there ye shall eat before the LORD your God
interpretive_questions: Does this obligate joyful worship generally, or specifically the eating at the designated place?
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > The Altar and Place of Worship
notes: Command to eat and rejoice before the LORD at the chosen place of worship.

verse_text:
Ye shall not do after all
the things that we do here this day, every man whatsoever
is right in his own eyes.
law_summary: Do not act according to individual judgment as done in the wilderness, but follow proper worship procedures
classification_reasoning: Fundamental principle against individualistic worship - requires following established procedures
textual_evidence: not do...every man whatsoever is right in his own eyes
interpretive_questions: What constitutes proper worship procedures in absence of the designated place?
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > The Altar and Place of Worship
notes: Prohibition against individualistic worship; establishes centralized worship at the chosen place.

verse_text: Notwithstanding thou mayest kill and eat flesh in all thy gates, whatsoever thy soul lusteth after, according to the blessing of the LORD thy God which he hath given thee: the unclean and the clean may eat thereof, as of the roebuck, and as of the hart.
law_summary: Permission to slaughter and eat meat in any location according to desire, with both ritually clean and unclean persons allowed to partake
classification_reasoning: Permission to eat meat locally (non-sacrificial) appears to be ongoing dietary allowance
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 12:15 - permissive language suggests permanent allowance for non-sacrificial meat consumption
interpretive_questions: Whether 'in all thy gates' applies only in the land of Israel or wherever Israelites dwell
notes: Permission to slaughter and eat non-sacred meat anywhere; distinguishes secular slaughter from sacrificial offerings.

verse_text: Thou mayest not eat within thy gates the tithe of thy corn, or of thy wine, or of thy oil, or the firstlings of thy herds or of thy flock, nor any of thy vows which thou vowest, nor thy freewill offerings, or heave offering of thine hand:
law_summary: Prohibition against eating certain sacred offerings (tithes, firstlings, vows, freewill offerings, heave offerings) within one's local gates/towns
classification_reasoning: Prohibition regarding sacred offerings requires functioning temple/tabernacle system to be applicable
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 12:17 - specific prohibition regarding tithes and offerings that require central sanctuary
interpretive_questions: Whether any of these offerings can be distinguished as still applicable without temple
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > The Altar and Place of Worship
notes: Tithes, firstlings, vows, freewill offerings, and heave offerings may not be eaten at home; they must be brought to the chosen place.

verse_text:
But thou must eat them before the LORD thy God in the place which the LORD thy God shall choose, thou, and thy son, and thy daughter, and thy manservant, and thy maidservant, and the Levite that
is within thy gates: and thou shalt rejoice before the LORD thy God in all that thou puttest thine hands unto.
law_summary: You must eat certain offerings before the LORD in the place He chooses, including your household and the Levite, and rejoice before the LORD in all your work
classification_reasoning: Command to eat sacred offerings at God's chosen place requires temple/tabernacle system
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 12:18 - 'before the LORD thy God in the place which the LORD thy God shall choose'
interpretive_questions: Whether the principle of rejoicing before the LORD remains applicable in other contexts
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > The Altar and Place of Worship
notes: Positive counterpart to 12:17; sacred offerings must be eaten at the chosen place with the household and Levite.

verse_text: Take heed to thyself that thou forsake not the Levite as long as thou livest upon the earth.
law_summary: Do not forsake or neglect the Levite as long as you live in the land
classification_reasoning: Conditional on living in the land - 'as long as thou livest upon the earth' refers to the promised land
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 12:19 - 'as long as thou livest upon the earth' provides temporal scope tied to land residence
interpretive_questions: Whether 'earth' means specifically the promised land or applies wherever Israelites live
notes: Ongoing obligation to support the Levites who have no land inheritance.

verse_text: When the LORD thy God shall enlarge thy border, as he hath promised thee, and thou shalt say, I will eat flesh, because thy soul longeth to eat flesh; thou mayest eat flesh, whatsoever thy soul lusteth after.
law_summary: When God enlarges Israel's territory, people may eat meat whenever they desire it
classification_reasoning: Explicitly conditional on God enlarging Israel's borders as promised
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 12:20 - 'When the LORD thy God shall enlarge thy border, as he hath promised thee'
interpretive_questions: Whether this condition has been fulfilled historically or awaits future fulfillment
notes: Reiterates permission for secular meat consumption when the territory expands.

verse_text: Only thy holy things which thou hast, and thy vows, thou shalt take, and go unto the place which the LORD shall choose:
law_summary: Holy things and vowed offerings must be taken to the place the LORD chooses for worship
classification_reasoning: Requires 'the place which the LORD shall choose' - central sanctuary prerequisite
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 12:26 - conditional on 'place which the LORD shall choose'
interpretive_questions: Whether this applies only to Temple or any designated worship place
categories: LOVE_GOD > WORSHIP_AND_OFFERINGS > The Altar and Place of Worship
notes: Holy things and vowed offerings must be brought to the chosen place.

verse_text: And thou shalt offer thy burnt offerings, the flesh and the blood, upon the altar of the LORD thy God: and the blood of thy sacrifices shall be poured out upon the altar of the LORD thy God, and thou shalt eat the flesh.
law_summary: Burnt offerings must be offered on the LORD's altar with flesh and blood, sacrificial blood must be poured on the altar, and the flesh may be eaten
classification_reasoning: Requires altar of the LORD and sacrificial system - temple prerequisites
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 12:27 - 'altar of the LORD thy God' requirement
interpretive_questions: Whether altar requirement is specific to Jerusalem Temple
notes: Specifies that burnt offerings (flesh and blood) go on the altar, blood of peace offerings poured on altar, and flesh eaten.

verse_text:
Observe and hear all these words which I command thee, that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee for ever, when thou doest
that which is good and right in the sight of the LORD thy God.
law_summary: Observe and obey all the commandments God has given
generational_phrase: with thy children after thee for ever
classification_reasoning: Explicit 'for ever' language with generational scope - meta-commandment about all commandments
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 12:28 - 'for ever' and 'thy children after thee for ever'
interpretive_questions: Scope of 'all these words' - immediate context vs. entire Torah
categories: LOVE_GOD > KNOW_FEAR_CLING > Love and Fear of God
notes: General exhortation to observe and obey all commandments.

verse_text: Thou shalt not hearken unto the words of that prophet, or that dreamer of dreams: for the LORD your God proveth you, to know whether ye love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul.
law_summary: Do not listen to or follow prophets or dreamers who lead you away from God
classification_reasoning: Test of covenant faithfulness - universally applicable principle for discerning true vs. false prophecy
textual_evidence: Deuteronomy 13:3 - test of love for LORD with heart and soul
interpretive_questions: Criteria for identifying such prophets in contemporary contexts
notes: Do not listen to false prophets who would lead Israel astray; tests Israel's loyalty to God.

verse_text:
And that prophet, or that dreamer of dreams, shall be put to death; because he hath spoken to turn
you away from the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt, and redeemed you out of the house of bondage, to thrust thee out of the way which the LORD thy God commanded thee to walk in. So shalt thou put the evil away from the midst of thee.
law_summary: A prophet or dreamer who tries to turn people away from the LORD must be put to death
classification_reasoning: Capital punishment law requiring established judicial system and authority to execute
textual_evidence: Command states 'shall be put to death' implying judicial process
interpretive_questions: What constitutes proper judicial authority for execution; geographic limitations
notes: Capital punishment for a prophet who incites apostasy.

verse_text: Thou shalt not consent unto him, nor hearken unto him; neither shall thine eye pity him, neither shalt thou spare, neither shalt thou conceal him:
law_summary: Do not consent to, listen to, pity, spare, or conceal someone who entices you to worship other gods
classification_reasoning: Personal moral prohibition against supporting idolatry with no special prerequisites
textual_evidence: Direct prohibitions using 'shalt not' with no limiting context
interpretive_questions: Scope of what constitutes 'other gods' in modern context
notes: Multiple prohibitions: do not consent, listen, pity, spare, or conceal one who entices to idolatry.

verse_text: But thou shalt surely kill him; thine hand shall be first upon him to put him to death, and afterwards the hand of all the people.
law_summary: You must execute someone who secretly entices you to worship other gods, with your hand being first to stone them, followed by all the people
classification_reasoning: Capital punishment requiring judicial system and legal authority to execute death penalty
textual_evidence: Command 'thou shalt surely kill him' implies legal execution process
interpretive_questions: Legal authority requirements; relationship to modern judicial systems
notes: The hand of the one enticed must be first in executing the idolater.

verse_text: And all Israel shall hear, and fear, and shall do no more any such wickedness as this is among you.
law_summary: All Israel must not commit idolatry or wickedness like those who entice others to worship false gods
classification_reasoning: Prohibition against idolatry with no special prerequisites or temporal limitations
textual_evidence: Command 'shall do no more any such wickedness' is general moral prohibition
interpretive_questions: Definition of idolatry and wickedness in contemporary contexts
notes: Deterrence principle: all Israel must hear and fear and not commit such wickedness.

law_summary: You must thoroughly investigate and inquire diligently when accusations of abomination are made
classification_reasoning: Judicial procedure requiring established court system and judicial authority
textual_evidence: Procedural command 'thou shalt enquire' directed at judicial process
interpretive_questions: What constitutes proper judicial authority; standards of investigation
notes: Requires thorough investigation before condemning an apostate city; establishes due process even in idolatry cases.

verse_text:
Thou shalt surely smite the inhabitants of that city with the edge of the sword, destroying it utterly, and all that
is therein, and the cattle thereof, with the edge of the sword.
law_summary: Completely destroy with the sword all inhabitants, possessions, and livestock of a city that has turned to idolatry
classification_reasoning: Requires sovereignty over cities and military authority to carry out destruction
textual_evidence: Command 'smite the inhabitants of that city' requires territorial control
interpretive_questions: Application to cities outside Israel; modern warfare ethics
notes: The ir ha-nidachat (apostate city) must be completely destroyed.

verse_text: And thou shalt gather all the spoil of it into the midst of the street thereof, and shalt burn with fire the city, and all the spoil thereof every whit, for the LORD thy God: and it shall be an heap for ever; it shall not be built again.
law_summary: When destroying an apostate city, gather all spoil in the street and burn the entire city and all its possessions as an offering to God, leaving it as a permanent ruin that must never be rebuilt
forever_phrase: it shall be an heap for ever; it shall not be built again
classification_reasoning: Contains explicit 'for ever' language but requires territorial control to implement
textual_evidence: Text explicitly states 'for ever' regarding the ruined city
interpretive_questions: Geographic scope; authority to implement such destruction
notes: Spoil of the apostate city must be gathered and burned; the city must never be rebuilt.

verse_text: And there shall cleave nought of the cursed thing to thine hand: that the LORD may turn from the fierceness of his anger, and shew thee mercy, and have compassion upon thee, and multiply thee, as he hath sworn unto thy fathers;
law_summary: Nothing from a devoted city must be taken or kept by the Israelites
classification_reasoning: Depends on the implementation of devoted city destruction laws
textual_evidence: Prohibition 'there shall cleave nought of the cursed thing' is conditional on preceding destruction
interpretive_questions: Application when city destruction laws cannot be implemented
notes: Nothing from the devoted (cherem) city may be kept; relates to the concept of cherem.

verse_text:
These
are the beasts which ye shall eat: the ox, the sheep, and the goat,
